worksheets.title: Cavallini Website Claim Verification and Missing-Evidence Checklist
worksheets.purpose: Converts Cavallini marketing claims and direct communications into pass/fail operational proof before the clinic can remain under active consideration.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 35 minutes per verification cycle
checklist_items: Verify that the legal clinic identity and lead dentist name match across the clinic website, email signature block, and any quote or brochure received from Cavallini — pass = the same provider identity appears consistently in all sources; fail = mismatched names or entities require written clarification from the international patient coordinator before Cavallini remains primary.

Verify that a direct email address or named coordinator contact for international patient communication is documented from the clinic website or written correspondence — pass = a named contact with reachable email or WhatsApp channel is recorded; fail = only a general contact form exists, requiring a request for a dedicated cross-border coordination contact.

Verify that the clinic states whether it will review U.S. diagnostic records before travel by checking email replies, intake instructions, or the consultation process description — pass = written confirmation that records can be reviewed before travel; fail = review deferred until arrival, requiring HOLD status and provider comparison review.

Verify that Cavallini specifies the minimum diagnostics needed for preliminary treatment planning by reviewing its written response for panoramic X-ray, CBCT, intraoral photos, periodontal charting, or medication list requirements — pass = minimum records are named explicitly; fail = vague language like 'send whatever you have' triggers a written follow-up within 48 hours.

Verify that any claim of IV sedation availability is tied to the planned procedure type by locating the website claim and asking for written confirmation about crowns, implant placement, extractions, or All-on-X applicability — pass = procedure-specific availability is stated; fail = generic sedation mention only, requiring escalation to clinical staff.

Verify who provides IV sedation or sedation oversight by obtaining the name or role of the dentist, anesthesiologist, or sedation-qualified provider from written clinic communication — pass = a responsible provider role is identified; fail = no provider identified, requiring management-level clarification before GO.

Verify what pre-clearance or medical screening is required for IV sedation by reviewing Cavallini's written instructions for medical history, lab work, physician clearance, or fasting rules — pass = pre-clearance steps are documented; fail = no pre-clearance pathway provided, making the sedation pathway unresolved.

Verify whether Cavallini requires a post-discharge escort or companion after IV sedation by checking written post-op or coordinator instructions — pass = escort requirement is stated clearly with timing; fail = no escort rule given, requiring direct clarification because travel planning depends on it.

Verify that the clinic can state whether an onsite laboratory supports prosthetic adjustments by locating the facility claim on aestheticdentistrycr.com or written replies about same-stay modifications — pass = onsite lab capability is documented; fail = no documentation or only vague statements, requiring evidence request.

Verify whether the clinic has an onsite pharmacy or a defined medication fulfillment pathway by reviewing facility descriptions and discharge process information — pass = medication pickup process is documented; fail = medication sourcing remains unclear, requiring clarification for post-op control.

Verify whether Cavallini offers onsite hotel integration or a preferred lodging pathway by checking clinic materials for hotel references, transport coordination, or building-adjacent lodging information — pass = lodging relationship is documented; fail = only general San José lodging suggestions are provided, requiring notation as non-integrated recovery support.

Verify that Nobel Biocare implant use is supported by a source you can save, such as a website page, brochure, or written email — pass = documentary evidence is captured and saved; fail = brand mentioned only verbally, requiring written confirmation tied to treatment type.

Verify that Zimmer implant use is supported by a source you can save, such as a website page, brochure, or written email — pass = documentary evidence is captured and saved; fail = brand mentioned only verbally, requiring written confirmation tied to treatment type.

Verify whether the implant brand claim is expected to apply to your likely case type by asking if crowns, single implants, implant-supported bridges, or All-on-X cases typically use the stated system — pass = the clinic links brand usage to the proposed treatment category; fail = brand use remains generic and not case-specific, so implant-system verification stays unresolved.

Verify that Cavallini will provide implant system and component details at discharge by requesting written confirmation about the documentation supplied after surgery — pass = the clinic confirms discharge documentation will identify implant brand and relevant restorative details; fail = no commitment to provide brand-specific records, requiring HOLD if implants are likely.

Verify that specialist access is available for complex restorative cases by reviewing staff listings or asking whether oral surgery, prosthodontic, or multidisciplinary support is available when needed — pass = specialist pathway or integrated complex-case support is documented; fail = no clear answer, requiring escalation if case complexity is high.

Verify that responses to at least three core due diligence questions arrived within 5 business days by reviewing sent emails and timestamps — pass = timely responses met the threshold; fail = delay exceeded threshold, triggering written escalation and reliability downgrade.

Verify that responses to core questions were specific rather than marketing-based by comparing the clinic's answers against the actual question asked about records, sedation, implant brands, or warranty limitations — pass = answers contain procedure-specific process detail; fail = repeated broad assurances require management-level clarification before GO.

Verify that the clinic distinguishes preliminary treatment planning from final diagnosis on arrival by reviewing consultation emails or quote language — pass = written distinction is made; fail = no distinction provided, raising risk of same-week treatment-plan change and requiring notation as control exception.

Verify that Cavallini does not require a deposit or surgery-date reservation before reviewing records by checking quote terms, intake instructions, and coordinator emails — pass = diagnostic review precedes financial commitment; fail = deposit requested before review, requiring control-exception notation and provider comparison review.

Verify that at least one written communication identifies how U.S. records should be transmitted, such as email, upload link, or coordinator handoff — pass = records-transfer pathway is documented; fail = no pathway is provided, which blocks Domain 02 readiness.

Verify that the clinic identifies whether crowns, limited implants, multi-unit implant cases, and All-on-X are all treated under the same intake pathway or different evaluation process — pass = procedure-specific intake differences are explained; fail = all cases handled under one vague pathway, requiring follow-up for complexity control.

Verify that warranty language appears in a source you can preserve, such as a website page, PDF, or written email — pass = a saved written source exists; fail = warranty discussed only verbally, requiring written evidence before ws-01-d can be completed.

Verify that the clinic states whether outside-provider adjustments in the U.S. affect warranty coverage by asking directly in writing — pass = boundary is clearly stated; fail = no answer or ambiguous answer, requiring unresolved warranty status.

Verify that two promised callbacks, documents, or follow-up answers were not missed by checking your communication log — pass = no more than one commitment was missed; fail = two or more misses trigger communication reliability downgrade and block GO without clarification.

decision_gates.condition: All core claims affecting provider acceptability—records review before travel, sedation pathway if relevant, implant-system documentation if implants are likely, communication responsiveness, and written warranty source—have pass/fail outcomes with escalations recorded for any failures.
decision_gates.pass_action: Advance to case-fit and warranty analysis using ws-01-c and ws-01-d.
decision_gates.fail_action: Keep Cavallini in HOLD and require written escalation or provider comparison review before any downstream activation.

cross_references: Cavallini Clinic Infrastructure and Restorative Capability Inventory
common_errors: Checking off a claim as verified because it appears on aestheticdentistrycr.com without documenting whether the claim applies to the patient's exact procedure type.
Failing to escalate when core due diligence questions about sedation, implant systems, or warranty exclusions go unanswered for more than 5 business days.
Accepting generic reassurances like 'we handle many international patients' instead of obtaining minimum diagnostics required for preliminary treatment planning.
example_items: Verified on 2026-04-16 that Cavallini will review U.S. records before travel by saving an email from María Fernanda Rojas stating 'please send your pano, CBCT if available, photos, and treatment history for preliminary review' — pass recorded because records review occurs before travel and minimum diagnostics were named.

Verified on 2026-04-18 that Nobel Biocare use is documented by saving the coordinator email and website implant page screenshot; pass recorded because the brand claim exists in preserved written sources and was linked to implant-supported cases rather than mentioned only in conversation.

Verified on 2026-04-21 that response time failed threshold by reviewing email timestamps showing three core questions on sedation and warranty exclusions remained unanswered for 6 business days; fail recorded and escalated in writing with a 3-business-day deadline.

worksheets.title: Dental Tourism Case-Fit Screening Form for Crowns, Implants, and All-on-X at Cavallini
worksheets.purpose: Determines whether the patient's specific restorative scope, travel capacity, recovery support, and follow-up environment make Costa Rica treatment operationally suitable at Cavallini.

sections.section_title: Probable Restorative Scope Classification and Complexity Banding
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather any U.S. treatment recommendations, recent panoramic X-rays or CBCT reports, prior crown or implant history, and any preliminary comments received from Cavallini. If ws-01-a and ws-01-b are not complete, finish them first so you know which clinic capabilities are verified before judging case fit. This section is complete when the likely treatment falls into one clear case band and the expected complexity drivers are named in writing rather than guessed from memory. If multiple scope possibilities still exist, identify the highest-complexity plausible path so downstream controls stay conservative.

fields.label: Most likely restorative case band for possible treatment at Cavallini
fields.placeholder: Full-arch / All-on-X reconstruction
fields.validation_hint: Choose one: Localized crowns; Limited implant restoration; Multi-unit implant/prosthetic case; Full-arch / All-on-X reconstruction

fields.label: Teeth or arches currently believed to require crowns, implants, bridgework, or full-arch replacement
fields.placeholder: Upper arch has multiple failing crowns and missing posterior teeth; lower left likely needs 2 implants and a bridge evaluation
fields.validation_hint: Example: 'Upper full arch may need extractions plus implant-supported bridge; lower right has 1 failing implant crown and 2 adjacent crowns under review.'

fields.label: Expected irreversible procedures under consideration before any final in-person diagnosis
fields.placeholder: Extractions, implant placement, provisional bridge delivery
options: Bone grafting or sinus-related graft support
Immediate provisional bridge or temporary arch
fields.validation_hint: Select all that may apply based on current records: crown preparation, extraction, implant placement, bone grafting, provisionalization, final prosthetic delivery

fields.label: Known complexity factors that increase cross-border execution risk for this dental case
fields.placeholder: History of cracked crowns, possible bruxism, limited bone volume mentioned by U.S. dentist, prior implant on lower left from another provider
fields.validation_hint: List only actual known factors such as bruxism, prior implant revisions, grafting history, heavy bite, multiple extractions, esthetic-zone work, or medical clearance issues.

fields.label: Has Cavallini confirmed in writing that this general case type is appropriate for pre-travel records review rather than evaluation only after arrival
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Enter true only if a saved email or written intake instruction confirms pre-travel review for your case type.

fields.label: Date current case-fit screening was performed against the latest available treatment information
fields.validation_hint: Use YYYY-MM-DD format, such as 2026-04-22.

sections.section_title: Travel Tolerance, Scheduling Capacity, and Multi-Trip Feasibility
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review your work leave limits, household calendar, passport status if known, and any timeline comments from Cavallini about single-trip versus multi-trip treatment. Use ws-01-b outputs to confirm whether the clinic has distinguished preliminary planning from final diagnosis and whether sedation or staged treatment is likely. This section is complete when your available time, repeat-trip capacity, and stay-length tolerance are quantified rather than described loosely. If the likely case could require more than one Costa Rica trip, document that explicitly here before proceeding to a GO decision.

fields.label: Maximum number of Costa Rica treatment trips realistically possible within the next 18 months
fields.validation_hint: Enter a whole number such as 1, 2, or 3 based on actual time and budget tolerance.

fields.label: Maximum continuous nights you can stay in San José for one restorative treatment phase without creating work or family failure
fields.validation_hint: Enter the maximum nights you can actually stay, such as 7, 9, or 14.

fields.label: Paid time off or work flexibility available for surgery, recovery, and possible schedule overrun
fields.placeholder: Can take 7 business days without issue; anything beyond 10 calendar days requires 30-day employer notice and may not be approved twice in one year
fields.validation_hint: State specific limits, approvals needed, and repeat-trip constraints rather than general comments like 'work is busy.'

fields.label: If Cavallini requires provisional-to-final conversion on a later trip, is that repeat-trip structure operationally sustainable
fields.placeholder: Conditional — possible only if second trip stays under 6 nights
fields.validation_hint: Choose one based on real capacity: Yes; Conditional; No

fields.label: Most likely travel-structure failure point for this case if treatment extends beyond the original plan
fields.placeholder: Second trip would conflict with year-end work blackout and childcare coverage
fields.validation_hint: Name the actual bottleneck: PTO, caregiving, finances, inability to travel twice, passport timing, or medical leave restrictions.

fields.label: Does the probable case remain viable if same-week treatment changes add 3 to 7 extra hotel nights in San José
fields.placeholder: Conditional — only with contingency reserve and remote work approval
fields.validation_hint: Choose Yes, Conditional, or No based on actual tolerance for stay extension.

sections.section_title: Sedation Recovery Support and First-48-Hours Management Fit
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review any written references from Cavallini about IV sedation, discharge escort rules, onsite hotel proximity, and next-day appointment expectations. You must also know whether your probable procedure band includes extractions, immediate implants, or full-arch surgery, because those change early recovery demands. This section is complete when you can name who would assist you, where you would stay, and whether the first 48 hours are manageable without improvisation. If any of those elements are unknown, the sedation pathway must be treated as unresolved.

fields.label: Is IV sedation or other sedation-supported surgery currently considered likely for the probable Cavallini treatment scope
fields.placeholder: Likely
fields.validation_hint: Choose one: Unlikely, Possible, Likely, Confirmed

fields.label: Name of companion or escort available to stay through discharge and first post-op night in San José if sedation is used
fields.placeholder: Sister — Elena Morales — available to travel for 4 nights if procedure date fixed 6 weeks ahead
fields.validation_hint: Enter a real person and actual availability; if none exists, write 'No confirmed escort available.'

fields.label: Planned lodging proximity strategy for the first 48 hours after implant surgery or full-arch treatment
fields.placeholder: Use clinic-associated hotel within immediate transport distance of Cavallini; avoid independent Airbnb across city during swelling and medication schedule
fields.validation_hint: State where you expect to stay and why that location is compatible with next-day review and reduced transport burden.

fields.label: Can you safely manage medications, food intake restrictions, transport, and next-day follow-up if discharged fatigued or swollen after treatment
fields.placeholder: Conditional — only with companion support and nearby hotel
fields.validation_hint: Choose Yes, Conditional, or No based on an actual recovery plan.

fields.label: Unresolved sedation or discharge-control questions still needing Cavallini clarification
fields.placeholder: Need written answer on who provides IV sedation, whether escort is mandatory, and whether next-day review is required before independent airport transfer later in trip
fields.validation_hint: List only unanswered operational questions that could change GO/HOLD status.

sections.section_title: U.S. Follow-Up Continuity and Emergency Triage Readiness
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, contact any current or former U.S. dentist, periodontist, prosthodontist, or emergency dental office that might be willing to evaluate you after return. Review the narrative threshold: implant-supported bridge and All-on-X cases should remain conditional HOLD if no plausible U.S. follow-up pathway exists. This section is complete when you have either identified a likely stateside follow-up option or documented that no option currently exists. Do not rely on assumptions that a local dentist will help later without asking.

fields.label: Name of most plausible U.S. dentist or specialist willing to provide post-return surveillance or emergency triage after Cavallini treatment
fields.placeholder: Dr. Jennifer Lee, DDS — Austin Restorative Dentistry — willing to evaluate urgent bite or provisional issues if records provided
fields.validation_hint: If no provider has been contacted, enter 'No plausible U.S. follow-up provider identified yet.'

fields.label: Current willingness status of U.S. follow-up provider for post-Costa Rica restorative monitoring
fields.placeholder: Tentative if implant brand and procedure records are supplied
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status that matches actual outreach results.

fields.label: How far from home is the nearest realistic emergency dental evaluation option if a temporary loosens or occlusion fails after return
fields.placeholder: Thirty minutes by car to existing general dentist; 75 minutes to implant specialist if component issue occurs
fields.validation_hint: State actual travel time and provider type, not a vague area name.

fields.label: Does the absence of confirmed U.S. follow-up support force this case into conditional HOLD under chapter rules
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Enter true if the likely case is implant-supported bridge or All-on-X and no realistic U.S. triage pathway exists.

fields.label: Follow-up continuity risk summary if treatment is performed at Cavallini and a complication appears within 30 days after return to the U.S.
fields.placeholder: Highest risk is loose provisional or bite issue with no local provider willing to adjust without brand-specific records; would create delay and possible emergency out-of-network cost
fields.validation_hint: Write the most likely post-return failure mode and why it matters operationally.

sections.section_title: Case-Fit Status and Release Recommendation
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review all prior sections in this worksheet together with ws-01-a and ws-01-b. You should also consider whether ws-01-d warranty review is already showing return-trip obligations that make your case harder to sustain. This section is complete when a clear case-fit status—GO-compatible, conditional HOLD, or NO-GO—is recorded with reasons tied to evidence, not hope. If any single unresolved factor could materially change case suitability, do not upgrade the case to GO-compatible.

fields.label: Overall cross-border case-fit status for receiving this restorative treatment at Cavallini
fields.placeholder: Conditional HOLD
fields.validation_hint: Choose one: GO-compatible, Conditional HOLD, NO-GO

fields.label: Top three reasons supporting the current case-fit status for Cavallini treatment
fields.placeholder: 1) Onsite lab and integrated lodging improve control for complex case; 2) probable need for 2 trips creates schedule stress; 3) no fully confirmed U.S. follow-up dentist yet
fields.validation_hint: List numbered reasons tied to evidence from this chapter.

fields.label: Specific fact that would most likely upgrade the case from HOLD to GO-compatible
fields.placeholder: Written confirmation of records-based preliminary plan plus a confirmed U.S. dentist willing to manage urgent post-return triage
fields.validation_hint: Name one decisive missing condition rather than multiple vague wishes.

fields.label: Specific fact that would force a NO-GO decision even if Cavallini itself appears clinically capable
fields.placeholder: If treatment requires 2 Costa Rica trips and I cannot support repeat travel or secure post-return follow-up for implant-supported temporaries
fields.validation_hint: State the actual patient-side failure threshold for this case.

decision_gates.condition: The probable restorative scope can be executed within the patient's actual trip capacity, sedation support plan, and U.S. follow-up environment without relying on unsupported assumptions.
decision_gates.pass_action: Allow Cavallini to remain under active GO/HOLD evaluation pending warranty review and provider comparison.
decision_gates.fail_action: Mark case Conditional HOLD or NO-GO and redirect effort toward U.S.-based provider comparison if operational fit is not realistic.

common_errors: Marking a full-arch or multi-trip implant case as suitable without identifying whether the patient can realistically return to Costa Rica for provisional-to-final conversion or complication care.
Assuming a future U.S. dentist will help after return without naming a plausible follow-up provider or triage pathway.
Ignoring sedation escort availability, work leave limits, or inability to stay near the clinic during early recovery when those constraints directly affect cross-border feasibility.

worksheets.title: Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Exclusions and Return-Trip Exposure Summary
worksheets.purpose: Transforms Cavallini's lifetime warranty language into a practical boundary summary that identifies what is covered, what voids coverage, and what return-to-Costa-Rica obligations remain with the patient.

sections.section_title: Written Warranty Source Capture and Scope Mapping
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the exact website page, PDF, quote attachment, or email text where Cavallini describes its lifetime warranty. If ws-01-b is incomplete, finish the warranty evidence checks there first so this worksheet relies on saved written language rather than memory. This section is complete when the warranty source is identifiable, preserved, and mapped to the patient's likely treatment type such as crowns, implants, bridgework, or All-on-X. If no written source exists, mark the warranty boundary unresolved rather than paraphrasing verbal claims.

fields.label: Primary written source describing Cavallini lifetime warranty terms
fields.placeholder: Email from International Patient Coordinator dated 2026-04-20 plus saved screenshot of website warranty paragraph on aestheticdentistrycr.com
fields.validation_hint: Identify the actual document or saved webpage where the warranty language appears.

fields.label: Date the latest written warranty language was captured for this due diligence file
fields.validation_hint: Use YYYY-MM-DD format matching the saved source date.

fields.label: Treatment categories the written Cavallini warranty appears to apply to
fields.placeholder: Implants, implant-supported prosthetics, crowns
fields.validation_hint: Select only categories actually named or clearly covered in the written source.

fields.label: Exact wording or closest verbatim summary of what Cavallini says is covered for the likely treatment
fields.placeholder: Clinic states a lifetime warranty applies to implant treatment and specified restorative work subject to maintenance compliance and clinical evaluation
fields.validation_hint: Use direct wording from the source where possible; do not rewrite into broader promises.

fields.placeholder: Partially — prosthetic scope unclear
fields.validation_hint: Choose Yes, Partially, or No based on the written language.

fields.label: Warranty-boundary evidence status for this chapter
fields.placeholder: Unresolved pending exclusions in writing

sections.section_title: Exclusions, Void Triggers, and Maintenance Compliance Conditions
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review all written warranty language line by line and ask Cavallini directly about omissions involving smoking, bruxism, hygiene visits, trauma, outside-provider adjustments, and missed maintenance. Compare the answers against your own risk factors and treatment complexity identified in ws-01-c. This section is complete when every known exclusion or void trigger is listed plainly and any missing answer is flagged as unresolved. Do not leave broad terms like 'non-compliance' undefined if they could later be used to deny coverage.

fields.label: Written list of acts, conditions, or patient behaviors that could void Cavallini warranty protection
fields.placeholder: Possible void triggers noted or needing confirmation: smoking, bruxism, trauma, poor hygiene, missed hygiene visits, outside occlusal adjustments, unauthorized modifications by U.S. dentist
fields.validation_hint: List each exclusion separately; if not provided in writing, state 'REQUIRES CLARIFICATION' for that item.

fields.label: Does Cavallini require periodic cleanings, maintenance visits, or documented follow-up to preserve warranty eligibility
fields.placeholder: Yes — maintenance requirement mentioned but frequency unclear
fields.validation_hint: Choose Yes, No, or Unclear from written source.

fields.label: Specific maintenance proof the patient may need to preserve warranty after returning to the United States
fields.placeholder: May need documented hygiene visits every 6 months and records of professional maintenance from U.S. dentist; exact proof requirement still needs written confirmation
fields.validation_hint: Describe exactly what records or behaviors may be required, not generic statements like 'keep up with care.'

fields.label: Does ordinary U.S.-side adjustment or emergency intervention risk voiding Cavallini warranty under current wording
fields.placeholder: Unclear — needs written clarification on outside-provider modifications
fields.validation_hint: Choose Yes, No, or Unclear based on actual written language.

fields.label: Patient-specific exclusion risk factors already present for this case
fields.placeholder: Night grinding history and likely need for local bite checks after return could create warranty friction if outside adjustments are restricted
fields.validation_hint: Name only actual risk factors relevant to your case, such as bruxism, smoking, or expected U.S. follow-up adjustments.

sections.section_title: Remedial Access Logistics and Return-to-Costa-Rica Exposure
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the written warranty for who must travel, who pays transport and lodging, and whether remedial work can only be done at Cavallini. Use case-fit data from ws-01-c to decide whether an extra international trip is operationally realistic for your situation. This section is complete when at least one practical return-trip scenario has been translated into time, lodging, escort, and travel burden. If the clinic has not stated who pays logistics, treat the patient as responsible until proven otherwise.

fields.label: If a covered prosthetic or implant issue occurs after return home, where Cavallini expects corrective care to be performed
fields.placeholder: Likely at Cavallini clinic in San José; no written indication that authorized U.S. providers can perform covered remedial work
fields.validation_hint: State the location expectation exactly as written or mark REQUIRES CLARIFICATION.

fields.label: Who appears responsible for airfare, hotel, meals, ground transport, and missed work if warranty service requires return to Costa Rica
fields.placeholder: Written materials do not mention travel reimbursement; assume patient pays flight, hotel, meals, transport, and time off unless clinic states otherwise in writing
fields.validation_hint: Identify each logistics cost class and who bears it under the current evidence.

fields.label: Estimated number of additional Costa Rica nights likely needed for one remedial warranty trip if a prosthetic issue appears
fields.validation_hint: Enter a realistic whole number based on travel and clinic-review assumptions, such as 3, 4, or 6 nights.

fields.label: Would a return-to-Costa-Rica warranty remedy be operationally possible for you within the next 12 months
fields.placeholder: Conditional — possible only with 30 days notice and separate travel budget
fields.validation_hint: Choose Yes, Conditional, or No based on actual travel and work capacity.

fields.label: Most likely real-world problem that could trigger a warranty-access trip after provisional or final restorative work
fields.placeholder: Fractured provisional, esthetic dissatisfaction requiring remake, or component issue needing brand-specific parts not available locally
fields.validation_hint: Name a realistic post-treatment failure mode for your likely case rather than abstract warranty language.

sections.section_title: Warranty Practical Value Assessment and Decision Boundary
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, compare the written warranty boundary against the probable treatment scope, your trip capacity, and your need for U.S.-side follow-up. Review ws-01-c to see whether a return trip or outside-provider adjustment is already a stress point. This section is complete when the warranty is classified as meaningful protection, limited protection, or unresolved, with a reason tied to access and exclusions. Do not confuse a long duration label with easy usability.

fields.label: Practical value of Cavallini lifetime warranty for this specific patient and restorative scope
fields.placeholder: Limited protection
fields.validation_hint: Choose Meaningful protection, Limited protection, Minimal practical value, or Unresolved.

fields.label: Main reason the warranty is weaker or stronger than it first appears
fields.placeholder: Looks valuable on paper but likely requires return travel to San José and may be affected by outside bite adjustments after return to Texas
fields.validation_hint: Explain the practical access issue or strength in one concise operational statement.

fields.label: Single unanswered warranty question that must be resolved before Cavallini can move to GO status
fields.placeholder: Whether a U.S. dentist can make emergency occlusal adjustments without voiding warranty on implant-supported provisional work
fields.validation_hint: State the one unresolved issue that most materially affects decision quality.

fields.label: Current warranty decision outcome for use in final Cavallini go/no-go record
fields.placeholder: HOLD pending written exclusions
fields.validation_hint: Choose GO-compatible, HOLD pending clarification, or NO-GO due to warranty exposure.

decision_gates.condition: The warranty has a preserved written source, known exclusions and maintenance conditions are either documented or explicitly marked unresolved, and return-trip obligations have been translated into real patient burden.
decision_gates.pass_action: Feed the warranty boundary summary into ws-01-e and later Domain 03 cost modeling.
decision_gates.fail_action: Keep Cavallini on HOLD and do not allow the warranty to be cited as financial protection until written clarification is obtained.

common_errors: Copying the phrase 'lifetime warranty' into the file without separating covered components, exclusions, maintenance requirements, and who pays travel for remedial care.
Failing to test whether ordinary U.S.-side bite or prosthetic adjustments could void warranty protection if performed outside Cavallini.
Ignoring the operational cost of one extra return trip to San José when estimating whether the warranty has real financial value.

sections.section_title: Provider Acceptability and Verified Capability Comparison
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, bring forward the outputs from ws-01-a and ws-01-b plus any saved website pages, emails, or quotes for the U.S. provider being used as the comparison baseline. Do not compare impressions; compare only capabilities or processes that have documentary support or a named source. This section is complete when both sides have enough detail to judge whether the provider can support the probable restorative scope and records-review process. If the U.S. comparator is not specific enough to name, this section requires clarification.

fields.label: Named provider or clinic identity being compared for likely implant/crown/full-arch treatment
fields.placeholder: Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic
fields.validation_hint: Left side example: 'Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic'; right side example should name a real U.S. practice such as 'Austin Prosthodontics & Implant Center.'

fields.label: Verified ability to review diagnostic records before treatment commitment
fields.placeholder: Written confirmation received that pano, CBCT if available, photos, and treatment history can be reviewed before travel
fields.validation_hint: Record the actual pre-treatment review pathway for each side, not a general statement like 'offers consultations.'

fields.label: Verified infrastructure or clinical support relevant to the probable case complexity
fields.placeholder: Onsite lab, possible IV sedation, integrated lodging pathway, implant brand documentation pending final case assignment
fields.validation_hint: List capabilities specific to the likely restorative scope for each side.

fields.label: Communication reliability based on actual due diligence exchanges
fields.placeholder: Conditional — answers mostly specific but two sedation questions still pending
fields.validation_hint: Choose Strong, Conditional, Weak, or REQUIRES CLARIFICATION based on actual response history.

sections.section_title: Continuity, Accessibility, and Remedial Convenience Comparison
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review ws-01-c and ws-01-d so you can compare not just treatment delivery but also what happens if adjustments, triage, or remakes are needed after the initial procedure. Use actual travel distance, return-visit feasibility, and U.S. follow-up burden as your comparison inputs. This section is complete when each side's convenience for emergency access, routine follow-up, and remedial correction is explicit. Do not let lower procedure price obscure a weak correction pathway.

fields.label: Ease of obtaining urgent evaluation within 72 hours if bite, swelling, or provisional problems develop after treatment
fields.placeholder: Weak
fields.validation_hint: Choose Easy, Moderate, Difficult, or REQUIRES CLARIFICATION based on real access conditions.

fields.label: Expected patient burden for remedial visits, remakes, or warranty-related corrections
fields.placeholder: Would likely require international return trip, hotel stay, time off work, and possible companion if major prosthetic issue arises
fields.validation_hint: State the actual burden for each side in operational terms.

fields.label: Compatibility with stateside follow-up or surveillance after definitive treatment
fields.placeholder: Depends on discharge records, implant brand details, and willingness of local U.S. dentist to triage provisional or occlusal issues
fields.validation_hint: Describe how easily each option integrates with ongoing follow-up after the main treatment event.

fields.label: Overall continuity advantage for this patient's likely restorative scope
fields.placeholder: U.S. provider advantage
fields.validation_hint: Choose Cavallini advantage, U.S. provider advantage, Roughly equal, or Unclear.

sections.section_title: Warranty Accessibility and Case-Fit Tradeoff Comparison
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, use ws-01-c for patient-side fit and ws-01-d for Cavallini warranty boundaries; gather any available warranty or remake policies from the U.S. comparator as well. The goal here is to compare not legal wording alone, but how reachable and usable corrective protection would be in real life. This section is complete when each side's protection is framed in terms of access, exclusions, and operational friction. If the U.S. provider has no stated warranty policy, record that explicitly instead of assuming equivalence.

fields.label: Practical accessibility of warranty, remake, or corrective policy for this patient's location and schedule
fields.placeholder: Lifetime wording exists but access may require return to Costa Rica; U.S. provider policy shorter in duration but far easier to use locally
fields.validation_hint: Compare practical usability, not just duration language.

fields.label: How well this option fits the patient's actual trip capacity, recovery support, and tolerance for repeat phases
fields.placeholder: Conditional fit
fields.validation_hint: Choose Strong fit, Conditional fit, Poor fit, or REQUIRES CLARIFICATION.

fields.label: Most important tradeoff this option creates versus the other side
fields.placeholder: Lower procedure cost and integrated treatment environment versus weaker convenience for later corrective access once back in the U.S.
fields.validation_hint: Write the tradeoff as a direct contrast, not a marketing description.

sections.section_title: Final Chapter Decision and Release Status
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, confirm that ws-01-a through ws-01-d are finished and that any unresolved items have been classified as critical or noncritical. Apply the chapter closure tests: capability sufficiency, case suitability, and warranty intelligibility. This section is complete when a dated GO, HOLD, or NO-GO determination is entered with a rationale that cites the comparison and unresolved-item status. No downstream domain should activate until this section is finished.

fields.label: Final chapter status for Cavallini as a provider option for this restorative case
fields.placeholder: HOLD
fields.validation_hint: Choose GO, HOLD, or NO-GO exactly as defined in the chapter narrative.

fields.label: Decision date for Cavallini provider status
fields.validation_hint: Use YYYY-MM-DD format for the date the decision is formally recorded.

fields.label: Reason Cavallini earned the final status instead of the compared U.S. provider
fields.placeholder: HOLD because infrastructure appears promising, but warranty exclusions and U.S. follow-up compatibility remain unresolved enough to affect acceptability
fields.validation_hint: Tie the reason to evidence from prior worksheets rather than preference or quote appeal.

fields.label: Downstream release instruction for Domains 02 and 03 based on this chapter outcome
fields.placeholder: Do not activate Domain 02 or Domain 03 until Cavallini confirms written warranty exclusions and records-review pathway; only evidence collection may continue
fields.validation_hint: State exactly what downstream activity is allowed or blocked by the final status.

decision_gates.condition: Cavallini can be marked GO only when infrastructure capability is sufficient, the patient's case is operationally suitable for cross-border care, and warranty boundaries are clear enough to price later; any unresolved critical capability keeps status at HOLD or NO-GO.
decision_gates.pass_action: Release structured outputs to Domain 02 and Domain 03 according to the recorded status and conditions.
decision_gates.fail_action: Prevent deposit, travel booking, surgery-date hold, and irreversible dental work until missing evidence is resolved or the provider is eliminated.

worksheets.title: Panoramic, CBCT, Periodontal, and Photo File Quality Verification Checklist for Cross-Border Use
worksheets.purpose: Verifies that each diagnostic file is current, readable, properly labeled, and clinically usable before it is sent to Cavallini for planning.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 25 minutes per packet version
checklist_items: Verify that the panoramic X-ray shows the full arch anatomy without severe blur or compression artifact by opening the actual file on a full-size screen — pass = both arches are readable and planning landmarks are visible, fail = request a fresh export from the originating U.S. dental office before transmission to Cavallini.

Verify that the panoramic X-ray file displays the patient’s name and capture date within the image viewer or on the exported file header by reviewing the original digital file — pass = identity and date are visible and match ws-02-a, fail = obtain a properly labeled export from the source office.

Verify that the panoramic X-ray is not older than 12 months for an implant, extraction, full-mouth, or All-on-X case by comparing the image date to the current date — pass = within 12 months or Cavallini has documented acceptance of an older image, fail = request updated imaging and log the age risk in ws-02-e.

Verify that any available CBCT can be opened in a clinic-usable digital format by launching the viewer or inspecting the DICOM folder from the imaging center export — pass = files open and scan data is navigable, fail = re-request exportable DICOM with viewer from the imaging center.

Verify that the CBCT capture date is clearly documented in the file set or accompanying export paperwork by checking the imaging center release package — pass = date is visible and attributable to the patient, fail = request corrected documentation before relying on the scan for planning.

Verify that the CBCT source office is identifiable and matches the requesting patient by reviewing the export paperwork or folder naming — pass = source office and patient identity are clear, fail = rename only after obtaining source confirmation and do not send an orphaned scan.

Verify that the intraoral photo set includes a frontal smile image by reviewing the folder contents against the packet inventory — pass = one clear frontal smile photo exists, fail = obtain the missing image before calling the photo set complete.

Verify that the intraoral photo set includes a retracted frontal view showing both arches by reviewing the actual photos at full size — pass = the image shows teeth clearly without glare blocking key surfaces, fail = request a proper retracted view from the U.S. office or take a replacement set if accepted by Cavallini.

Verify that the intraoral photo set includes right buccal and left buccal views by checking for two distinct side images with adequate lighting and focus — pass = both sides are present and readable, fail = mark photo packet incomplete for restorative planning and obtain replacements.

Verify that upper occlusal and lower occlusal photos are included if possible by inspecting the full image set — pass = both occlusal views are present or Cavallini has stated they are not required for preliminary review, fail = log the omission and request missing views.

Verify that no photo in the packet is merely a screenshot from a text thread or patient selfie crop by checking file metadata and image framing — pass = source files appear to be original clinical photos or clearly usable originals, fail = replace low-grade derivatives with original office exports.

Verify that the periodontal chart is dated and identifies the treating periodontal or general dental office by reviewing the PDF or printed chart scan — pass = office and date are visible, fail = request a clearer or complete chart from the source office.

Verify that the periodontal chart includes probing depths and not just a narrative summary by reading the chart content — pass = probing data are present and legible, fail = ask the periodontal office for the most recent full chart instead of progress notes only.

Verify that recession and bleeding notation are included if available by reviewing the periodontal chart legend and charted values — pass = included or explicitly unavailable from the office, fail = document limitation as a quality risk rather than assuming chart completeness.

Verify that the restorative and extraction history is concise, dated, and arch-specific by reading the compiled treatment history summary — pass = prior crowns, implants, root canals, extractions, failures, and current symptoms are chronologically stated, fail = rewrite the summary before transmission.

Verify that the medication list has been reviewed against what the patient is actually taking on the date of packet transmission by comparing it to current pill bottles, pharmacy app, or physician summary — pass = list is current as of transmission date, fail = update the medication list immediately and replace prior versions.

Verify that the medication list includes anticoagulants, bisphosphonates, diabetes medications, immunosuppressive drugs, and relevant supplements if taken by checking the compiled list against the patient’s actual regimen — pass = all active items are disclosed, fail = revise before any sedation or surgery planning request.

Verify that allergy information relevant to anesthesia, antibiotics, analgesics, latex, or dental materials is included when applicable by reviewing the patient medical history sheet or current provider records — pass = allergies are listed or explicitly documented as none known, fail = add the allergy statement before submission.

Verify that every file name follows the packet naming convention of LastName_FirstName_RecordType_SourceOffice_YYYY-MM-DD by reviewing the folder before upload — pass = all files match the convention, fail = rename the entire packet consistently before transmission.

Verify that the packet does not contain duplicate superseded versions without version labeling by reviewing file dates and names in the submission folder — pass = only current files or clearly marked replacement versions remain, fail = rebuild the packet and remove ambiguous duplicates.

Verify that any office-provided link for CBCT or imaging download will still be active long enough for Cavallini to access it by checking expiration terms in the release email — pass = link remains active through expected review window or files are downloaded locally first, fail = obtain a non-expiring copy or resend with a fresh link.

Verify that zipped folders can actually be opened on a second device before international transmission by testing the compressed file outside the source system — pass = folder opens and contents are visible, fail = recreate the archive or send the files in another clinic-approved format.

Verify that each transmitted image is oriented correctly and not mirrored or rotated in a way that would confuse planning by opening the files in the intended viewer — pass = standard orientation is clear, fail = correct the export or annotate the issue before sending.

Verify that any known medical condition likely to affect surgery or IV sedation is documented in the packet by checking the medical history summary against current physician records — pass = conditions such as diabetes, cardiac history, or anticoagulation are disclosed, fail = update the medical summary and consider clearance routing in ws-02-c.

Verify that the packet is suitable for either preliminary planning or clearly labeled with quality limitations by comparing all findings to ws-02-a and ws-02-e requirements — pass = the packet is either complete or deficiencies are explicitly logged, fail = do not present it as a final planning packet to Cavallini.

decision_gates.condition: All critical files required for the current treatment context are readable, dated, identity-linked, and either fully usable or explicitly flagged with limitations.
decision_gates.pass_action: Transmit only the verified packet version and log it in ws-02-d.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not represent the packet as final; re-request low-quality or stale diagnostics and record deficiencies in ws-02-e.

cross_references: U.S. Diagnostic Record Packet Inventory for Cavallini Treatment Planning

Cavallini Diagnostic Sufficiency Confirmation and Missing-Item Resolution Form
common_errors: Treating cellphone photos of printed radiographs as equivalent to original digital images, which often makes Costa Rica-side review unusable.
Checking off 'photos included' when the set contains only smile photos and lacks retracted right, left, upper occlusal, or lower occlusal views needed for restorative planning.
Confusing clinic receipt with diagnostic usability; a file can arrive successfully and still fail quality standards for treatment planning.
example_items: Verified that the panoramic X-ray from Lakeside Family Dentistry dated 2026-03-04 opens clearly, shows both arches, and includes Jane Smith’s name in the viewer header — PASS; no re-request needed before Cavallini upload.

Verified that the CBCT export from Chicago Oral & Maxillofacial Imaging Center dated 2026-02-18 opens in the included viewer and the DICOM folder is readable on a second laptop — PASS; acceptable for cross-border submission.

Verified that the photo folder contains frontal smile, retracted frontal, right buccal, and left buccal images but is missing upper and lower occlusal views — FAIL; replacement occlusal photos requested from U.S. office and packet remains incomplete for final aesthetic/restorative planning.

If an office outsourced imaging, confirm the actual records-holding entity before entering routing details. Complete this section only when each requested record has a named releasing office, a direct release contact, and a specific deliverable description tied to Cavallini planning. If you cannot identify who actually controls the file, mark the unresolved field as REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and do not assume the treating dentist holds it.

This section depends on the source office details already being entered in the prior section. Complete this section when the authorization path is specific enough that the office can legally release the records without calling back for missing identity information or signature defects. If any office uses its own mandatory form, note that here rather than assuming a generic HIPAA release will work.

sections.section_title: Cavallini-Compatible Delivery Routing
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review Cavallini’s preferred intake method from email instructions, portal guidance, or coordinator messages and have ws-02-d ready for later transmission logging. Do not guess at the destination method because this section determines whether U.S. offices send files in a format Cavallini can actually retrieve. Complete this section when the release route from the U.S. source to the patient or clinic is explicit, time-bounded, and compatible with packet assembly. If direct release to Costa Rica would scatter files across channels, route to the patient first and document that decision.

fields.label: Reason this routing path is safest for the Cavallini pre-op packet

sections.section_title: Medical Clearance Dependency Identification
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review your current medication list, known medical conditions, any Cavallini messages about IV sedation or surgery prerequisites, and recent physician visit summaries. This section should not be completed from memory alone if anticoagulants, diabetes treatment, cardiac issues, or other surgical risks may be relevant. Complete this section when you have a documented yes/no answer on whether physician clearance may be needed and have identified the responsible U.S. clinician if the answer is yes. If no one has yet confirmed the requirement, mark it as pending rather than assuming clearance is unnecessary.

fields.label: Does the current planned Cavallini treatment scenario indicate possible physician clearance for surgery or IV sedation
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Enter true if implants, extensive surgery, sedation, or relevant medical conditions suggest clearance may be needed.

fields.placeholder: Active apixaban use for atrial fibrillation noted on current medication list; likely requires physician guidance before implant surgery or sedation
fields.validation_hint: If none known, state 'No current condition identified as a known clearance trigger as of 2026-03-11'.

fields.validation_hint: Use the specific clinician who can answer the clearance question, not a generic practice name.

fields.validation_hint: Record the actual inquiry date so unresolved clearance risk can be escalated before travel planning.

options: Clearance likely required — physician not yet contacted
fields.validation_hint: Choose the blocking status exactly; example: Clearance required and not yet requested.

sections.section_title: Release Delay Escalation and Recovery Routing
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the promised turnaround dates entered above, any unanswered calls or portal messages, and the chapter escalation rules for 7-business-day delays from U.S. providers. This section is complete when there is a named next action, escalation contact, and date for any provider who has not released records on time. If the office is blocked by a missing signature, fee, or identity issue, record the exact blocker instead of writing 'delayed.' A completed section lets another person take over the follow-up without guessing what happened.

fields.label: Has this U.S. source office exceeded its promised release time for the Cavallini packet item
fields.placeholder: false
fields.validation_hint: Enter true if the committed date passed without release or usable delivery.

fields.validation_hint: Use a real escalation path such as practice manager or radiology supervisor.

fields.label: Date scheduled for next follow-up if release remains incomplete
fields.validation_hint: Set a real follow-up date within the chapter escalation window, not 'ASAP'.

fields.placeholder: Packet assembly; Cavallini preliminary planning request; Costa Rica travel readiness

decision_gates.pass_action: Proceed with record acquisition and use ws-02-d once files are actually transmitted to Cavallini.
decision_gates.fail_action: Keep this worksheet active and resolve missing release mechanics before expecting a complete packet.

Do not complete this section from memory or from a phone call unless the clinic has also provided written confirmation. This section is complete when the specific packet version reviewed by Cavallini is identified and the clinic’s actual written language on receipt, readability, and sufficiency is captured. If the clinic has not yet issued a written statement, this section remains open.

Complete this section when every deficiency named by Cavallini has an owner, source, and operational impact recorded. If the clinic stated there are no missing items, enter that explicitly rather than leaving fields blank.

worksheets.title: Costa Rica Treatment Budget Inclusion Checklist for Travel, Recovery, and Return-Trip Risk
worksheets.purpose: Verifies that every non-clinical Costa Rica cost driver and recovery-related exposure has been included before any savings claim or booking decision is approved.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 25 minutes on first use, 10 minutes after quote updates
checklist_items: Verify that round-trip airfare for the patient has been priced using a real itinerary from the actual departure airport to San José, Costa Rica — use airline checkout screens or a travel agent quote, not a search-result teaser fare — pass = ticket price including taxes is entered in the Costa Rica model, fail = reprice using a bookable itinerary before claiming savings.

Verify that checked-bag or carry-on fees for medically necessary supplies, recovery items, or extended-stay clothing are included using the selected airline baggage policy — pass = baggage cost is entered as its own line item or documented as zero because the fare includes bags, fail = add baggage costs before approving the budget.

Verify that the baseline lodging plan near Cavallini clinic or in Cavallini-related onsite lodging is priced from an actual booking source for the expected treatment dates — pass = nightly rate, taxes, and number of baseline nights are documented, fail = replace memory-based hotel estimates with a current booking quote.

Verify that a separate contingency lodging extension has been priced for at least one recovery-driven delay scenario using the same hotel or nearby equivalent — pass = extra nights are shown as a distinct contingency line, fail = mark the lodging model incomplete and recalculate.

Verify that airport-to-hotel and hotel-to-clinic ground transportation in San José has been priced using the planned transport method — use clinic-arranged transfer details, rideshare estimates, or car service quote — pass = all known transfers are budgeted, fail = add local transport before proceeding.

Verify that daily food spending for the patient during Costa Rica recovery has been modeled using the expected number of nights and post-op diet limitations — pass = meal budget is entered with calculation method, fail = replace vague 'food buffer' entry with a per-day estimate.

Verify that onsite pharmacy or outside pharmacy medication purchases likely to be paid in Costa Rica are included using the clinic medication list or coordinator guidance — pass = medications are entered as a Costa Rica line item or documented as included, fail = request clarification and hold the all-in total as incomplete.

Verify that companion airfare is treated as mandatory whenever the treatment path includes IV sedation, extensive extractions, or immediate post-op dependency unless Cavallini explicitly states otherwise in writing — pass = companion travel is included or a written no-companion rationale is attached, fail = classify the budget as undercounted.

Verify that companion lodging and daily food costs are included for every night the patient is expected to need post-sedation or post-surgical assistance — pass = companion stay is modeled across the correct dates, fail = revise the budget to include full support duration.

Verify that missed work for the patient has been converted into cash exposure using payroll records, PTO policy, or self-employment revenue history — pass = a dollar value and calculation basis are documented, fail = do not leave lost income as a narrative note.

Verify that missed work for the companion has been converted into cash exposure whenever the companion is required for sedation-day control or first-48-hours assistance — pass = companion lost-income impact is entered or documented as zero with supporting reason, fail = add the figure before net savings review.

Verify that passport renewal or expedited passport fees are included if the current U.S. passport will not remain valid for the planned trip architecture — use the passport expiration date and current Department of State fee schedule — pass = passport cost is entered or documented as not required, fail = add renewal cost to the travel budget.

Verify that child-care or elder-care costs triggered specifically by the Costa Rica treatment trip are included using actual caregiver quotes or usual paid rates — pass = these dependency-care costs are entered or marked not applicable, fail = revise the non-clinical budget.

Verify that U.S. pre-travel costs directly caused by the international case, such as records mailing, CBCT disc duplication, or notarized payment paperwork, are captured if not already included elsewhere — pass = these case-triggered admin costs are listed, fail = update the budget before approval.

Verify that the model distinguishes a one-trip case from a two-trip or staged prosthetic case using the trip architecture from ws-03-a — pass = all trip-related cost categories are multiplied by the correct number of trips, fail = invalidate the all-in budget and rebuild it.

Verify that a second planned prosthetic trip, if required for final zirconia or final implant prosthetic delivery, includes fresh airfare, lodging, food, local transport, and time-off-work costs rather than only the chairside clinical fee — pass = second-trip architecture is fully budgeted, fail = mark total savings as invalid.

Verify that emergency or unplanned return-trip exposure has been priced at least at a baseline level using one emergency flight, three hotel nights, and local transport — pass = return-trip exposure is entered as its own risk line, fail = the reserve model cannot be approved.

Verify that recovery-driven hotel extension risk after implant placement, All-on-X surgery, or occlusal adjustment has been included separately from the planned stay — pass = extension risk is visible as contingency, fail = rewrite the lodging model.

Verify that foreign transaction fees, wire fees, and exchange spread are included when the planned payment method will trigger them — use card issuer disclosures, bank wire schedule, and quoted currency handling rules — pass = payment friction is separately budgeted when applicable, fail = ws-03-d must be completed before budget approval.

Verify that the budget does not count any Cavallini packaged hotel, transfer, imaging, or medication inclusion twice — compare ws-03-a quote lines against non-clinical entries — pass = every packaged inclusion is either priced once or marked included, fail = remove duplicate costs and recalculate.

Verify that the U.S. comparator in ws-03-c has not been padded with Costa Rica-only travel expenses or stripped of equivalent sedation and prosthetic categories — pass = ancillary travel costs sit only on the Costa Rica side, fail = rebuild the comparison model before decision review.

Verify that non-clinical Costa Rica cost categories have been totaled and tested against the quoted clinical fee from ws-03-a — pass = if they exceed 20% of clinical fee the earlier cost conclusion is formally invalidated and recalculated, fail = no approval can be issued until that recalculation occurs.

Verify that all line items marked 'estimate' are tied to a named source and date rather than guessed from memory — pass = each estimated cost has a source note such as airline screenshot date or hotel quote date, fail = replace unsupported estimates.

Verify that the all-in Costa Rica budget includes patient cash exposure before travel, during travel, and after return instead of a single lump sum only — pass = timing-sensitive items are distinguishable for cash-flow planning, fail = send the case to ws-03-d for restructuring before sign-off.

Verify that the final budget output states whether Costa Rica remains lower-cost, marginal-savings/high-friction, justified-overrun, or incomplete for commitment based on entered costs — pass = one status is explicitly assigned, fail = the worksheet is not complete enough to trigger travel readiness.

decision_gates.condition: All non-clinical cost categories required by the chapter narrative are either priced, documented as included by Cavallini, or explicitly marked not applicable with justification; no major travel or recovery exposure is missing.
decision_gates.pass_action: Release the all-in Costa Rica budget to ws-03-c and ws-03-e for final net-cost modeling.
decision_gates.fail_action: Mark financial status incomplete for commitment and hold all booking activity.

decision_gates.condition: If non-clinical Costa Rica costs exceed 20% of the quoted clinical fee, the original cost conclusion has been invalidated and recalculated using the updated full model.
decision_gates.pass_action: Proceed with revised savings analysis only after recalculation is documented.
decision_gates.fail_action: Stop approval and rebuild the cost conclusion before any deposit, airfare, or lodging commitment.
