common_errors: Counting only airfare and hotel while omitting missed work, companion travel, and emergency return-trip exposure, which produces false savings
Using one optimistic hotel stay number instead of separating planned nights from contingency extension nights after swelling, occlusion issues, or sedation recovery
Burying card fees, wire fees, and exchange spread in miscellaneous spending rather than modeling them as a separate payment-friction category on high-value treatment
example_items: Verified that a second planned prosthetic trip for final zirconia delivery was included using the trip architecture from ws-03-a and actual pricing from Delta checkout and Courtyard Escazú nightly rates — pass = airfare USD 912, hotel 6 nights at USD 148 plus tax, food USD 55/day, rideshare USD 120 entered; fail = no second-trip travel lines existed, so net savings was invalidated and the model was rebuilt.

Verified that companion travel was treated as mandatory because the Cavallini quote includes IV sedation for upper-arch All-on-X surgery — source was coordinator email dated 2026-02-15 and clinic discharge guidance — pass = companion airfare USD 875, shared lodging incremental cost USD 0, meals USD 40/day, lost wages USD 1,200 entered; fail would have required classifying the budget as undercounted.

Verified that foreign transaction and wire costs were priced from Chase Sapphire fee disclosure and bank outbound wire schedule — pass = 0% foreign transaction fee on primary card, backup wire fee USD 45, exchange spread exposure estimated at USD 280 entered as separate payment-friction category; fail would have blocked approval until ws-03-d was completed.

worksheets.title: U.S.-Versus-Cavallini Like-for-Like Restorative Cost Comparison Matrix
worksheets.purpose: Produces a decision-grade side-by-side comparison of the normalized U.S. restorative quote and the Cavallini Costa Rica pathway for the same clinical scope and trip assumptions.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 50 minutes on first build, 20 minutes for quote revisions
worksheets.right_column_label: Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Treatment Path

sections.section_title: Normalized Restorative Scope Match Test
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the written Cavallini treatment quote captured in ws-03-a, at least one written U.S. quote for the same restorative intent, and any consultation notes that explain what is or is not included. Do not use verbal estimates or marketing price ranges. You must confirm arch count, implant count, prosthetic type, sedation assumptions, and whether temporaries and finals are included on both sides. This section is complete when the U.S. and Costa Rica pathways can be stated in matched clinical language or the mismatch is explicitly documented as blocking comparison.

fields.label: Restorative case type being compared on both sides
fields.placeholder: Full-arch implant-supported bridge (All-on-X)
fields.validation_hint: Choose the exact same restorative intent for both pathways, such as 'Single-tooth implant with crown' or 'Upper and lower full-mouth restoration'.

fields.label: U.S. quote scope summary exactly as written by the domestic provider
fields.placeholder: Upper arch All-on-4 with extractions and temporary fixed bridge; final zirconia prosthesis quoted separately after healing
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry states the U.S. provider's scope in detail, including whether final prosthetics and sedation are separate or included.

fields.label: Cavallini quote scope summary exactly as written by the San José clinic
fields.placeholder: Upper arch extraction, Nobel Biocare implant placement, immediate provisional fixed bridge, final zirconia on second trip, IV sedation separate
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry mirrors the written Cavallini scope without paraphrasing away key inclusions or exclusions.

fields.label: Like-for-like comparison status after normalization review
fields.placeholder: Requires one clarification before valid comparison
options: Requires one clarification before valid comparison
fields.validation_hint: Select 'Valid like-for-like match' only when implant brand assumptions, prosthetic phase, sedation, and trip count are matched or clearly normalized.

sections.section_title: Clinical Category Cost Alignment
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, use ws-03-a for the Cavallini side and the itemized U.S. estimate or coordinator notes for the domestic side. If a category is missing on either side, mark it missing and do not smooth it into another line item. This section depends on the scope-match test being completed first. This section is complete when each major restorative category has a visible cost position or an explicit missing-data flag on both sides.

fields.label: Diagnostics, imaging, and consultation normalization note across U.S. and Cavallini quotes
fields.placeholder: U.S. CBCT and surgical consult quoted at USD 650; Cavallini diagnostic imaging included in package per PDF page 1
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry shows the category on both sides or clearly states which side has it missing.

fields.label: Implant surgery, extractions, and bone work normalization note across both providers
fields.placeholder: U.S. quote includes implant placement and extractions but excludes sinus lift; Cavallini quote includes extraction and implant placement, grafting not stated
fields.validation_hint: List surgery-related inclusions and exclusions explicitly so hidden add-ons can be seen.

fields.label: Abutments, crowns, bridgework, or full-arch prosthetic normalization note
fields.placeholder: U.S. final zirconia prosthesis quoted separately at USD 9,800; Cavallini final zirconia included on second trip
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry distinguishes provisional from final prosthetic phases and notes any separate charges.

fields.label: Sedation, medications, and immediate follow-up inclusion comparison note
fields.placeholder: U.S. IV sedation listed at USD 1,200; Cavallini IV sedation USD 850 separate; post-op meds included in Costa Rica discharge packet not yet confirmed
fields.validation_hint: A complete note shows whether these support items are included, separate, or unknown on each side.

sections.section_title: Trip Architecture and Access Cost Comparison
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the trip architecture from ws-03-a and the ancillary Costa Rica costs from ws-03-b. Review whether the U.S. option is local or requires regional travel, because a fair comparison still requires noting domestic travel if material. This section cannot be completed accurately until non-clinical Costa Rica costs are captured. This section is complete when trip count, travel burden, and non-clinical access costs are visible as comparison drivers rather than buried assumptions.

fields.label: Expected number of treatment trips required under the U.S. provider pathway
fields.placeholder: Local care; 5 office visits, no overnight travel required
fields.validation_hint: State whether the U.S. pathway is local or requires overnight stays, not just the number of clinical appointments.

fields.label: Expected number of Costa Rica treatment trips required under the Cavallini pathway
fields.placeholder: 2 international trips: surgery/provisional trip and later final-zirconia delivery trip
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry explicitly says 1-trip, 2-trip, or staged multi-trip and names the phase purpose.

fields.label: Costa Rica non-clinical travel and recovery total imported from ws-03-b
fields.validation_hint: Enter the full non-clinical total as a number only, for example 6850 representing USD 6,850.

fields.label: Material domestic travel or lodging cost on the U.S. side if not local
fields.validation_hint: Enter 0 if the U.S. pathway is local and requires no hotel or airfare; otherwise enter the real domestic travel amount.

sections.section_title: Net Difference and Financial Classification
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, confirm that missing categories have been resolved or explicitly identified as blocking. Use totals from ws-03-a and ws-03-b for the Costa Rica side and the normalized U.S. quote totals for the domestic side. This section should not be used to hide mismatches; if the underlying categories are not like-for-like, classify the comparison as invalid. This section is complete when the modeled difference is stated numerically and one valid comparison status is assigned.

fields.label: Normalized total cost of comparable U.S. restorative pathway
fields.validation_hint: Enter the total comparable U.S. cost in dollars as a number only, such as 38000.

fields.label: All-in modeled total cost of Cavallini Costa Rica pathway before complication reserve
fields.validation_hint: Enter the Costa Rica total including clinical fee plus ancillary costs but before reserve, such as 31800.

fields.label: Modeled cost difference before reserve (U.S. total minus Costa Rica total)
fields.validation_hint: Use a positive number when Costa Rica is lower-cost and a negative number when Costa Rica is higher-cost.

fields.label: Comparison classification after like-for-like review
fields.placeholder: Marginal savings - one disruption could erase benefit
options: Costa Rica lower-cost with meaningful margin
Marginal savings - one disruption could erase benefit
Costa Rica higher-cost but may qualify as justified overrun
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status that best matches the real modeled result after normalization, not the marketing headline.

fields.label: Specific mismatch or risk note that could invalidate this comparison if left unresolved
fields.placeholder: Cavallini final prosthesis delivery confirmed on second trip, but U.S. quote still missing whether zirconia final is included; cannot approve until clarified
fields.validation_hint: State the exact unresolved item that would force the comparison to be rebuilt if not clarified.

decision_gates.condition: The U.S. comparator matches Cavallini on restorative intent, arch count, sedation assumptions, provisional/final prosthetic scope, and major categories, or any mismatch is resolved through explicit normalization.
decision_gates.pass_action: Use this matrix as the benchmark for net savings or justified-overrun review in ws-03-e.
decision_gates.fail_action: Invalidate the comparison, rebuild the U.S. comparator, and do not approve Costa Rica on cost grounds.

decision_gates.condition: The Cavallini pathway shows a confirmed 1-trip, 2-trip, or staged multi-trip architecture.
decision_gates.pass_action: Proceed with all-in cost comparison.
decision_gates.fail_action: Assign financial status REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and suspend deposit authorization until trip count is confirmed in writing.

cross_references: Cavallini Restorative Quote Line-Item Capture for Implants, Crowns, and All-on-X
Costa Rica Treatment Budget Inclusion Checklist for Travel, Recovery, and Return-Trip Risk
Complication Reserve and Savings Threshold Approval Form for Dental Tourism Commitment
common_errors: Comparing a full Cavallini treatment bundle against a U.S. surgical-only estimate that excludes final prosthetics, sedation, or post-op visits
Allowing unknown trip count on the Costa Rica side while treating the U.S. side as a single completed episode, which understates international cost exposure
Using a U.S. quote that lacks implant brand, prosthetic material, or arch count specificity, then treating it as a valid benchmark anyway

worksheets.title: Deposit, Final Payment, and Foreign Transaction Timing Planner for Cavallini Care
worksheets.purpose: Maps when money leaves the account, what event makes each payment nonrecoverable, and which payment rails are safe enough to prevent treatment-week funding failure in Costa Rica.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 40 minutes on first build, 15 minutes for updates before travel

sections.section_title: Cavallini Payment Schedule and Trigger Event Mapping
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the written Cavallini quote, coordinator emails about deposits or date holds, and any treatment-plan PDF that states when payments are due. Use the line-item structure from ws-03-a so you can tell whether surgery, temporaries, sedation, and final prosthetics are billed together or in phases. Do not enter rough percentages without the source note that created them. This section is complete when every expected payment has a due timing, amount, and event trigger that explains why the clinic expects the money at that point.

fields.label: Date-hold or scheduling deposit amount required by Cavallini before treatment dates are reserved
fields.validation_hint: Enter the deposit amount as a number only, such as 3000 for USD 3,000.

fields.label: Exact calendar date or milestone when the Cavallini deposit becomes due
fields.placeholder: Due within 5 business days of written treatment-plan acceptance
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry states the real due rule, such as a fixed date or milestone tied to scheduling confirmation.

fields.label: Specific event that makes the Cavallini deposit nonrecoverable
fields.placeholder: Nonrefundable once surgery date is locked and lab planning begins
fields.validation_hint: Name the actual trigger event, not vague language like 'after processing'.

fields.label: Final payment amount due before surgery, prosthetic seating, or discharge
fields.validation_hint: Enter the remaining amount due for the relevant treatment phase as a number only.

fields.label: Exact timing of final payment relative to treatment day
fields.placeholder: Balance due the business day before implant surgery check-in
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry specifies when final payment is expected, such as before sedation day or before prosthetic delivery.

fields.label: Separate payment timing for second-trip final prosthetics if not included in first-trip settlement
fields.placeholder: Final zirconia balance due at second-trip try-in; written terms pending
fields.validation_hint: If the case is one-trip, write 'N/A - one-trip case confirmed'; otherwise state the second-phase rule.

sections.section_title: Refund, Scope-Change, and Cancellation Exposure Control
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, retrieve the refund policy language from the coordinator, any cancellation terms in the treatment proposal, and notes from records-based case review. This section should be filled only after ws-03-a is complete, because scope-change risk depends on what is and is not included in the original quote. Distinguish refundable date holds from clinical deposits and lab commitments. This section is complete when you can identify exactly which payments are recoverable, partially recoverable, or sunk if the scope changes after in-person exam.

fields.label: Refund rule if Cavallini changes treatment scope after in-person exam in San José
fields.placeholder: Coordinator states deposit may be applied to revised plan but airfare and hotel are patient responsibility; no written refund percentage provided yet
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry states the actual scope-change rule and notes whether it is written or verbal.

fields.label: Cancellation rule if the patient withdraws before travel after paying the scheduling deposit
fields.placeholder: USD 3,000 deposit refundable minus USD 500 admin fee until custom lab work starts; after that nonrefundable
fields.validation_hint: State the specific patient-cancellation rule with thresholds and fees, not just 'see policy'.

fields.label: Percent of projected total Costa Rica case cost that becomes nonrefundable before records-based treatment pathway is fully accepted
fields.validation_hint: Enter a percentage number such as 18, not a decimal, representing pre-acceptance nonrecoverable exposure.

fields.label: High-exposure payment structure flag based on the 25 percent pre-acceptance threshold
fields.placeholder: false
fields.validation_hint: Enter true if more than 25% becomes nonrefundable before records transfer and written preliminary treatment pathway are complete; otherwise false.

sections.section_title: Primary and Backup International Payment Rail Verification
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the issuing bank disclosures for the credit card, debit card, or wire account you plan to use, and call the bank to verify foreign usage, transfer limits, and fraud controls. This section must be completed before travel booking is treated as operationally safe, because payment failure can delay surgery or prosthetic delivery. Use actual bank names, card products, and limit confirmations. This section is complete when one primary and one backup payment method are verified for Costa Rica use and the access limits are documented.

fields.label: Primary payment rail designated for Cavallini charges over USD 5,000
fields.placeholder: Chase Sapphire Reserve Visa ending 4421 approved for Costa Rica healthcare charges
fields.validation_hint: Name the exact card or account intended for the main treatment payment, including issuer and identifying detail.

fields.label: Primary payment rail foreign transaction fee or currency spread percentage
fields.validation_hint: Enter the actual fee percentage charged by the primary payment method, such as 0 or 3.

fields.label: Backup payment rail if primary card is blocked or declined during treatment week
fields.placeholder: Bank of America outbound wire account with daily transfer limit raised to USD 25,000 after branch verification
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry names a real backup rail with known access method and limit.

fields.label: Date bank, card issuer, or wire desk confirmed international usage and limit settings
fields.validation_hint: Enter the date you obtained live confirmation from the financial institution, for example 2026-03-04.

sections.section_title: Payment Friction and Cash-Flow Readiness Decision
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, total the payment-friction costs from your card, bank, or wire information and compare them against the treatment payment amount. Review ws-03-b for any duplicated fee entries so payment costs are visible only once. This section should be completed after the payment schedule and rail verification sections are done. This section is complete when payment friction is quantified, compared to the 3 percent threshold, and linked to a clear go/hold status for financial readiness.

fields.label: Combined foreign transaction fee, wire fee, and exchange spread expected on Cavallini payments
fields.validation_hint: Enter the total dollars expected to be lost to payment friction, such as 725.

fields.label: Payment friction exceeds 3 percent of treatment payment amount
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Enter true if combined fees exceed 3% of the treatment payment amount; otherwise false.

fields.label: Cash-flow readiness status for releasing deposit and proceeding toward travel booking
fields.placeholder: On hold pending refund clarification and bank limit confirmation
options: Do not proceed with payment commitment
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status that matches actual readiness after schedule, refund terms, and payment rails are verified.

fields.label: Specific next control action required before any Cavallini payment is sent
fields.placeholder: Obtain written refund terms for scope change after in-person exam and raise backup wire limit above USD 20,000
fields.validation_hint: State the exact pre-payment control step still required, not a generic reminder.

decision_gates.condition: If combined foreign transaction fee, wire fee, and exchange spread exceed 3% of the treatment payment amount, those costs are modeled separately and the net savings calculation is re-approved.
decision_gates.pass_action: Proceed with updated total-cost analysis and payment-method selection.
decision_gates.fail_action: Hold payment approval until friction costs are separately modeled and accepted.

decision_gates.condition: No more than 25% of the projected Costa Rica total becomes nonrefundable before records transfer is complete and a written preliminary treatment pathway tied to actual diagnostics is accepted, unless the patient explicitly flags the case as high exposure.
decision_gates.pass_action: Allow payment timing to move forward to final decision review.
decision_gates.fail_action: Flag the case as high exposure and keep commitment on hold pending clarification or renegotiation of timing.

Costa Rica Treatment Budget Inclusion Checklist for Travel, Recovery, and Return-Trip Risk
Complication Reserve and Savings Threshold Approval Form for Dental Tourism Commitment
common_errors: Recording the total treatment fee without tying each amount to the triggering event that makes it nonrefundable, such as lab start or surgery prep
Assuming a credit card will work internationally without confirming fraud holds, per-transaction limits, or backup payment rails at least a week before travel
Ignoring prosthetic-phase payment timing, which can leave the patient short of funds right before final seating or discharge

worksheets.title: Complication Reserve and Savings Threshold Approval Form for Dental Tourism Commitment
worksheets.purpose: Documents whether Cavallini remains financially acceptable after reserve funding and records the binding approval outcome: net savings, justified overrun, or no-go.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 30 minutes for initial decision, 10 minutes after model changes

sections.section_title: Savings Threshold and Strategic Acceptance Standard
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the final normalized comparison from ws-03-c, the all-in Costa Rica budget from ws-03-b, and the payment exposure findings from ws-03-d. This section defines what financial result must be true before Costa Rica is approved. Do not use emotional preference or marketing language here. This section is complete when a numeric threshold or explicit strategic-justification standard is written clearly enough that a future reviewer could reach the same approval outcome.

fields.label: Minimum net savings in U.S. dollars required before Costa Rica is approved on cost grounds
fields.validation_hint: Enter the minimum acceptable savings threshold as a dollar figure, such as 5000.

fields.label: If Costa Rica is not lower-cost, documented reason a justified overrun could still be accepted
fields.placeholder: Acceptable only if Cavallini offers integrated onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, and materially faster full-arch scheduling than the U.S. provider
fields.validation_hint: State concrete operational reasons that would justify paying more, not vague preference statements.

fields.label: Current modeled cost position after all travel and payment-friction costs but before reserve
fields.placeholder: Costa Rica lower-cost
fields.validation_hint: Choose the modeled position reflected in ws-03-c before applying the reserve.

sections.section_title: Dedicated Complication Reserve Design
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, retrieve the trip architecture from ws-03-a, the travel-risk budget from ws-03-b, and any notes about complexity level such as crowns, limited implant work, multi-unit bridgework, or All-on-X. The reserve must be real money with an identified storage location and immediate access path, not an informal intention to use a credit card later. Use the chapter rule that the reserve must at least cover one emergency return flight, three hotel nights, and local transport. This section is complete when the reserve amount, complexity tier, and access method are all documented and verifiable.

fields.label: Clinical complexity tier used to size the Cavallini complication reserve
fields.placeholder: High-complexity full-mouth / All-on-X
options: Low-complexity crown or limited implant work
fields.validation_hint: Select the tier that matches the actual case architecture, such as low-complexity crown work or high-complexity full-arch treatment.

fields.label: Minimum emergency return-flight cost assumption used in reserve calculation
fields.validation_hint: Enter the assumed cost of one urgent return flight in dollars, such as 1200.

fields.label: Three-night hotel and local-transport assumption used in reserve calculation
fields.validation_hint: Enter the combined lodging and local transport amount for an emergency reassessment stay, such as 650.

fields.label: Total dedicated complication reserve amount set aside before any Cavallini deposit
fields.validation_hint: Enter the actual dollars reserved and available, such as 3500.

fields.label: Where the complication reserve will sit and how fast it can be accessed
fields.placeholder: Ally High Yield Savings ending 1184 with same-day transfer to checking; debit card backup available
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry names the real account or funding source and the access speed.

sections.section_title: Reserve Adequacy and Financial Fragility Test
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, compare the reserve amount against the emergency travel assumptions and the modeled savings from ws-03-c. Apply the chapter thresholds exactly: reserve must cover one emergency return flight plus three hotel nights and local transport, and if modeled savings are positive but less than the reserve, the case is financially fragile. This section depends on both the reserve-design section and the comparison matrix being complete. This section is complete when reserve adequacy and fragility status are both explicitly classified.

fields.label: Reserve is at least equal to one emergency return flight plus three hotel nights and local transport
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Enter true only if the reserve amount fully covers the minimum emergency travel threshold defined above.

fields.label: Modeled net savings after reserve is applied
fields.validation_hint: Enter the remaining savings after subtracting the dedicated reserve, such as 2700. Use a negative number if Costa Rica is now more expensive.

fields.label: Financial fragility classification when savings are positive but less than the reserve amount
fields.placeholder: Financially fragile - explicit sign-off required
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status that reflects whether one moderate disruption could erase the savings.

sections.section_title: Final Cavallini Financial Commitment Decision Record
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, confirm that ws-03-a through ws-03-d are complete and that no blocker remains unresolved. This final section records the only acceptable outcomes for the chapter: approved net savings, approved justified overrun, or rejected/no-go. If the comparison is invalid, reserve is unfunded, or payment timing remains high exposure, this form cannot be approved. This section is complete when one final status is selected, dated, and supported by a written rationale tied to the numbers.

fields.label: Final Chapter 3 financial decision status for Cavallini treatment commitment
fields.placeholder: Approved net savings
fields.validation_hint: Choose exactly one final outcome based on the completed model and reserve status.

fields.label: Written rationale for the final financial decision using actual modeled numbers and operational facts
fields.placeholder: Costa Rica remains USD 6,200 lower than the normalized U.S. comparator before reserve and USD 2,700 lower after a USD 3,500 reserve; two-trip architecture confirmed; payment friction modeled; proceed with caution acknowledgment
fields.validation_hint: A correct rationale cites the actual totals, reserve, trip count, and any strategic reason for approval or rejection.

fields.label: Date the patient signs off on this Chapter 3 financial commitment outcome
fields.validation_hint: Enter the calendar date the final financial status was accepted, such as 2026-03-08.

fields.label: Travel-readiness trigger released for Chapter 4 booking execution
fields.placeholder: false
fields.validation_hint: Enter true only when final status is approved net savings or approved justified overrun and all blockers are cleared.

decision_gates.condition: The dedicated complication reserve is at least equal to one emergency return flight plus three hotel nights and local transport, and the reserve location plus access method are documented.
decision_gates.pass_action: Allow final financial decision review to proceed.
decision_gates.fail_action: Mark reserve status inadequate and do not approve final commitment even if Costa Rica appears cheaper.

decision_gates.condition: Either Costa Rica shows net savings after reserve and all modeled costs, or a written strategic justification for justified overrun is explicitly accepted; otherwise the outcome is no-go.
decision_gates.pass_action: If approved, release Chapter 4 travel-readiness trigger with the documented decision package.
decision_gates.fail_action: Record rejected on cost control grounds or financially underprepared and block booking execution.

chapters.chapter_title: Passport-to-Chair Travel Execution, Sedation-Compatible Scheduling, and San José Recovery Logistics Setup

worksheets.title: Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage
worksheets.purpose: Creates a single dependency-controlled inventory of every trip component required to get a U.S. restorative dental patient from home departure to Cavallini treatment attendance and immediate recovery in San José.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 35 minutes on first build, 10 minutes for each itinerary revision
table_columns: Trip Dependency Component for Cavallini Treatment Sequence
Booked Vendor / Responsible Party and Direct Contact Method
Date-Time Window This Component Must Support
Linked Cavallini Event or Recovery Function
Primary Confirmation Number or Evidence Source
Backup Plan if This Component Fails
Current Status (Not Started / Held Pending Clinical Sequence / Confirmed / Needs Revision)

decision_gates.condition: Cavallini has provided a date-linked sequence including at least a pre-op attendance window, procedure date, and expected local recovery duration.
decision_gates.pass_action: Treat flights, lodging, and transfer bookings as eligible for confirmation and populate all dependent rows in this inventory.
decision_gates.fail_action: Set affected rows to Held Pending Clinical Sequence, mark overall travel status HOLD FOR CLINICAL SEQUENCE, and escalate back to clinic scheduling coordination before any nonrefundable booking.

decision_gates.gate_title: Escort Coverage Gate for Sedation or Major Surgery
decision_gates.condition: If IV sedation, major implant surgery, or extensive same-day prosthetic work is in scope, a named escort with direct contact information and procedure-day availability is documented.
decision_gates.pass_action: Mark sedation-day discharge transport and first recovery-phase support as confirmed.
decision_gates.fail_action: Mark itinerary SEDATION ITINERARY NONCOMPLIANT and do not treat trip logistics as complete until the escort dependency is resolved or the treatment plan is restructured.

Using generic airline or hotel confirmation emails without recording the exact Cavallini-linked clinic date each booking is supporting, which makes rebooking decisions impossible during disruption.
Recording 'companion coming' without a named escort, direct mobile number, and procedure-day physical availability window for IV sedation or major implant surgery.

worksheets.title: Passport Validity, Entry Document, and Sedation-Day Escort Compliance Checklist for Costa Rica Dental Travel
worksheets.purpose: Verifies that legal travel readiness, return eligibility, and escort-dependent sedation compliance are complete before tickets and procedure dates are treated as operationally locked.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 25 minutes on first review, 5 minutes for pre-departure recheck
checklist_items: Verify that the physical U.S. passport is in hand by checking the actual booklet, not a renewal receipt or photo copy — pass = booklet is physically available for packing today, fail = stop airfare finalization and escalate immediately to passport replacement or treatment-date movement.

Verify that the passport expiration date supports the planned Costa Rica trip window by inspecting the data page of the passport booklet — pass = expiration is clearly after the planned return date with no same-week uncertainty, fail = mark trip readiness blocked and escalate to expedited document resolution before any nonrefundable booking.

Verify that the passport is not materially damaged by examining the data page, cover, and page integrity under normal light — pass = no tearing, water damage, detached binding, or unreadable data page, fail = treat passport as noncompliant and initiate replacement rather than gambling on airline acceptance.

Verify that the airline reservation name exactly matches the passport name by comparing the booking confirmation against the passport data page character-for-character — pass = complete match including middle names or initials used by the airline, fail = correct the reservation before check-in risk is created.

Verify that the patient has the documents needed to re-enter the United States on the planned return itinerary by reviewing the passport and return booking file together — pass = same passport supports departure, entry to Costa Rica, and boarding home, fail = trip remains blocked until the return-document issue is resolved.

Verify that passport readiness remains stable within 45 days of departure by checking whether any renewal, replacement, or correction request is still pending — pass = no unresolved document process remains open, fail = escalate to expedited resolution and move the treatment date instead of assuming turnaround.

Verify that the planned return date on the itinerary still matches the passport-based travel window by comparing the latest airline confirmation to the passport expiration date — pass = return date remains within the document window, fail = revise the trip or documents before tickets are treated as final.

Verify that a digital backup of the passport identity page is stored in the travel folder and accessible from the phone without checked baggage — pass = file opens offline and is legible, fail = create the backup before departure.

Verify that the patient has a printed copy of the outbound and return itinerary in the carry-on document set by checking the physical travel folder — pass = printed itinerary is present and readable, fail = print and insert before packing is marked complete.

Verify that the Cavallini clinic has the current arrival date and contactable mobile number by checking the most recent email or WhatsApp exchange with scheduling — pass = clinic acknowledgement exists in writing, fail = send same-day operational update before travel.

Verify that the treatment plan has been reviewed for escort dependency by checking the written clinical sequence for IV sedation, major implant surgery, or extensive same-day prosthetic work — pass = escort requirement is clearly identified as yes or no, fail = request clarification from Cavallini before treating the itinerary as compliant.

Verify that a named sedation-day escort is assigned by reviewing this checklist against the travel roster — pass = one specific person is listed by full name, fail = itinerary remains noncompliant until a substitute is confirmed or sedation is restructured.

Verify that the named escort’s direct mobile number is documented and tested by calling or messaging the escort from the patient’s phone — pass = successful live contact or confirmed reply is logged, fail = obtain a working number before departure.

Verify that the escort will be physically present in San José on the procedure date by comparing their travel confirmation or local presence plan against the Cavallini surgery schedule — pass = documented overlap exists for the full discharge window, fail = treat escort coverage as absent and escalate to clinic scheduling.

Verify that the escort understands the discharge role by reviewing written discharge-support expectations with them before travel — pass = escort confirms they will accompany the patient from clinic to lodging and monitor first transport, fail = replace or brief the escort before sedation-day approval is granted.

Verify that escort lodging alignment supports immediate post-sedation transport by comparing the escort’s hotel booking or local address to the patient’s surgery-day lodging — pass = escort can realistically reach the clinic and lodging without reliance on the sedated patient, fail = revise lodging or escort plan.

Verify that a backup escort path or clinic-approved alternative has been explored if the primary escort cancels by checking written notes or clinic communication — pass = substitute path is documented, fail = elevate risk status and contact Cavallini before travel.

Verify that no escort-dependent sedation case remains unresolved within 24 hours of the scheduled procedure by reviewing the final pre-op status — pass = escort is confirmed and reachable, fail = mark the itinerary SEDATION ITINERARY NONCOMPLIANT and contact the clinic immediately.

Verify that all critical travel documents needed on arrival are assigned to carry-on rather than checked baggage by physically inspecting the carry-on packout — pass = passport, itinerary, and clinic contact sheet are in carry-on, fail = repack before airport departure.

Verify that the patient can contact the airline, hotel, escort, and Cavallini clinic if an itinerary disruption occurs by checking the phone contact list and charger availability — pass = all four contact paths are saved and accessible, fail = build the contact sheet before travel readiness is approved.

Verify that any travel disruption affecting pre-op, surgery, or discharge transport within 48 hours will trigger same-day clinic notification by reviewing the communication rule with the patient — pass = patient acknowledges the rule and clinic contact path is ready, fail = do not close this checklist until escalation steps are documented.

Verify that the current itinerary has not changed since the last escort compliance review by comparing the latest airline confirmation to the escort plan date-time entries — pass = dates still align, fail = re-run escort verification immediately.

Verify that return flight timing does not force commercial travel before discharge transport and immediate recovery needs are met by comparing the flight record against the current treatment sequence — pass = no same-day compression exists against clinic expectations, fail = rebook or extend lodging before trip approval.

Verify that this checklist has been re-reviewed after any change in arrival date, procedure date, escort, or lodging by checking the revision log or date of last review — pass = review date reflects the latest change, fail = treat compliance status as stale and reopen the checklist.

decision_gates.condition: Passport is in hand, undamaged, matches the booking name, and supports departure to Costa Rica and return to the United States on the planned dates.
decision_gates.pass_action: Approve legal travel readiness and allow ticketing and departure preparation to continue.
decision_gates.fail_action: Stop trip execution immediately, delay airfare finalization if not yet booked, and escalate to expedited document resolution or treatment-date movement.

decision_gates.condition: For any IV sedation or escort-dependent surgery case, a named escort with direct contact information, procedure-day presence, and lodging alignment is confirmed no later than 24 hours before the procedure.
decision_gates.pass_action: Set status to SEDATION-COMPATIBLE ITINERARY CONFIRMED.
decision_gates.fail_action: Set status to SEDATION ITINERARY NONCOMPLIANT and escalate to Cavallini to restructure or postpone the sedation-dependent plan.

cross_references: Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage
Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner
common_errors: Assuming a valid passport alone means trip readiness without verifying exact name match to the airline reservation and physical passport condition.
Treating an escort as 'available' because they verbally agreed, without documenting their direct contact number, same-day physical presence, and lodging alignment for post-sedation discharge.
Failing to re-check document and escort status within 48 hours of departure after a schedule change, leaving stale compliance assumptions in place.
example_items: Verified that the airline reservation name exactly matches the passport name by comparing the AA confirmation to the passport data page on 06/03/2026 — pass: both show 'EMILY RUTH CARTER' with no spacing or middle-name mismatch; no correction required.

Verified that a named sedation-day escort is assigned by reviewing the surgery plan and companion file on 06/04/2026 — pass: Lisa Moreno, mobile +1 512-555-0149, arrives 06/16/2026 and remains through 06/19/2026; escort compliance met.

Verified that all critical travel documents are in carry-on by physically inspecting the black zipper document pouch on 06/15/2026 — pass: passport booklet, printed return itinerary, Cavallini appointment email, and backup passport copy all present; carry-on continuity requirement met.

worksheets.title: Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner
worksheets.purpose: Maps the confirmed Cavallini clinical sequence against international travel timing so the patient can approve only itineraries with adequate disruption margin before treatment and after clinically significant events.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 40 minutes on first build, 15 minutes after each schedule revision

sections.section_title: Confirmed Cavallini Clinical Sequence Anchors
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the latest written appointment sequence from Cavallini, including the pre-op attendance window, procedure date, estimated chair duration, any onsite lab dependencies, and the clinic’s expected local recovery period. Do not use memory or an earlier email thread if the schedule has changed. This section depends on the clinic having accepted the diagnostic records packet and issued a date-linked treatment path. This section is complete when every clinically consequential event for this trip phase is dated, timed if known, and tied to the specific treatment objective in Costa Rica.

fields.label: Cavallini treatment phase being scheduled on this trip
fields.placeholder: All-on-X upper arch extractions, implant placement, and provisional delivery
options: Single crown or multi-crown restorative visit
Full-mouth restoration with mixed surgery and prosthetics
fields.validation_hint: Choose the actual trip phase such as 'Implant placement with IV sedation' or 'Final zirconia bridge delivery after osseointegration.'

fields.label: Written Cavallini pre-op appointment date and local San José time
fields.placeholder: 06/17/2026 — 9:00 AM CST (San José)
fields.validation_hint: Enter both date and local Costa Rica time exactly as provided by Cavallini.

fields.label: Written Cavallini primary procedure date and planned start time
fields.placeholder: 06/18/2026 — 8:00 AM implant surgery with IV sedation
fields.validation_hint: Example: '07/09/2026 — 7:30 AM full-arch surgery and immediate provisional workflow.'

fields.label: Estimated chair-time duration for the main procedure as communicated by Cavallini
fields.placeholder: Approximately 5–6 hours including sedation recovery before discharge
fields.validation_hint: Record the clinic’s estimate, not your guess.

fields.label: Onsite lab, prosthetic try-in, or specialist dependency that affects same-trip timing
fields.placeholder: Provisional bridge adjustment expected on 06/19/2026; onsite lab may require same-day return if bite refinement is needed
fields.validation_hint: Describe any lab-linked or specialist-linked step that could delay departure.

fields.label: Clinic-stated minimum number of local recovery days after this treatment phase
fields.validation_hint: Enter the numeric minimum communicated by Cavallini, such as 3 for post-op observation through Day 5.

fields.label: Medication pickup and first-dose timing requirement after procedure
fields.placeholder: Pharmacy pickup immediately after discharge; first antibiotic dose within 1 hour of hotel return
fields.validation_hint: Include timing, not just the fact that medications exist.

sections.section_title: Outbound Flight Margin and Arrival Robustness Test
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the current outbound airline itinerary, connection times, expected baggage plan, and the route from Juan Santamaría International Airport to the clinic-area lodging. Review failure scenarios from this chapter, especially immigration delay, baggage delay, and traffic into San José. This section depends on the clinical anchors in Section 1 being complete. This section is complete when the chosen arrival plan can absorb routine disruption without causing loss of the first required Cavallini event.

fields.label: Selected outbound flight path from U.S. origin airport to SJO
fields.placeholder: Austin (AUS) → Dallas/Fort Worth (DFW) → San José (SJO) on American Airlines
fields.validation_hint: List the full route, not just the final destination.

fields.label: Scheduled touchdown time in San José for the chosen arrival itinerary
fields.placeholder: 06/16/2026 — 1:55 PM CST (San José)
fields.validation_hint: Use local arrival time in Costa Rica.

fields.label: Disruption buffer between scheduled SJO arrival and first required Cavallini event
fields.placeholder: 19 hours 5 minutes between touchdown and 06/17/2026 9:00 AM pre-op
fields.validation_hint: Express as elapsed time, not as 'arrive day before.'

fields.label: Would a delay of four hours or less cause loss of the first clinic event
fields.placeholder: false
fields.validation_hint: False means the itinerary survives a routine disruption; true means the itinerary fails robustness review.

fields.label: Arrival compression risk classification for this Cavallini trip phase
fields.placeholder: Low risk — arrival day before pre-op with hotel check-in margin
options: Low risk — full buffer before first clinic event
Moderate risk — manageable but sensitive to one disruption
High risk — same-day arrival or narrow margin
Unacceptable — one routine delay breaks the sequence
fields.validation_hint: Choose the risk level that matches the actual timeline margin.

fields.label: Decision on same-day arrival before chair time for this treatment phase
fields.placeholder: Rejected for major implant surgery due to fatigue and immigration-delay risk
options: Rejected — major restorative case requires pre-treatment overnight
Accepted only with explicit Cavallini instruction
fields.validation_hint: Record whether same-day arrival is accepted or rejected and why.

fields.label: Rebooking trigger if arrival margin becomes non-robust
fields.placeholder: If any schedule change reduces buffer below one full disruption window, move to earlier inbound flight or add pre-treatment hotel night and notify Cavallini same day
fields.validation_hint: State the exact trigger and corrective action.

sections.section_title: Post-Procedure Local Stay and Return Flight Clearance Logic
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the current return itinerary, the clinic’s stated observation period, expected follow-up check timing, and any known provisional or bite-adjustment appointments. Review hotel booking flexibility and airfare change rules alongside the treatment schedule. This section depends on Section 1 and Section 2 being complete because departure can only be judged against a real procedure timeline. This section is complete when the return flight is shown to occur after all clinically significant events and after the planned local recovery window, with an extension path documented if recovery runs long.

fields.label: Scheduled return flight departure from SJO
fields.validation_hint: Use the booked departure from Costa Rica, not the final U.S. arrival time.

fields.label: Last clinically significant Cavallini event before airport travel
fields.placeholder: 06/20/2026 — 10:00 AM post-op review and provisional bite adjustment
fields.validation_hint: Examples include post-op review, medication reconciliation, bite check, discharge clearance, or prosthetic delivery.

fields.label: Elapsed buffer between last clinically significant event and airport departure
fields.placeholder: 26 hours 40 minutes
fields.validation_hint: Calculate the time gap; do not write 'next day.'

fields.label: Flexible lodging extension capacity if Cavallini requests additional local observation
fields.placeholder: Current hotel can extend 2 extra nights at medical rate if requested by 11:00 AM prior day
fields.validation_hint: Record the real extension mechanism and cutoff.

fields.label: Return itinerary status against current recovery assumptions
fields.placeholder: Aligned to clinic sequence with extension reserve
options: Aligned to clinic sequence with extension reserve
Aligned but fragile if swelling or adjustment needs increase
Misaligned — return too early for likely recovery window
fields.validation_hint: Select the status that reflects the documented schedule, not your preference.

fields.label: Commercial travel departure rule for this trip phase
fields.placeholder: Do not depart until medications are in hand, discharge instructions are understood, oral intake is tolerable as expected, and Cavallini has not directed continued local stay
fields.validation_hint: State the operational rule exactly as it will be followed.

sections.section_title: Trip Readiness Status and Escalation Outputs
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the finished timing analysis, the logistics inventory, and the legal-readiness checklist. Confirm whether any unresolved travel issue alters events within 48 hours of pre-op, surgery, or discharge transport. This section depends on the earlier sections of this planner and on ws-04-a and ws-04-b being updated to match the current itinerary. This section is complete when a clear go/hold status is recorded, failure triggers are named, and the next escalation path is explicit.

fields.label: Current itinerary decision status for Cavallini appointment-sequenced treatment
fields.placeholder: SEDATION-COMPATIBLE ITINERARY CONFIRMED
options: TRIP READY FOR APPOINTMENT-SEQUENCED TREATMENT EXECUTION
fields.validation_hint: Choose the exact operational status supported by the evidence.

fields.label: Specific event that currently blocks completion if status is not trip-ready
fields.placeholder: Return flight departs before planned Day-3 post-op review for full-arch provisional adjustment
fields.validation_hint: If nothing blocks completion, state 'No current blocker.'

fields.label: Clinic communication deadline if itinerary changes within 48 hours of pre-op, surgery, or discharge transport
fields.placeholder: Same-day notice by WhatsApp and email to Cavallini scheduling as soon as disruption is known
fields.validation_hint: Record both timing and channel.

fields.label: Date this timing plan was last rebuilt from current confirmations
fields.validation_hint: Use the date of the latest full review after changes were incorporated.

fields.label: Operator acknowledgement that stale assumptions were removed after the last schedule change
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: True only if flights, lodging, and clinic timing now all reflect the latest version.

decision_gates.condition: Scheduled arrival in San José provides at least one full disruption buffer before the first required Cavallini event, and a delay of four hours or less would not cause loss of that event.
decision_gates.pass_action: Approve the outbound timing as robust enough for clinic scheduling and travel execution.
decision_gates.fail_action: Reject the itinerary, rebook to an earlier arrival or add a pre-treatment hotel night, and withhold appointment confirmation until a robust itinerary is documented.

decision_gates.condition: Return flight occurs only after the last clinically significant event and after the clinic-stated local recovery period, with a documented extension path if additional observation is needed.
decision_gates.pass_action: Approve the return timing as compatible with the treatment phase.
decision_gates.fail_action: Rebuild return travel and lodging before trip readiness can be issued.

cross_references: Passport Validity, Entry Document, and Sedation-Day Escort Compliance Checklist for Costa Rica Dental Travel
Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage
San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form
common_errors: Planning around the cheapest fare instead of the clinic’s actual pre-op, surgery, lab, and recovery sequence, which creates same-day compression on arrival or departure.
Treating only chair-time appointments as important while ignoring clinically consequential events such as medication pickup, bite checks, provisional delivery, and discharge clearance.
Using airline schedule times without stress-testing whether a four-hour delay, missed connection, baggage delay, or San José traffic would break the first clinic event.

worksheets.title: San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form
worksheets.purpose: Builds the local operating plan for lodging, clinic transfers, pharmacy access, and emergency fallback so post-procedure movement in San José does not rely on improvisation.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 30 minutes on first setup, 10 minutes for route or lodging updates

sections.section_title: Recovery Lodging Suitability Near Cavallini Clinic
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the hotel confirmation, Cavallini lodging guidance if provided, the property address, amenity list, and any communication from an onsite or affiliated hotel coordinator. Review the treatment intensity and expected recovery window from the appointment planner before selecting lodging. This section depends on the appointment sequence being known well enough to estimate the recovery period and follow-up attendance needs. This section is complete when the chosen lodging is shown to support post-op rest, clinic proximity, communication access, and the expected length of stay with extension capability.

fields.label: Chosen San José lodging for the Cavallini treatment phase
fields.placeholder: Cavallini-affiliated onsite hotel adjacent to clinic complex
fields.validation_hint: Record the exact property name or affiliated arrangement, not 'hotel near clinic.'

fields.label: Lodging address or pickup-recognizable location for drivers in San José
fields.placeholder: Rohrmoser, San José — clinic-affiliated hotel entrance beside Cavallini complex
fields.validation_hint: Enter a driver-usable location string.

fields.label: Estimated one-way transfer time from lodging to Cavallini clinic under normal traffic
fields.placeholder: 3 minutes by hotel shuttle / 8 minutes on foot
fields.validation_hint: Use a realistic local estimate, not 'close by.'

fields.label: Post-op suitability features confirmed at this lodging
fields.placeholder: Elevator access, in-room soft-food availability, private bathroom, reliable Wi‑Fi
options: Quiet room away from street noise
Companion can stay in same room
Late checkout or medical extension available
fields.validation_hint: Select all features actually confirmed with the property or coordinator.

fields.label: Medical-stay extension rule for additional nights if swelling or follow-up delays departure
fields.placeholder: Can extend nightly at clinic rate if requested before 11:00 AM; coordinator confirms by WhatsApp
fields.validation_hint: Document the actual extension mechanism and cutoff time.

fields.label: Lodging risk note if not using Cavallini-related onsite accommodation
fields.placeholder: Independent hotel requires 20–30 minute rides in traffic; escort must manage route after sedation without patient input
fields.validation_hint: If using affiliated lodging, note 'No additional routing risk beyond standard transfer coordination.'

sections.section_title: Assigned Local Movement Segments From Airport Through Recovery
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the airline itinerary, hotel booking, clinic appointment times, and any transfer offers from the hotel, clinic, or private driver. Map each movement segment separately instead of assuming one transport method covers the entire stay. This section depends on a chosen lodging location and a date-linked clinic sequence. This section is complete when every required local movement has a named transport method, responsible party, contact path, and timing window.

fields.label: Assigned transport from SJO airport to first-night lodging
fields.placeholder: Pre-booked private driver arranged by hotel coordinator — meet at SJO arrivals exit with printed surname sign
fields.validation_hint: Name the actual method and pickup protocol.

fields.label: Assigned transport from lodging to Cavallini pre-op appointment
fields.placeholder: Hotel shuttle departs 8:15 AM for 9:00 AM pre-op review
fields.validation_hint: Include departure timing tied to the appointment.

fields.label: Assigned transport from Cavallini discharge to lodging after sedation or surgery
fields.placeholder: Escort Lisa Moreno accompanies patient in clinic-arranged car directly back to affiliated hotel room
fields.validation_hint: This field must not be left vague for sedation or major surgery days.

fields.label: Assigned pharmacy-access route for same-day medication pickup if not dispensed onsite
fields.placeholder: Clinic staff directs escort to affiliated pharmacy 4 minutes away; driver waits during pickup
fields.validation_hint: State exactly how medications will be obtained after discharge.

fields.label: Average traffic-sensitive travel time for the most critical local segment
fields.placeholder: Airport to hotel may take 35–60 minutes at 3:00 PM weekday arrival
fields.validation_hint: Use the segment most likely to threaten the schedule if delayed.

fields.label: Local movement segment most vulnerable to patient impairment after treatment
fields.placeholder: Clinic discharge to hotel after IV sedation
options: Airport to hotel after international arrival fatigue
Clinic discharge to hotel after IV sedation
Hotel to follow-up review while swollen or on medications
Return transfer to airport during recovery
fields.validation_hint: Choose the segment where improvisation would be most dangerous.

sections.section_title: Driver Contacts, Pickup Instructions, and Fallback Routing
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, collect the direct mobile numbers for the hotel coordinator, private driver, escort, rideshare backup, and clinic front desk or patient coordinator. Confirm pickup locations in writing when possible, especially at the airport and on surgery discharge day. This section depends on the movement segments in Section 2 being assigned. This section is complete when a third party could execute the route plan using these instructions without needing the patient to improvise after treatment.

fields.label: Primary local routing coordinator for this San José treatment trip
fields.placeholder: Cavallini hotel coordinator via WhatsApp +506 8888-2147
fields.validation_hint: Record the real coordinator and direct channel.

fields.label: Exact airport pickup instruction for arrival at Juan Santamaría International Airport
fields.placeholder: After customs, exit main arrivals door, turn right to authorized pickup area, look for driver holding 'CARTER / CAVALLINI' sign, contact WhatsApp if not visible within 10 minutes
fields.validation_hint: Write a step-by-step instruction the traveler or escort can follow under fatigue.

fields.label: Backup ground transport if primary airport or clinic transfer fails
fields.placeholder: Official airport taxi stand as fallback; escort holds hotel address in Spanish and English
fields.validation_hint: State the actual fallback, not 'call another ride.'

fields.label: Emergency transport fallback if post-op symptoms make standard car transfer unsuitable
fields.placeholder: Call clinic first for routing; if urgent and instructed, request ambulance via Costa Rica emergency services 9-1-1 and notify hotel front desk
fields.validation_hint: Record the escalation path for true transport failure or medical escalation.

fields.label: Can the escort execute all planned routes without the patient giving directions
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: True only if the escort has the addresses, contacts, and pickup instructions independently.

sections.section_title: Recovery-Environment Handoff Readiness
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the lodging suitability, assigned routes, driver contacts, and medication-access plan together with the current treatment schedule. Check that the first 24 hours after discharge can be executed even if the patient is fatigued, swollen, sedated, or dealing with delayed luggage. This section depends on all prior sections and on the packing worksheet for communication and document continuity. This section is complete when the local recovery environment is stable enough to hand off to the recovery-monitoring domain without preventable routing gaps.

fields.label: First-24-hours-in-San-José recovery environment status
fields.placeholder: Recovery environment complete
options: Recovery environment fragile — one missing dependency remains
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status supported by the documented lodging, transport, and supply plan.

fields.label: Specific missing local dependency if recovery environment is not complete
fields.placeholder: No confirmed same-day pharmacy route after surgery if medications are not dispensed onsite
fields.validation_hint: If complete, enter 'No open local dependency.'

fields.label: Date local routing plan was last verified against current flight and appointment confirmations
fields.validation_hint: Use the latest date all route assumptions were checked against actual bookings.

fields.label: Operator acknowledgement that any disruption within 48 hours will trigger same-day clinic notification and route-plan revision
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: True only if the escalation rule is understood and actionable.

decision_gates.condition: For surgery or sedation days, the clinic-to-lodging discharge segment is fully assigned with a transport method, responsible escort or driver, and fallback path.
decision_gates.pass_action: Approve local discharge routing as executable.
decision_gates.fail_action: Block completion of the worksheet and do not treat the recovery environment as ready until discharge transport is defined.

decision_gates.condition: Lodging covers the expected treatment and recovery window, medication access is routed, communication is available, and backup transport exists if the primary transfer fails.
decision_gates.pass_action: Hand off local readiness to Costa Rica Recovery Monitoring and Return-to-Travel Clearance.
decision_gates.fail_action: Set status to RECOVERY ENVIRONMENT INCOMPLETE and revise lodging, routing, or support coverage before handoff.

worksheets.title: Major Restorative Dental Travel Packing and Payment Instrument Preparation Sheet
worksheets.purpose: Prepares the carry-on-critical items, recovery supplies, and primary/backup payment paths needed to attend Cavallini treatment and survive the first 24 hours in San José even if checked luggage is delayed.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 35 minutes on first pack plan, 15 minutes for final pre-departure verification

sections.section_title: Carry-On Continuity Kit for Arrival, Intake, and First-Night Recovery
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the passport, printed itinerary, Cavallini appointment confirmations, current medication list, and the first 24-hour recovery items you would need if checked luggage disappeared overnight. Physically place these items into the intended carry-on while filling out the section. This section depends on the travel dates and clinic sequence already being confirmed. This section is complete when the patient could land in San José, check into lodging, attend intake, pay charges, and manage the first night of recovery using carry-on contents alone.

fields.label: Carry-on document pouch contents for Costa Rica dental travel
fields.placeholder: Passport booklet, printed round-trip itinerary, Cavallini appointment email printout, passport ID-page copy, hotel confirmation, driver instructions
fields.validation_hint: List the actual documents physically placed in the carry-on pouch.

fields.label: Current medication list carried in accessible form for Cavallini intake and medication reconciliation
fields.placeholder: Amlodipine 5 mg daily; Levothyroxine 75 mcg each morning; Ibuprofen avoided due to gastric history
fields.validation_hint: Include medication name, strength, and dosing pattern.

fields.label: Essential prescription medications packed in carry-on with original containers where appropriate
fields.placeholder: 7-day supply of levothyroxine and blood-pressure medication in labeled pharmacy bottles in front zipper pocket
fields.validation_hint: Name the medications and where they are packed.

fields.label: First-24-hours recovery items packed in carry-on for post-procedure comfort
fields.placeholder: Phone charger, lip balm, gauze, neck pillow, protein drink packets, spoon, extra-large button-front shirt
fields.validation_hint: List only items that would matter on arrival day or first recovery night.

fields.label: Digital backup location for passport copy, itinerary, and clinic contact sheet
fields.placeholder: Offline files saved in iPhone Files app and Google Drive folder 'Cavallini June 2026'
fields.validation_hint: State where the backups can be accessed without checked baggage.

fields.label: Carry-on continuity status if checked baggage is delayed until the next day
fields.placeholder: Can still attend pre-op and manage first recovery night
options: Can still attend pre-op and manage first recovery night
Can attend clinic but recovery comfort is weak
Cannot safely operate without checked bag items
