sections.section_title: Treatment-Phase Clothing and Recovery Supply Loadout
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the planned procedure intensity, expected swelling, dietary limitations, and how the patient will dress for airport transit, clinic attendance, and return to lodging. Gather the actual clothing and comfort supplies rather than drafting from memory. This section depends on knowing whether the trip involves crowns, implant surgery, or full-arch work with provisionals. This section is complete when the clothing and recovery loadout supports clinic access, swelling accommodation, oral recovery, and airport movement without relying on shopping after arrival.

fields.label: Clothing chosen for procedure day and post-sedation discharge comfort
fields.placeholder: Loose black zip-front hoodie, elastic-waist joggers, slip-on shoes, no tight collar over jaw swelling area
fields.validation_hint: Record the actual outfit or garment set prepared for surgery day.

fields.label: Soft-diet or oral-recovery support items packed for the San José stay
fields.placeholder: Protein shakes, electrolyte packets, instant oatmeal cups, silicone baby spoon, travel mug for lukewarm liquids
fields.validation_hint: List items that support the actual post-op diet, not general snacks.

fields.label: Medication-timing and symptom-management aids packed for the hotel room
fields.placeholder: Pill organizer, phone alarms preset for antibiotic schedule, small notebook for pain and swelling log, cold-pack sleeve if hotel freezer available
fields.validation_hint: List the tools that help execute the first 72 hours after treatment.

fields.label: Items intentionally left out of checked baggage because they are mission-critical to treatment execution
fields.placeholder: Passport, medications, payment cards, chargers, eyeglasses, medication list, first-night gauze and comfort items
fields.validation_hint: Name the items you protected from baggage delay.

fields.label: Would lost checked luggage create a same-day treatment blocker under the current packing plan
fields.placeholder: false
fields.validation_hint: False means all mission-critical items are already in carry-on.

sections.section_title: Primary and Backup Payment Execution for Cavallini Charges
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the approved payment calendar from the cost-control domain, the expected amount due on arrival or procedure day, the actual cards or accounts to be used, and any recent issuer communication about foreign transactions. Check online account limits and test that issuer support numbers are reachable internationally. This section depends on the trip-phase payment amount already being approved upstream. This section is complete when one primary and one backup payment path are documented, usable in Costa Rica, and sufficient to cover near-term charges without relying on guesswork.

fields.label: Primary payment instrument designated for Cavallini charges due on this trip phase
fields.placeholder: Chase Sapphire Visa ending 4421 — planned for $8,500 procedure installment
fields.validation_hint: Name the actual card or account and its intended use.

fields.label: Backup payment instrument that could independently cover near-term charges if the primary is declined
fields.placeholder: Charles Schwab debit card ending 1184 with sufficient balance for hotel, medications, and emergency clinic charges
fields.validation_hint: The backup must be real and usable, not hypothetical family help.

fields.label: Expected largest single Cavallini-related charge during this travel window
fields.validation_hint: Enter the numeric amount in U.S. dollars expected for the largest single charge.

fields.label: Card issuer notification or foreign-use readiness status for the primary payment instrument
fields.placeholder: Confirmed foreign use not blocked / travel notice documented
options: Confirmed foreign use not blocked / travel notice documented
Issuer says travel notice not required but card tested
fields.validation_hint: Choose the actual issuer-readiness state.

fields.label: International fraud-resolution contact path if primary card is flagged in Costa Rica
fields.placeholder: Chase collect support via app secure chat and back-of-card international number saved in phone
fields.validation_hint: Record the exact support path available from Costa Rica.

fields.label: Payment readiness status for departure
fields.placeholder: Primary and backup both sufficient
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status that matches the evidence from your actual instruments.

sections.section_title: Departure-Ready Verification for Documents, Power, and Execution Continuity
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, inspect the packed carry-on, wallet, chargers, phone, and contact list together. Cross-check against the travel logistics inventory and local routing form so nothing required on arrival or surgery day is missing. This section depends on all prior packing and payment sections being complete. This section is complete when the patient can depart the U.S. with verified documents, communication power, money access, and first-24-hours treatment continuity intact.

fields.label: Phone power and charging continuity for airport, clinic communication, and post-op coordination
fields.placeholder: iPhone charger, USB-C battery pack 20,000 mAh, Costa Rica-compatible wall plug setup packed in carry-on
fields.validation_hint: List the actual power items packed for uninterrupted communication.

fields.label: Printed and digital contact sheet for Cavallini, hotel, driver, escort, and card issuers
fields.placeholder: Printed one-page contact sheet in document pouch; duplicate stored offline on phone
fields.validation_hint: State where both the print and digital versions are stored.

fields.label: Final pre-departure date when carry-on mission-critical items were physically verified
fields.validation_hint: Use the last date the packed carry-on was physically checked, not merely planned.

fields.label: Overall departure continuity status if checked baggage fails and one payment card is declined
fields.placeholder: Operationally ready despite one failure path
options: Operationally ready despite one failure path
Ready but fragile if both baggage and payment issues occur
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status that reflects both packing and payment resilience.

decision_gates.condition: The patient can land, attend Cavallini intake, pay immediate charges, and manage the first post-op night using carry-on contents alone if checked baggage is delayed.
decision_gates.pass_action: Approve packing continuity for departure.
decision_gates.fail_action: Repack mission-critical items into carry-on and do not close this worksheet until the lost-luggage failure mode is neutralized.

decision_gates.condition: At least one primary and one backup payment path are documented and the backup can independently cover near-term charges due on arrival or procedure day.
decision_gates.pass_action: Approve payment readiness for departure.
decision_gates.fail_action: Set status to PAYMENT FRAGILE and delay departure or treatment-day commitment until a second usable payment path is established.

cross_references: Costa Rica Surgical Trip Logistics Inventory for Flights, Lodging, Transfers, and Escort Coverage
Cavallini Appointment-to-Flight Timing Planner
San José Post-Procedure Lodging and Ground Transport Routing Form
common_errors: Packing passports, medication lists, payment cards, or chronic prescriptions in checked baggage, which creates immediate treatment-day instability if the bag is delayed.
Traveling with a single credit card for a five-figure installment without confirming foreign-use operability, backup coverage, and a reachable fraud-resolution contact path.
Packing for comfort or vacation optics instead of procedure execution, leaving out post-op essentials such as chargers, soft-food tools, medication timing aids, or swelling-compatible clothing.

chapters.chapter_title: Procedure-Day Identity Verification, Sedation Control, Implant/Prosthetic Specification Capture, and Discharge Handoff

worksheets.title: Procedure-Day Treatment Scope and Material Verification Record at Cavallini
worksheets.purpose: Produces the signed same-day record of what Cavallini actually performed, what changed from the pre-trip plan, and whether cost or recovery re-approval is required before the patient leaves the clinic.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 25 minutes before and immediately after treatment, plus 10 minutes if the scope changes mid-day

sections.section_title: Arrival Identity and Pre-Consent Scope Lock
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, retrieve the written preliminary treatment plan accepted before travel, the clinic appointment confirmation, your passport or matching government ID, and any pre-trip quote showing planned teeth, arches, implant count, or restorative scope. If Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness has not already produced the confirmed appointment date and escort arrangement, stop and resolve that first. Complete this section before any anxiolytic, IV sedation discussion, or consent signature so recall is not impaired. This section is complete when the identity record, planned procedure type, operative sites, and same-day branch conditions all match a written source or are flagged as a variance requiring explanation.

fields.label: Passport full name used to match Cavallini patient file and consent documents
fields.placeholder: Emily Anne Parker
fields.validation_hint: Enter the exact passport-style name used at check-in, e.g., 'Emily Anne Parker'.

fields.label: Date of birth confirmed against clinic chart on procedure day
fields.validation_hint: Use YYYY-MM-DD format matching the clinic chart and passport, e.g., '1981-09-14'.

fields.label: Cavallini procedure-day appointment date in San José
fields.validation_hint: Enter the actual Costa Rica treatment date, e.g., '2026-07-22'.

fields.label: Pre-trip written treatment pathway being matched at check-in
fields.placeholder: Implant placement with same-day provisional full-arch temporary if primary stability achieved
fields.validation_hint: Choose the closest written pathway already issued by Cavallini before travel.

fields.label: Teeth or arches listed on the accepted pre-trip plan
fields.placeholder: Maxillary full arch; planned extractions of remaining upper teeth; 4 implants for immediate-load temporary
fields.validation_hint: Specify arch or tooth numbers exactly as planned, e.g., 'Sites #3, #5, #12 crown replacement' or 'Mandibular full arch'.

fields.label: Conditional treatment branch stated before consent finalization
fields.placeholder: Same-day temporary only if primary stability is achieved on all four maxillary implants; otherwise implants placed and removable provisional used during healing.
fields.validation_hint: Capture any if/then condition exactly, including limits on same-day prosthetic delivery.

sections.section_title: Same-Day Clinical Scope Presented by Cavallini
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, ask the treating dentist or treatment coordinator to restate the day’s actual intended procedure after in-person exam and any new imaging. Use the written preliminary plan from Section 1 as the comparison baseline, and do not rely on memory or hallway conversation. If the clinic mentions extractions, grafting, more implants, fewer implants, a different arch, a different implant brand, or inability to deliver the expected prosthesis, capture it here before sedation starts. This section is complete when the actual planned procedure, same-day lab involvement, sedation pathway, and expected recovery implications are documented in terms precise enough to compare against the pre-trip plan.

fields.label: Actual procedure scope stated by Cavallini immediately before consent signing
fields.placeholder: Extract remaining upper teeth, place 4 Nobel Biocare implants in maxilla, convert immediate denture to screw-retained provisional if torque allows; otherwise healing provisional only.
fields.validation_hint: Write the full operative plan in one sentence with actions, arch, and provisional/final status.

fields.label: Same-day extractions confirmed at chairside
fields.placeholder: Upper remaining teeth #6-11, #13, #14
fields.validation_hint: List exact teeth or enter 'None planned today' if no extractions are expected.

fields.label: Implant placement count presented before treatment start
fields.validation_hint: Enter a whole number such as '4' for a full-arch case or '2' for a segmental case.

fields.label: Sedation or anesthesia pathway presented for today’s procedure
fields.placeholder: IV sedation plus local anesthesia
fields.validation_hint: Select the method actually proposed for today, not a prior assumption.

fields.label: Onsite lab fabrication or adjustment expected during this visit
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true if Cavallini’s onsite lab will fabricate, convert, adjust, or reline any prosthetic component today.

fields.label: Expected same-day restorative output before leaving the clinic or onsite hotel
fields.placeholder: Screw-retained maxillary provisional temporary delivered same day if implant stability adequate
fields.validation_hint: State exactly what the patient expects to leave with, e.g., 'No prosthesis delivered today' or 'Temporary bridge delivered'.

sections.section_title: Variance, Cost Delta, and Stay-Extension Control
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, compare the pre-trip quote and accepted treatment pathway against the revised day-of-treatment plan stated by Cavallini. You will need the written quote, any updated payment sheet presented onsite, and your current lodging and return-flight assumptions from Costa Rica Medical Travel and Surgical Trip Readiness. If the procedure changes materially, document the reason in operational terms, not just 'doctor recommendation.' This section is complete when every material variance is tied to a reason, an added cost estimate, an added-night estimate, and a clear go/no-go re-approval outcome.

fields.label: Material difference between accepted pre-trip plan and day-of-treatment plan
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true if arch, tooth count, implant count, brand, sedation method, prosthetic type, cost, or stay length changed.

fields.label: Exact clinical reason Cavallini gave for changing the plan onsite
fields.placeholder: CBCT and intraoral findings showed insufficient bone at posterior maxilla for original 4-implant distribution without grafting; doctor proposed 6 implants plus sinus graft and 3 added recovery nights.
fields.validation_hint: Record the stated reason with anatomy or findings, not a vague summary like 'better option'.

fields.label: Increase in total same-trip treatment cost quoted onsite in U.S. dollars
fields.validation_hint: Enter the added amount only, e.g., '3200', not the total case cost.

fields.label: Additional hotel nights now expected in San José because of the change
fields.validation_hint: Enter '0' if no added nights are required; otherwise enter the added count.

fields.label: Financial and logistics re-approval outcome before proceeding with revised scope
fields.placeholder: Pending re-approval — do not start procedure
options: Original plan confirmed — no re-approval needed
Pending re-approval — do not start procedure
Revised plan declined — treatment paused or limited
fields.validation_hint: Choose the actual disposition reached before consent or treatment start.

fields.label: Person who authorized proceeding after variance review
fields.placeholder: Patient and spouse escort reviewed revised quote at 10:40 AM; approved written add-on after confirming 3 extra hotel nights available
fields.validation_hint: Enter who approved and how, or state 'No approval granted; case paused'.

sections.section_title: Actual Procedure Performed Before Facility Departure
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, obtain the treating dentist’s verbal or written summary of what was actually completed and compare it to the pre-treatment plan captured earlier on this worksheet. Use operative notes, procedure summary printouts, implant stickers, lab slips, or discharge documents if available. Do not leave this section for later recollection after returning to the onsite hotel. This section is complete when the final performed scope, actual teeth or arches treated, same-day prosthetic delivery status, and any uncompleted portions are written in a way that a U.S. dentist could understand what happened.

fields.label: Actual treatment completed at Cavallini on this date
fields.placeholder: All remaining maxillary teeth extracted; 4 Nobel Biocare implants placed; bone graft at site #4 region; screw-retained immediate provisional delivered and adjusted onsite.
fields.validation_hint: State what was completed, not what was planned, with procedure actions in past tense.

fields.label: Operative sites or teeth actually treated today
fields.placeholder: Maxillary full arch; grafting in posterior maxilla; immediate provisional seated
fields.validation_hint: List sites or arch in the final completed form, e.g., 'Teeth #18 and #19 crowns seated'.

fields.label: Temporary, provisional, or final prosthesis status at discharge
fields.placeholder: Provisional delivered today
fields.validation_hint: Choose the status the patient physically left with.

fields.label: Onsite lab work actually performed during the visit
fields.placeholder: Onsite lab converted denture to provisional full-arch bridge, adjusted intaglio surface, and refined bite after insertion.
fields.validation_hint: Describe the lab’s actual role or enter 'No onsite lab involvement documented'.

fields.label: Planned treatment elements deferred to a later Costa Rica visit or U.S. follow-up
fields.placeholder: Final zirconia full-arch prosthesis deferred until osseointegration; return trip anticipated in 5 months.
fields.validation_hint: List anything not completed today and the expected next phase.

fields.label: Name of Cavallini clinician who verbally confirmed final procedure summary
fields.placeholder: Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini
fields.validation_hint: Enter the confirming provider’s full name if stated, not just 'the dentist'.

sections.section_title: Export Readiness for Recovery and U.S. Follow-Up
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the completed sections of this worksheet, any preliminary discharge papers, and your phone camera or scanning method for record duplication. This section depends on the actual procedure summary already being entered accurately above. Verify that the outputs needed for Costa Rica Recovery Monitoring and Return-to-Travel Clearance and U.S. Follow-Up Continuity are present before leaving the clinic area. This section is complete when next-step timing, urgent contact route, and record duplication status are all confirmed.

fields.label: Next in-Costa Rica follow-up appointment date or recheck timing given before discharge
fields.placeholder: Return to Cavallini tomorrow at 9:00 AM for bite check and tissue review
fields.validation_hint: Enter exact date/time if given, or a precise instruction such as 'clinic will call by 5 PM today'.

fields.label: Urgent post-op contact route given for after-hours swelling, bleeding, or severe pain
fields.placeholder: WhatsApp urgent line +506 8888-1234; send photo and state patient name plus hotel room number
fields.validation_hint: Enter the direct urgent route, not the public website or general reception line.

fields.label: Procedure-day records duplicated before leaving clinic area
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if photos/scans of the worksheet and received clinical documents have been made.

fields.label: U.S.-side dentist or file destination for today’s procedure record
fields.placeholder: Dr. Lauren Chen DDS, Austin TX — records to be forwarded after return for provisional surveillance
fields.validation_hint: Identify the U.S. recipient or enter 'REQUIRES CLARIFICATION' if no follow-up provider is secured yet.

fields.label: Procedure-day operational status before leaving Cavallini facility
fields.placeholder: Complete and ready for recovery handoff
options: Complete and ready for recovery handoff
Incomplete — missing treatment or discharge details
Escalated — waiting for revised documents or records
fields.validation_hint: Choose 'Incomplete' if any procedure summary, contact route, or change documentation is still missing.

fields.label: Outstanding information that still must be produced by Cavallini within 24 hours
fields.placeholder: Awaiting written implant component sheet and emailed warranty terms for provisional maxillary bridge by tomorrow 3:00 PM.
fields.validation_hint: Enter 'None' only if all expected records are already in hand.

decision_gates.condition: If the day-of-treatment plan differs materially from the written preliminary plan in arch, tooth count, extraction count, implant count, implant brand, prosthetic type, sedation method, cost, or expected recovery duration.
decision_gates.pass_action: Document the variance in ws-05-a, obtain explanation and revised implications, and proceed only after explicit approval is recorded.
decision_gates.fail_action: Pause consent, sedation, and procedure start until the variance is explained and documented; if unresolved, limit treatment to the documented pre-consented scope or reschedule.

decision_gates.condition: If the same-day change increases total case cost by more than 10% of the accepted quote or extends the stay by more than 2 nights.
decision_gates.pass_action: Record revised financial and lodging approval, then update downstream recovery and cost-control worksheets.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not authorize expanded treatment until funding, hotel nights, flights, and companion availability are re-approved.

cross_references: Travel Readiness Checklist
Deposit and Payment Calendar
All-in Costa Rica treatment budget
Procedure-Day Treatment Scope and Material Verification Record at Cavallini
common_errors: Recording the originally quoted treatment plan instead of the procedure actually performed after in-person exam, extractions, or imaging review at Cavallini.

Failing to document the exact reason for a same-day change such as inadequate bone quality, additional decay, or inability to deliver a same-day temporary, which later breaks warranty and follow-up continuity.
Leaving cost delta and added hotel-night impact blank when the clinic expands from the accepted scope, causing downstream recovery and budget worksheets to become inaccurate.

worksheets.title: IV Sedation Consent, Escort Presence, and Discharge Readiness Checklist
worksheets.purpose: Verifies that sedation-day consent, fasting, escort, transport, instruction transfer, and release conditions are satisfied before and after IV sedation or other impairment-producing treatment at Cavallini.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 15 minutes pre-procedure and 10 minutes at discharge
checklist_items: Verify that the patient’s full legal name and date of birth on the IV sedation consent match the passport and Cavallini chart — source: passport, wristband/chart label, and printed consent — pass = all identifiers match exactly before signature; fail = stop signing and require chart correction from admissions or the treatment coordinator before any sedative is administered.

Verify that the procedure listed on the sedation consent matches the same-day scope captured in ws-05-a, including arch, extraction count, implant count, and whether same-day provisional delivery is conditional — source: ws-05-a and consent packet — pass = scope language aligns or documented addendum exists; fail = pause consent and escalate to the treating dentist for rewritten consent.

Verify that the sedation pathway actually planned today is stated in writing as IV sedation, oral anxiolysis, or local anesthesia only — source: consent form and dentist/treatment coordinator confirmation — pass = written pathway matches clinical plan; fail = require corrected paperwork before pre-medication.

Verify that all medication allergies and prior adverse reactions to anesthesia or sedation are documented on the clinic intake and were verbally reconfirmed today — source: medical history form and verbal confirmation with staff — pass = allergies/reactions are listed or explicitly marked none; fail = notify anesthesia-capable provider before proceeding.

Verify that current prescription medications, blood thinners, diabetes medications, sleep medications, and supplements taken within the last 72 hours were disclosed to the treating team — source: patient medication list and day-of verbal review — pass = staff confirms updated list in chart; fail = do not sedate until medication reconciliation is completed.

Verify that last food and last clear-liquid times meet the fasting instructions given by Cavallini for today’s sedation plan — source: patient and escort statement plus clinic fasting instructions — pass = timing satisfies clinic rule or written exception is approved; fail = escalate to sedation provider for go/no-go decision.

Verify that the patient has not taken unapproved alcohol, cannabis, recreational drugs, or non-cleared sedatives before arrival — source: direct patient/escort disclosure and pre-op instructions — pass = none taken or cleared in writing by provider; fail = notify sedation provider immediately and pause the case.

Verify that the patient removed contact lenses, removable dentures, oral jewelry, and other items the clinic required to be removed for sedation or surgery — source: pre-op checklist and direct visual confirmation — pass = all required items removed and secured; fail = resolve before transport to procedure room.

Verify that the escort is physically present in the clinic or designated waiting area before sedation starts — source: direct visual confirmation by staff and worksheet entry — pass = escort is onsite and acknowledged; fail = escalate before sedation administration and document resolution.

Verify that the escort’s full name, mobile number that functions in Costa Rica, and relationship to the patient are documented — source: escort phone screen and verbal confirmation — pass = a reachable direct number is recorded; fail = obtain correct contact or identify alternate escort before procedure.

Verify that the escort understands they must remain reachable for the full procedure window and cannot board a flight, leave the area, or silence the phone — source: direct instruction from clinic staff and escort acknowledgment — pass = escort verbally confirms availability; fail = designate another escort or delay sedation per clinic policy.

Verify that the discharge destination after sedation is a safe lodging location such as the Cavallini onsite hotel or equivalent nearby hotel already booked — source: lodging confirmation and escort statement — pass = exact property and room plan are known; fail = resolve lodging before sedation starts.

Verify that the transport route from clinic to lodging after sedation is assigned and immediately available — source: escort plan, driver booking, or clinic transport arrangement — pass = named driver/escort and route are documented; fail = arrange transport before sedation.

Verify that no self-driving, public bus transfer, or solo rideshare by the sedated patient is planned after discharge — source: escort transport plan — pass = escorted private route confirmed; fail = clinic discharge must be held until safe transport is arranged.

Verify that the first-night supervision plan covers the entire first post-sedation night, especially for IV sedation, extractions with implants, or full-arch work — source: escort statement and lodging plan — pass = a named adult will remain with or monitor the patient as instructed; fail = escalate to clinic before sedation or release.

Verify that decision authority boundaries are clear if final treatment choices could arise after sedation or extraction findings — source: consent conversation and ws-05-a branch conditions — pass = only pre-consented scope may proceed or a documented authority path exists; fail = restrict treatment to pre-consented scope.

Verify that the escort understands the minimum duties: receive discharge instructions directly, confirm medications in hand, escort patient to lodging, and supervise first-dose timing — source: verbal handoff from clinic staff — pass = escort repeats responsibilities accurately; fail = re-instruct before procedure continues.

Verify that written English instructions are requested if the escort is not comfortable receiving complex discharge instructions in Spanish — source: escort language preference discussion — pass = suitable written language copy will be issued; fail = do not accept verbal-only discharge to a language-mismatched escort.

Verify that the patient’s baseline pain, nausea, bleeding risk, and anxiety status before sedation are noted for comparison after discharge — source: nursing intake or verbal pre-op check — pass = baseline status documented; fail = request notation before sedative administration.

Verify that the clinic confirms whether any first doses of antibiotic, analgesic, anti-inflammatory, or rinse will be given onsite — source: nurse, dentist, or pharmacy handoff — pass = each medication is marked given onsite or first dose due later; fail = do not leave this ambiguous at discharge.

Verify that the escort receives the urgent after-hours contact route directly from clinic staff, not secondhand from the sedated patient — source: discharge conversation and written contact card — pass = escort can state the number/WhatsApp route back; fail = re-issue instructions before release.

Verify that the escort is told the specific symptom thresholds for urgent contact, including repeated gauze saturation, worsening swelling, fever, uncontrolled pain, or inability to drink — source: written post-op instructions and verbal briefing — pass = thresholds are written and explained; fail = discharge is incomplete until clarified.

Verify that the patient can maintain airway, follow simple commands, and transfer safely with assistance at the time of discharge — source: discharge nurse/provider assessment — pass = clinic states patient meets release criteria; fail = continue recovery observation onsite.

Verify that nausea, dizziness, or excessive somnolence at discharge were specifically assessed after IV sedation — source: discharge assessment and escort observation — pass = symptoms are absent or controlled with instructions; fail = prolong observation and update release timing.

Verify that medications, written instructions, and any procedure summary physically leave the clinic with the escort, not in a separate admin queue or later promise unless documented — source: visual check of packet and pharmacy bag — pass = packet is in hand or missing-item deadline documented; fail = do not leave clinic area.

Verify that the exact time the patient left the chair area, the time transport departed, and the lodging arrival expectation were recorded — source: discharge desk or escort notation — pass = times are documented for first-dose planning; fail = record immediately before departure.

Verify that the escort confirms the first 24-hour medication grid in ws-05-d can actually be executed despite sedation, sleep, travel time, or nausea — source: ws-05-d review with escort — pass = dosing plan is feasible and understood; fail = call clinic for revised schedule before leaving.

Verify that no discharge occurs until the escort, not the sedated patient alone, can explain the immediate restrictions on eating, rinsing, smoking, alcohol, and physical exertion — source: verbal teach-back and written instructions — pass = escort accurately restates restrictions; fail = repeat instruction and hold discharge.

Verify that any failure in escort presence, transport safety, or comprehension was escalated to a named Cavallini staff member and resolved in writing — source: discharge notes and worksheet entry — pass = resolution documented; fail = sedation-day control remains open and chapter completion is blocked.

decision_gates.gate_title: Escort Presence at Sedation Discharge Gate
decision_gates.condition: If IV sedation is scheduled and no escort is physically present and reachable at discharge.
decision_gates.pass_action: Proceed with the planned sedation pathway and release only when escort duties, transport, and first-night supervision are confirmed.
decision_gates.fail_action: Escalate before procedure or before sedation administration depending on clinic policy; document companion arrival, approved sedation-plan change, or rescheduling.

decision_gates.gate_title: Instruction Transfer Gate for Impaired Patient
decision_gates.condition: If key post-op instructions were given only verbally or only to the sedated patient.
decision_gates.pass_action: Re-issue instructions directly to the escort in written form and confirm teach-back before release.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not finalize discharge until escort receives understandable written instructions and urgent thresholds.

cross_references: Procedure-Day Treatment Scope and Material Verification Record at Cavallini
Onsite Pharmacy Medication and Post-Op Instruction Logging Sheet
Cavallini Discharge Packet Receipt and Missing-Document Resolution Form
common_errors: Assuming a companion who is waiting offsite or not answering their phone counts as an acceptable sedation escort at discharge.
Letting the sedated patient receive verbal instructions without requiring the escort to hear and confirm the same instructions in English or another language they understand.
Failing to verify whether the first antibiotic or pain dose was already given onsite, which leads to missed early dosing in the Cavallini hotel or nearby lodging.
example_items: Verified that escort Ana Morales was physically present in the Cavallini waiting area at 8:15 AM and remained reachable at Costa Rica mobile +506 8777-4411 — source checked: direct visual confirmation and phone call test — pass: phone rang in clinic and escort answered; no escalation required.

Verified that last food was 11:30 PM and last clear liquid was 4:45 AM for a 9:00 AM IV sedation start per clinic instructions — source checked: patient/escort statement and written fasting instructions — pass: sedation nurse confirmed acceptable timing and documented clearance.

Verified that first amoxicillin-clavulanate dose was not given onsite and is due at 6:00 PM with food after arrival at the Cavallini hotel — source checked: pharmacy label, nurse discharge review, and ws-05-d medication grid — pass: escort repeated dose time correctly and set phone alarm.

worksheets.title: Implant System, Abutment, and Prosthetic Specification Capture Form
worksheets.purpose: Captures the exact implant, abutment, grafting, and prosthetic identifiers placed or delivered at Cavallini so later U.S. adjustment, repair, warranty review, or emergency intervention is possible.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 20 minutes at discharge, plus 5 minutes per added implant or prosthetic component

Fixture / Prosthetic Category Actually Delivered (Implant Fixture / Multi-Unit Abutment / Crown / Full-Arch Provisional / Final Prosthesis)

Platform, Diameter, Length, or Restorative Material Exactly as Provided
Connection / Retention / Loading Status Documented at Discharge
Source of This Specification (Sticker / Lab Slip / Procedure Summary / Verbal Only)

decision_gates.gate_title: Missing Implant or Prosthetic Identifier Gate
decision_gates.condition: If any implant or prosthetic brand, component, or restorative designation is missing at discharge.
decision_gates.pass_action: Request completion before leaving the facility and attach available stickers, lab slips, or summaries to ws-05-c.
decision_gates.fail_action: Obtain a named staff contact and written commitment for delivery by email or print within 24 hours; flag U.S. follow-up readiness as at-risk.

decision_gates.gate_title: Provisional vs Final Status Clarity Gate
decision_gates.condition: If the patient cannot tell whether the delivered crown, bridge, or full-arch prosthesis is provisional or final.
decision_gates.pass_action: Record the exact delivered status and any next-stage conversion plan in ws-05-c and ws-05-a.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not close specification capture until Cavallini clarifies delivered status in writing.

cross_references: Procedure-Day Treatment Scope and Material Verification Record at Cavallini
Cavallini Discharge Packet Receipt and Missing-Document Resolution Form
common_errors: Writing 'Nobel' or 'Zimmer' without site-specific component details, leaving U.S. providers unable to determine compatibility.
Failing to distinguish provisional conversion work from a final prosthesis, which creates warranty confusion and inappropriate expectations for U.S. follow-up.
Omitting whether the component information came from implant stickers, a procedure summary, or verbal staff confirmation, making later reconciliation harder when records conflict.

worksheets.title: Onsite Pharmacy Medication and Post-Op Instruction Logging Sheet
worksheets.purpose: Creates the patient-and-escort medication execution log by converting Cavallini discharge instructions into exact first-24-hour actions and urgent symptom thresholds.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 20 minutes at discharge and 10 minutes for first-night updates
worksheets.right_column_label: Patient/Escort Execution Plan for the First 24 Hours in San José

sections.section_title: Medication Reconciliation Between Cavallini Discharge Instructions and Pharmacy Labels
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the printed prescriptions or medication list, the actual onsite pharmacy labels, and any nurse or dentist discharge sheet. This section should only be completed after ws-05-b has confirmed whether any first doses were already administered onsite. Compare each medication name, strength, and intended purpose line by line instead of relying on memory. This section is complete when every medication in hand has a matched instruction source and any mismatch has been escalated before leaving the clinic area.

fields.label: Left-column entry: Antibiotic exactly as written on Cavallini prescription or pharmacy label
fields.placeholder: Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875 mg/125 mg tablet
fields.validation_hint: Enter the full medication and strength exactly, e.g., 'Amoxicillin-clavulanate 875 mg/125 mg tablet'.

fields.label: Right-column execution: First antibiotic dose status after today’s procedure
fields.placeholder: Not given onsite; first dose due at 6:00 PM with soft food at Cavallini hotel
fields.validation_hint: State whether given onsite or the exact due time and location for the first dose.

fields.label: Left-column entry: Primary pain medication exactly as dispensed by onsite pharmacy
fields.placeholder: Ibuprofen 600 mg tablet
fields.validation_hint: Use the pharmacy label wording and strength, not 'pain pill'.

fields.label: Right-column execution: Pain-medication interval converted into clock times for tonight and overnight
fields.placeholder: Take at 4:00 PM, 10:00 PM, and 4:00 AM with food if pain reaches clinic-defined threshold; escort will set alarms.
fields.validation_hint: Convert the interval into exact times for the first 24 hours, including overnight if required.

sections.section_title: Food, Oral Hygiene, and Activity Restrictions After Implant, Extraction, Crown, or Full-Arch Work
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the written post-op instructions from Cavallini and ask staff to clarify any verbal-only restrictions involving rinsing, spitting, brushing, smoking, alcohol, prosthesis removal, or chewing. Use the actual procedure performed in ws-05-a, because restrictions differ if the patient had implants, grafting, full-arch temporaries, or a simpler crown visit. Do not summarize broadly; write what the patient may do and when. This section is complete when the first-night eating, cleaning, and activity rules are specific enough that the escort could enforce them without asking the clinic again.

fields.label: Left-column entry: Eating and drinking restriction stated by Cavallini for the first 12 to 24 hours
fields.placeholder: Soft, cool foods only for first evening; no straws, no alcohol, avoid hot foods until numbness resolved.
fields.validation_hint: Capture the exact food/liquid limits, not 'eat carefully'.

fields.label: Right-column execution: First post-op meal plan at San José lodging
fields.placeholder: Protein yogurt at 5:30 PM, room-temperature soup at 8:00 PM, water bottle kept at bedside; escort to monitor intake because IV sedation used.
fields.validation_hint: List real foods, timing, and supervision steps for the first evening.

fields.label: Left-column entry: Oral hygiene and rinsing restriction given after today’s procedure
fields.placeholder: Do not rinse vigorously today; begin gentle chlorhexidine rinse tomorrow morning; brush non-surgical teeth tonight only.
fields.validation_hint: State exact start/stop instructions and protected areas.

fields.label: Right-column execution: Bathroom-counter setup and hygiene timing for first night
fields.placeholder: Escort will place chlorhexidine, gauze, and soft toothbrush in hotel bathroom; no rinsing tonight; gentle non-surgical brushing at 9:00 PM.
fields.validation_hint: Translate the instruction into a physical setup and time-based action.

sections.section_title: Urgent Symptom Thresholds and Contact Activation Rules
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the written symptom thresholds, urgent contact card, and any verbal instructions provided by the dentist, nurse, or pharmacy handoff. This section depends on the urgent contact route already being captured in ws-05-a or ws-05-e. Record thresholds in observable terms such as repeated gauze saturation, fever, worsening swelling, uncontrolled pain, or inability to maintain oral intake. This section is complete when the patient and escort know exactly what symptom triggers a message or call and what information must be sent to the clinic.

fields.label: Left-column entry: Bleeding, swelling, fever, or pain threshold that requires urgent contact with Cavallini
fields.placeholder: Call or WhatsApp if gauze saturates repeatedly beyond expected threshold, swelling worsens after first day, fever develops, or pain stays severe after scheduled medication window.
fields.validation_hint: Write the trigger exactly as instructed by clinic staff.

fields.label: Right-column execution: What the escort will send or report if an urgent symptom occurs
fields.placeholder: Send patient full name, today’s procedure, hotel name and room, symptom start time, medications already taken, and requested photos through WhatsApp urgent line.
fields.validation_hint: List the actual data package to send so contact is not delayed during an overnight issue.

fields.label: Left-column entry: After-hours contact route provided by Cavallini for tonight
fields.placeholder: WhatsApp +506 8888-1234 for urgent post-op issues after clinic closes
fields.validation_hint: Enter the exact direct route, not the public website or general reception number.

fields.label: Right-column execution: Escort acknowledgement that urgent-contact route has been tested or saved correctly
fields.placeholder: Saved to Ana's phone under 'Cavallini Urgent'; test message draft prepared but not sent
fields.validation_hint: State how the number was saved, tested, or made immediately usable.

sections.section_title: First-Night Dosing and Recovery Feasibility Check
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, use the medication names and restrictions already entered above, then compare them against transport time to lodging, sedation level, nausea risk, and sleep expectations. This section should be completed with the escort present if sedation or major surgery occurred. Convert every critical medication into realistic clock times and note any interval that seems hard to execute overnight. This section is complete when the first 24-hour plan is feasible without guessing and any unworkable schedule has been clarified with Cavallini before departure.

fields.label: Left-column entry: Medication or recovery instruction most likely to be missed overnight
fields.placeholder: Antibiotic every 8 hours beginning 6:00 PM
fields.validation_hint: Identify the specific instruction posing execution risk, not a general concern.

fields.label: Right-column execution: Corrected overnight plan approved by patient, escort, or Cavallini staff
fields.placeholder: Escort will wake patient for 2:00 AM antibiotic dose with crackers and water; if nausea prevents dosing, WhatsApp clinic for restart instructions.
fields.validation_hint: Describe the actual plan to execute or the fallback escalation path.

fields.label: Right-column completion status: First-24-hour medication and restriction plan is executable at current lodging
fields.placeholder: Yes — executable as written
options: No — discharge timing or plan must be revised
fields.validation_hint: Choose 'Needs clinic clarification' if any dose timing, food rule, or symptom threshold remains uncertain.

decision_gates.condition: If the clinic cannot provide a written record of what was prescribed, dispensed, or changed that day.
decision_gates.pass_action: Complete ws-05-d using the written sources and verify label-to-instruction consistency before transport.
decision_gates.fail_action: Classify discharge as incomplete and remain onsite until documents are produced or a documented delivery deadline within 24 hours is assigned.

decision_gates.condition: If the medication interval schedule cannot realistically be executed because of travel time, sleep, nausea, or patient impairment.
decision_gates.pass_action: Record the executable clock schedule and proceed to lodging with alarms and escort plan in place.
decision_gates.fail_action: Request Cavallini clarification on how to restart or adjust dosing, then update ws-05-d before departure.

cross_references: IV Sedation Consent, Escort Presence, and Discharge Readiness Checklist
Procedure-Day Treatment Scope and Material Verification Record at Cavallini
Cavallini Discharge Packet Receipt and Missing-Document Resolution Form
common_errors: Copying the pharmacy bag label without reconciling whether the first dose was already given in the clinic chair area or recovery area.
Recording 'as needed' pain instructions without a defined severity threshold or maximum interval, which causes under-treatment or unsafe overuse in the hotel.

Failing to translate hygiene and food restrictions into clock-based actions for the first evening after sedation, especially when the patient is sleepy, nauseated, or traveling between clinic and lodging.

worksheets.title: Cavallini Discharge Packet Receipt and Missing-Document Resolution Form
worksheets.purpose: Confirms that the patient physically received the records, instructions, contacts, and follow-up commitments required to close the Cavallini visit and hand off safely to recovery and U.S. follow-up.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 15 minutes at discharge desk and 10 minutes if any record is missing

sections.section_title: Required Discharge Packet Receipt Audit
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather every paper, printout, receipt, medication sheet, implant sticker page, appointment card, and digital file received from Cavallini on procedure day. This section depends on ws-05-a and ws-05-d already identifying the actual procedure performed and the medications prescribed. Check each expected document physically rather than assuming it is somewhere in the packet. This section is complete when each required item is marked received, missing, or promised with a named delivery path.

fields.label: Written post-operative instruction sheet physically received before leaving clinic
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if a written instruction sheet is actually in hand or saved digitally before departure.

fields.label: Procedure summary describing what Cavallini actually performed today
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if the completed procedure is documented in writing, not just explained verbally.

fields.label: Implant/prosthetic specification record, stickers, or lab documentation received if applicable
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if site/component information or source documents were physically received or emailed while onsite.

fields.label: Medication list or prescriptions received with dosing instructions
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if medication names and dosing instructions are in writing.

fields.label: Urgent after-hours contact route card, number, or WhatsApp instruction received
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if a direct usable route was provided in writing or saved digitally.

fields.label: Next in-country follow-up appointment timing or review plan documented
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if date/time or a documented callback plan is present.

sections.section_title: Missing-Document Escalation and 24-Hour Resolution Control
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review any missing items from the receipt audit above and identify whether they are absent because of clinic delay, lab delay, or provider completion delay. Use the discharge desk, treatment coordinator, or named clinical staff member as the source for promised delivery details. Do not accept 'we will send it later' without a direct owner and timeframe. This section is complete when every missing item has a named responsible person, delivery channel, and deadline within 24 hours or a written explanation for why that is impossible.

fields.label: Exact discharge item still missing at time patient is ready to leave Cavallini
fields.placeholder: Implant sticker sheet and written confirmation whether delivered maxillary bridge is provisional conversion or final prosthesis
fields.validation_hint: List every missing document specifically; enter 'None' only if packet is complete.

fields.label: Named Cavallini staff member responsible for sending missing item
fields.placeholder: Laura Gómez, international patient coordinator
fields.validation_hint: Enter a real person’s name and role, not 'front desk'.

fields.label: Promised delivery channel for missing discharge item
fields.placeholder: Email PDF to patient and escort
options: Email PDF to patient and escort
fields.validation_hint: Choose the specific agreed channel for delivery.

fields.label: Promised deadline for missing-item delivery
fields.placeholder: By 3:00 PM tomorrow, 2026-07-23
fields.validation_hint: Enter a specific date and time, not 'tomorrow' or 'soon'.

fields.label: Escalation route if the missing item is not received by deadline
fields.placeholder: Follow up with international coordinator first, then treating dentist office line and urgent WhatsApp if travel departure is within 12 hours
fields.validation_hint: State a concrete escalation sequence with contacts or roles.

sections.section_title: Warranty and Delivered-Status Clarification Capture
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, ask whether any warranty language discussed today applies to the actual work delivered, especially if a temporary, provisional, conversion prosthesis, or staged implant case was involved. Review any written warranty summary already obtained during due diligence and compare it against the procedure actually performed in ws-05-a and specifications captured in ws-05-c. Do not assume that a lifetime implant warranty covers provisional prosthetics, bite changes, fractures, or return-travel costs. This section is complete when the patient knows whether today’s delivered work is provisional or final and what treatment-specific warranty conditions were stated.

fields.label: Delivered restoration status clarified at discharge
fields.placeholder: Provisional temporary only
options: Mixed case — some provisional, some final
fields.validation_hint: Choose the actual status of what the patient left with today.

fields.label: Treatment-specific warranty conditions stated for the work completed today
fields.placeholder: Implant fixtures covered per clinic program, but maxillary conversion provisional not treated as final prosthesis; maintenance compliance and return evaluation required for warranty review.
fields.validation_hint: Write the conditions tied to today's delivered work, not general website warranty language.

fields.label: Return-to-Costa-Rica obligation or exclusion mentioned for warranty claims
fields.placeholder: Any definitive remake or implant-related warranty review requires in-person evaluation at Cavallini; travel costs not stated as covered.
fields.validation_hint: Enter exact travel or exclusion language, or 'REQUIRES CLARIFICATION' if not provided.

sections.section_title: Duplication, Export, and Chapter Closure Confirmation
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, photograph or scan the entire discharge packet, completed worksheets, medication labels, and any implant stickers or lab documents. This section should be completed before leaving the clinic area or immediately on arrival at the onsite hotel if the clinic is still reachable for same-day correction. Verify that the recovery handoff and U.S. follow-up handoff each have usable source documents. This section is complete when backup copies exist, the packet has a destination for U.S. transfer, and chapter closure status can be truthfully marked complete or incomplete.

fields.label: Discharge packet duplicated by photo, scan, or PDF before departure from clinic area
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if backup copies of key records were created the same day.

fields.label: Location where duplicate packet is stored for access during Costa Rica recovery and after U.S. return
fields.placeholder: iPhone Files folder 'Cavallini July 2026' plus emailed PDF copy to spouse and Dropbox medical folder
fields.validation_hint: Name the actual storage locations, not just 'phone'.

fields.label: U.S. recipient or future provider designated to receive discharge packet after return home
fields.placeholder: Dr. Lauren Chen DDS secure email via office records coordinator after first post-return visit
fields.validation_hint: Identify the U.S. recipient or enter 'REQUIRES CLARIFICATION' if not yet established.

fields.label: Chapter-5 discharge handoff status at the moment patient leaves Cavallini
fields.placeholder: Operationally complete
options: At-risk — no clear U.S. handoff destination
fields.validation_hint: Choose 'Incomplete' if any required packet element, urgent contact route, or missing-item deadline is unresolved.

decision_gates.condition: If the clinic cannot provide a written record of what was placed, prescribed, or changed that day.
decision_gates.pass_action: Mark the packet complete, duplicate it, and release the case to recovery and U.S. handoff workflows.
decision_gates.fail_action: Classify discharge as incomplete and remain onsite until the documents are produced or a documented follow-up delivery deadline is assigned within 24 hours.

decision_gates.gate_title: Urgent Contact and U.S. Handoff Readiness Gate
decision_gates.condition: If the patient cannot identify a functioning urgent contact route or cannot identify who will receive U.S.-side records after return.
decision_gates.pass_action: Close Chapter 5 and hand off to recovery and long-term follow-up control.
decision_gates.fail_action: Flag the next domain as at-risk and treat the unresolved item as a priority task within 24 hours.

common_errors: Leaving with only verbal instructions because the front desk promises to email documents later, but without naming who will send them or by when.
Failing to request treatment-specific warranty conditions when a provisional prosthesis was delivered, leading the patient to assume lifetime coverage applies immediately and fully.
Not duplicating the discharge packet before leaving the clinic or onsite hotel, which creates a single point of failure if papers are lost during return travel to the U.S.

chapters.chapter_title: Post-Operative Symptom Surveillance, Medication Adherence, Emergency Escalation, and Return-Flight Clearance in Costa Rica

worksheets.title: Costa Rica Dental Recovery Daily Symptom and Medication Tracking Log
worksheets.purpose: Produces the day-by-day recovery trend record needed to detect deterioration, document actual medication adherence, and support flight-clearance and U.S. handoff decisions after Cavallini restorative treatment.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 25 minutes on first use, 5-8 minutes per monitoring interval
table_columns: Recovery Check-In Date and Local San José Time
Post-Op Day Number and Monitoring Interval
Exact Cavallini Procedure Being Recovered From
Pain Score 0-10 and Response After Most Recent Medication
Swelling Location / Symmetry / Effect on Swallowing or Mouth Opening
Bleeding Status Since Last Check (oozing / pink saliva / active gauze soaking)
Temperature °F, Oral Intake, Hydration, and Mobility Status
Medication Dose Actually Taken or Missed Since Last Check-In
Escalation Lane Selected From ws-06-b and Action Taken

decision_gates.condition: Discharge instructions, medication list, urgent contact route, and procedure details from Cavallini are present before the first recovery entry is made.
decision_gates.pass_action: Begin scheduled symptom and medication logging immediately on arrival at hotel or recovery lodging.
decision_gates.fail_action: Stop recovery logging setup and obtain missing packet items from Cavallini before treating the stay as actively monitored.

decision_gates.condition: Any major variable worsens for two consecutive monitoring intervals, or pain remains 7/10 or higher for more than two consecutive intervals despite medication taken as directed.
decision_gates.pass_action: Move the issue from observation into the contact lane and update ws-06-b and ws-06-c with the escalation taken.
decision_gates.fail_action: Continue the scheduled monitoring cadence only if symptoms are stable or improving.

cross_references: Post-Implant and Full-Mouth Surgery Red-Flag Escalation Checklist for San José Stay
Clinic Contact, Local Emergency Option, and Escalation Routing Form for Post-Op Issues

common_errors: Recording only one pain score per day instead of morning/mid-day/evening/bedtime intervals during the first 48 hours after implants, extractions, or All-on-X treatment, which hides worsening trends before the return flight.
Copying the prescribed medication schedule from the Cavallini discharge sheet instead of logging the doses actually taken, delayed, vomited, or skipped, making symptom interpretation unreliable.
Writing 'swelling normal' without noting side, spread, and functional effect; unilateral expansion or swallowing difficulty can require same-day reassessment in San José.

worksheets.title: Post-Implant and Full-Mouth Surgery Red-Flag Escalation Checklist for San José Stay
worksheets.purpose: Produces a documented escalation decision showing whether symptoms remain in self-monitoring, require same-day clinic contact, need urgent reassessment, or justify emergency intervention before any flight proceeds.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 20 minutes on first setup, 3-5 minutes per symptom event review
checklist_items: Verify that the exact Cavallini discharge instructions for bleeding, swelling, rinsing, diet stage, sleeping position, and activity limits are physically available at the recovery lodging by checking the paper packet, clinic email, or WhatsApp attachment — pass = the instructions can be read without contacting the clinic again; fail = request the missing instruction set from Cavallini before relying on self-monitoring.

Verify that ws-06-a contains the procedure performed, the local time anesthesia or IV sedation ended, the first pain score, and the first oral intake by reviewing the first row of the recovery log — pass = all four baseline items are recorded; fail = reconstruct the baseline immediately with the patient, escort, and discharge paperwork before symptoms are interpreted.

Verify that every prescribed medication from Cavallini is identified by name, strength, dose interval, and start timing using the onsite pharmacy label or written prescription — pass = each medication can be matched to an actual package or written order; fail = contact the clinic or pharmacy before the next dose window.

Verify that current pain has been compared across at least two monitoring intervals in ws-06-a rather than judged from a single moment — pass = trend direction is visible; fail = continue logging until trend data exist unless another emergency threshold is already met.

Verify whether pain remains uncontrolled at 7/10 or higher for more than two consecutive monitoring intervals despite prescribed medication being taken as directed by checking ws-06-a dose entries and pain scores — pass = no such pattern exists or it has already been escalated; fail = contact Cavallini the same day for reassessment instructions and suspend travel-preparation tasks.

Verify whether active bleeding continues beyond the clinic's expected window after pressure measures by checking gauze-change frequency, pooling, and restart episodes in ws-06-a against the discharge instructions — pass = bleeding is light and decreasing; fail = seek immediate clinic guidance and do not continue routine departure planning.

Verify whether swelling is stabilizing or declining rather than rapidly extending, becoming one-sided, or interfering with swallowing by comparing check-in notes and companion observations — pass = swelling follows expected early-peak behavior; fail = move to same-day clinic contact or urgent reassessment based on functional impact.

Verify whether fever has reached 100.4 F or higher after the initial recovery window, especially when paired with increasing swelling, foul taste, chills, or malaise, by checking actual thermometer readings and symptom notes — pass = temperature remains below threshold or clearly improving; fail = treat as possible infection pathway and request urgent reassessment before any flight segment proceeds.

Verify whether the patient can drink adequate fluids and keep medications down for at least 12 hours after expected sedation recovery using the medication log, oral intake notes, and nausea/vomiting entries — pass = hydration and medication continuity are intact; fail = contact Cavallini because recovery control is broken and flight readiness will likely fail.

Verify whether any antibiotic, analgesic, anti-inflammatory, or mouth rinse was skipped, delayed by more than 2 hours, or vomited soon after dosing by reviewing ws-06-a medication events — pass = adherence is intact or deviation has been reviewed by the clinic; fail = document the event clearly and contact Cavallini if symptoms are worsening or repeated doses are affected.

Verify whether the patient can ambulate safely to the bathroom and within the hotel without instability by direct observation from the escort or by a self-check if no sedation was used — pass = mobility is safe for basic recovery needs; fail = treat return-flight clearance as not met and consider urgent reassessment if decline is new.

Verify whether IV sedation was used and, if so, whether an escort completed the first-night viability check for swallowing medication, confusion level, overnight dosing, and bathroom safety by reviewing escort notes and ws-06-a — pass = escort verification exists; fail = document the gap and increase direct monitoring immediately.

Verify whether oral intake matches the diet stage specified by Cavallini rather than patient experimentation by checking the discharge sheet and meal notes — pass = intake follows the permitted stage; fail = stop unauthorized foods, document the deviation, and reassess symptoms for behavior-linked worsening.

Verify whether the patient used a straw, smoked, drank alcohol, rinsed vigorously too early, or tested solid food against instructions by asking directly and reviewing notes — pass = no prohibited behavior occurred or it was reported promptly; fail = document the deviation and contact Cavallini if pain, bleeding, or prosthetic symptoms worsened afterward.

Verify whether swallowing water causes severe pain, choking concern, or functional obstruction by testing only within clinic instructions and recording the result — pass = swallowing is possible with controlled discomfort; fail = move out of self-monitoring and seek urgent guidance.

Verify whether mouth opening is narrowing compared with the previous check-in, especially when paired with unilateral swelling, by comparing ws-06-a entries and direct observation — pass = opening is stable or improving; fail = same-day clinic contact or urgent reassessment depending on severity.

Verify whether the provisional full-arch, temporary bridge, or fresh crown work has developed a sudden high spot, clicking, shifting sensation, or localized pain on closure by patient report and gentle instructed function only — pass = no mechanical warning sign is present; fail = stop testing the prosthesis and request evaluation before airport day.

Verify whether the patient is waking due to escalating pain or requiring rescue pain medication at intervals shorter than prescribed by reviewing overnight log entries and pill timing — pass = medication spacing matches the prescription and sleep disruption is limited; fail = request clinic input before self-clearing for travel.

Verify whether bleeding, pain, swelling, hydration, urination, medication completion, overnight contact ability, and bathroom mobility were all checked before sleep on post-op day 0 and day 1 using ws-06-a and escort notes — pass = all viability items were reviewed; fail = perform the viability check immediately and escalate any deficit.

Verify whether the clinic contact route for after-hours issues is known and reachable from the hotel by checking ws-06-c phone numbers, messaging apps, and front-desk assistance options — pass = at least one active overnight route is confirmed; fail = obtain the contact path before the next overnight interval.

Verify whether the patient still appears to be improving overall rather than deteriorating after an earlier brief improvement by comparing the last three logged intervals — pass = trend remains stable or improving; fail = treat delayed deterioration as operationally significant and contact Cavallini.

Verify whether any red-flag cluster is present: fever plus increasing swelling plus worsening pain, or uncontrolled nausea plus missed medications plus poor hydration, using ws-06-a entries — pass = no cluster exists; fail = urgent reassessment is indicated before travel proceeds.

Verify whether emergency fallback beyond Cavallini has been identified, including local transport and emergency facility routing in San José, by reviewing ws-06-c — pass = fallback is documented and usable; fail = complete ws-06-c immediately because delay during an acute event is unacceptable.

Verify whether routine self-monitoring remains appropriate by checking that bleeding is decreasing, pain is controlled, fluids are tolerated, and ambulation is safe — pass = stay in self-monitoring lane with scheduled checks; fail = move to the appropriate higher lane and document the transition.

Verify whether any unresolved item above the self-monitoring threshold remains open at the end of the day by reviewing this checklist and the action notes in ws-06-a — pass = every higher-level item has documented resolution or active clinician instruction; fail = the domain cannot close and travel clearance is blocked.

decision_gates.condition: No unresolved item exceeds routine self-monitoring thresholds; worsening variables have not persisted across two consecutive check-ins; no red-flag cluster is present.
decision_gates.pass_action: Remain in routine monitoring and continue scheduled logging in ws-06-a.
decision_gates.fail_action: Escalate to same-day clinic contact, urgent reassessment, or emergency intervention using ws-06-c.

decision_gates.condition: Active bleeding persists beyond expected window, fever/infection cluster appears, hydration or medication continuity breaks, or mobility fails for safe transport.
decision_gates.pass_action: Travel-preparation tasks may continue only if all thresholds are cleared.
decision_gates.fail_action: Suspend packing, checkout, and airport transfer planning until Cavallini or emergency stabilization resolves the issue.

cross_references: Costa Rica Dental Recovery Daily Symptom and Medication Tracking Log
Clinic Contact, Local Emergency Option, and Escalation Routing Form for Post-Op Issues
Return-Flight Fitness Assessment After Cavallini Restorative Treatment
common_errors: Treating this as a comfort checklist instead of a decision threshold tool; the point is to classify worsening bleeding, swelling, fever, prosthetic instability, or medication failure before airport day.
Using anxiety or reassurance from travel companions as the escalation standard instead of the actual Cavallini discharge thresholds, trend pattern, and functional decline.
Boarding a return flight with an unresolved high-bite or shifting provisional because nothing is visibly broken; mechanical instability in All-on-X cases must be escalated while still in San José.
example_items: Verified that pain remained below the escalation threshold by reviewing ws-06-a entries from 8:00 AM and 2:00 PM on 2026-02-14 — pass = pain fell from 5/10 to 3/10 after scheduled ibuprofen and no extra rescue dosing was needed; no clinic contact required.

Verified possible infection pathway on 2026-02-13 at 2:00 PM using thermometer reading 100.5 F, worsening right-side swelling, and malaise documented in ws-06-a — fail = urgent reassessment requested from Cavallini by WhatsApp and hotel departure prep paused pending instructions.

Verified mechanical stability of immediate provisional before planned departure by asking about closure pain and checking patient-reported clicking on the left posterior segment — fail = patient reported new high bite and localized soreness; same-stay evaluation scheduled at Cavallini before airport transfer.
