sections.section_title: Cavallini Post-Operative Contact Hierarchy
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the Cavallini discharge packet, any WhatsApp messages or email confirmations from the clinic, and the medication handoff paperwork from procedure day. You should also have ws-06-a open so the exact procedure and recovery date can be copied consistently into any escalation message. Complete this section before the first night in the hotel, especially if IV sedation, implants, extractions, bone grafting, or All-on-X treatment was performed. This section is complete when at least one daytime contact path and one after-hours path are recorded with a tested or confirmed method of use.

fields.label: Primary Cavallini daytime post-op contact name and role for recovery questions
fields.placeholder: Ana Rodríguez — International Patient Coordinator
fields.validation_hint: Enter a named person or role used for post-op routing, such as 'Ana Rodríguez — International Patient Coordinator'.

fields.label: Direct daytime phone or WhatsApp number actually provided for post-treatment issues
fields.placeholder: +506 8888-2147 (WhatsApp enabled)
fields.validation_hint: Record the direct number exactly as dialed from Costa Rica, e.g. '+506 8888-2147 (WhatsApp enabled)'.

fields.label: After-hours Cavallini escalation route for bleeding, fever, swelling, or uncontrolled pain
fields.placeholder: After-hours post-op WhatsApp monitored by on-call dental team: +506 8701-4422
fields.validation_hint: Specify the route and use method, such as 'After-hours post-op WhatsApp monitored by on-call dental team: +506 8701-4422'.

fields.label: Preferred message format requested by Cavallini when reporting a complication
fields.placeholder: Send: patient full name, procedure date, hotel name/room, symptom timeline, temperature, pain score, photos if swelling visible, medication doses already taken
fields.validation_hint: A complete entry names the exact information the clinic expects in the first message, not a generic note like 'text them if needed'.

fields.label: Exact Cavallini procedure description to include in every escalation message
fields.placeholder: Upper All-on-6 with 4 extractions, IV sedation, immediate provisional delivered on 2026-02-12
fields.validation_hint: State the actual procedure and date, e.g. 'Implant at #19 with bone graft on 2026-02-14'.

fields.label: Companion or escort assigned to communicate if the patient is sedated, nauseated, or unable to explain symptoms clearly
fields.placeholder: Michael Costa — spouse and first-night recorder — U.S. mobile +1 617-555-0184
fields.validation_hint: If an escort is present, record full identity and number, such as 'Michael Costa — spouse — +1 617-555-0184'.

sections.section_title: Recovery Lodging and Local Movement Control
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, retrieve the hotel confirmation, room assignment, front desk phone card, airport-transfer booking, and any clinic-arranged transport details. If the clinic's onsite hotel or preferred lodging changed after surgery, use the actual current location, not the original reservation. This section must be completed before the first overnight period because hotel staff or drivers may need to help route urgent reassessment. This section is complete when a third party could locate you and arrange transport without asking follow-up questions.

fields.label: Current San José recovery lodging name, full address, and room number
fields.placeholder: Holiday Inn Express Forum Costa Rica — Del Centro Empresarial Forum 2, Santa Ana — Room 314
fields.validation_hint: Include enough detail for pickup, e.g. 'Hotel name — street/location — room number'.

fields.label: Hotel front desk number and whether English-language assistance is available overnight
fields.placeholder: +506 2208-2100 — overnight English support available
fields.validation_hint: Record both number and language support, such as '+506 2208-2100 — overnight English support available'.

fields.label: Fastest transport option back to Cavallini for same-day reassessment from this lodging
fields.placeholder: Uber from hotel main entrance to Cavallini clinic, approx. 12 minutes outside rush hour
fields.validation_hint: Name the actual transport method and pickup point, not just 'taxi'.

fields.label: Airport transfer provider and cancellation cutoff if departure must be postponed for medical reasons
fields.placeholder: Clinic-arranged shuttle through Interbus — must cancel 4 hours before pickup to avoid charge
fields.validation_hint: A complete entry includes provider name and cutoff, such as 'Interbus — cancel 4 hours before pickup'.

fields.label: Stair, elevator, or mobility limitation at current lodging that could affect departure-day safety
fields.placeholder: Room on 3rd floor; elevator available; no stairs required to reach lobby pickup area
fields.validation_hint: State the real movement condition, e.g. 'One flight of stairs to breakfast area; elevator to lobby available'.

fields.label: Nearest pharmacy or hotel assistance source if replacement gauze, ice, bottled water, or soft-food support is needed overnight
fields.placeholder: Hotel front desk can send bottled water/ice; Farmacia Fischel at Momentum Pinares closes 10 PM
fields.validation_hint: Record a specific support source, such as 'Hotel front desk can deliver ice and extra bottled water overnight'.

sections.section_title: Emergency Fallback Outside the Planned Treatment Chain
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the discharge instructions for any emergency directions from Cavallini, confirm local emergency numbers usable from your Costa Rica phone or roaming setup, and note whether your travel companion can communicate in Spanish. Do not wait until symptoms accelerate to research emergency fallback. If this section cannot be completed with verified details, mark REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and obtain the information from the clinic or hotel immediately. This section is complete when both urgent clinic reassessment and true emergency care routes are documented separately.

fields.label: Costa Rica emergency number to call from local mobile or hotel line if airway, uncontrolled bleeding, or collapse occurs
fields.placeholder: 911 from Costa Rica mobile or hotel phone
fields.validation_hint: Enter the actual emergency number and call context, e.g. '911 from Costa Rica mobile or hotel phone'.

fields.label: Nearest emergency facility or hospital the hotel or Cavallini would use if post-op symptoms exceed clinic scope
fields.placeholder: Hospital CIMA San José — Autopista Próspero Fernández, Escazú
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry names a real facility and location, such as 'Hospital CIMA San José — Escazú'.

fields.label: Emergency transport fallback if rideshare is unavailable or the patient cannot sit upright safely
fields.placeholder: Hotel front desk to call licensed taxi immediately; ambulance via 911 if airway concern or uncontrolled bleeding
fields.validation_hint: State both non-emergency and emergency transport fallback, not just 'call a cab'.

fields.label: Spanish-language phrase or hotel assistance note for reporting 'recent dental implants/full-mouth surgery with swelling, bleeding, fever, or medication problems'
fields.placeholder: Necesito ayuda médica. Tuve cirugía dental con implantes ayer y ahora tengo hinchazón, sangrado y fiebre.
fields.validation_hint: Use a phrase that directly describes the dental-surgery situation, e.g. 'Tuve cirugía dental ayer y ahora tengo sangrado que no para'.

fields.label: U.S. emergency contact who should be updated if reassessment, hospitalization, or flight delay occurs
fields.placeholder: Laura Benton — daughter — +1 404-555-0171 — update if overnight observation or flight change needed
fields.validation_hint: Record full name, relationship, number, and update trigger.

sections.section_title: Rapid Report Packet for Any Escalation Call or Message
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, open ws-06-a and copy the most recent symptom data, medication timing, temperature, and oral-intake status. You will also need the pharmacy labels, the discharge packet, and any photos of swelling, bleeding, or prosthesis concerns taken during the stay. This section should be updated every time a significant symptom changes because it becomes the script for contacting Cavallini or emergency services. This section is complete when another person could send an accurate escalation message using only the entries below.

fields.label: Most recent temperature, pain score, and exact local time ready to report verbatim
fields.placeholder: 100.5 F, pain 7/10 at 2:00 PM San José time
fields.validation_hint: Use the real measured values, e.g. '98.9 F, pain 4/10 at 8:00 AM'.

fields.label: Current bleeding, swelling, and swallowing status statement for first-line triage
fields.placeholder: Bleeding no longer soaking gauze, but right-side facial swelling increased since morning; swallowing water is uncomfortable but possible
fields.validation_hint: Provide a concise triage statement covering the core oral issue, not a vague phrase like 'feels worse'.

fields.label: Last three Cavallini medication doses actually taken, delayed, or vomited
fields.placeholder: 1:00 PM ibuprofen 600 mg taken; 12:30 PM Augmentin delayed 2 hrs due to nausea; 8:00 AM dexamethasone taken with yogurt
fields.validation_hint: List actual dose events in sequence, such as '8:00 AM clindamycin taken; 12:00 PM acetaminophen vomited 20 min later'.

fields.label: Hydration, urination, and soft-food tolerance status for the last 12 hours
fields.placeholder: Approx. 24 oz water tolerated, urinated twice, soup caused nausea, yogurt tolerated
fields.validation_hint: A complete entry addresses all three variables: fluids, urination, and food tolerance.

fields.label: Photo or video evidence location for swelling, bleeding, or prosthesis problem
fields.placeholder: iPhone album 'Cavallini recovery' — photos taken 2:05 PM and 4:40 PM showing right facial swelling
fields.validation_hint: Identify exactly where the evidence is stored and when captured.

fields.label: Go-to escalation script to send first if symptoms worsen after hours
fields.placeholder: This is Julia Benton, upper All-on-6 on 2026-02-12. At 2:00 PM I have 100.5 F fever, pain 7/10 despite medication, and right-side swelling increasing since morning. Staying at Holiday Inn Express Forum, Room 314. Please advise if I should return to clinic now.
fields.validation_hint: The script should include identity, procedure/date, current symptoms, lodging location, and request for instruction.

decision_gates.gate_title: Escalation Routing Ready Before First Overnight Stay
decision_gates.condition: Daytime clinic route, after-hours route, lodging location, and emergency fallback are all documented and usable during the stay.
decision_gates.pass_action: Treat communication readiness as active and use this form for any post-op event.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not rely on memory; complete missing routing items immediately with the clinic or hotel before overnight recovery continues.

decision_gates.gate_title: Report Packet Ready for Rapid Reassessment Request
decision_gates.condition: A third party could accurately report the procedure, symptoms, medications, hotel location, and callback route using the completed form.
decision_gates.pass_action: Use the rapid report packet for all calls, WhatsApp messages, or emergency desk interactions.
decision_gates.fail_action: Update the missing fields before symptoms worsen further or transport is initiated.

cross_references: Costa Rica Dental Recovery Daily Symptom and Medication Tracking Log
Post-Implant and Full-Mouth Surgery Red-Flag Escalation Checklist for San José Stay
common_errors: Saving only the general clinic website or main reception number without confirming the actual after-hours or urgent post-op route used during the San José recovery stay.
Failing to write down the exact hotel, room, and transport pickup details, which delays reassessment when swelling, bleeding, or sedation-related problems make verbal explanation difficult.
Leaving medication names in unlabeled packaging from the onsite pharmacy without transcribing them into this routing form; emergency clinicians or drivers may need the list immediately.

worksheets.title: Return-Flight Fitness Assessment After Cavallini Restorative Treatment
worksheets.purpose: Produces the go/no-go decision on whether the patient is clinically and operationally fit to complete commercial travel from San José back to the United States after Cavallini treatment.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 20-30 minutes on departure-evaluation day

sections.section_title: Departure-Day Symptom Stability and Function Screen
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the last 24 to 48 hours of ws-06-a entries, the results of ws-06-b, and the Cavallini discharge instructions or any updated reassessment notes. Do not complete this section from memory; use measured temperature, actual pain scores, and direct observations from the current day. If the patient had implants, extractions, bone-related work, or All-on-X treatment, compare the present state against the trend, not just this morning's comfort level. This section is complete when each travel-critical symptom is documented as improving, stable, or unacceptable for airport transit.

fields.label: Planned departure date and airline first-segment departure time from San José
fields.placeholder: 2026-02-15 — United 1087 departing SJO at 1:20 PM
fields.validation_hint: Record the actual departure timing, e.g. '2026-02-15 — Delta 1742 departing SJO at 1:20 PM'.

fields.label: Current pain score and whether control is predictable through a 6- to 10-hour airport plus flight block
fields.placeholder: Pain 3/10 at rest, 4/10 with speech; controlled on current schedule and no breakthrough dosing needed since midnight
fields.validation_hint: A correct entry states score plus travel-block predictability, not just 'pain manageable'.

fields.label: Current bleeding status at all surgical or restorative sites on departure morning
fields.placeholder: No active bleeding; occasional faint pink saliva only; no fresh gauze needed overnight
fields.validation_hint: Describe the real bleeding state, e.g. 'No active bleeding; gauze not required since 9 PM'.

fields.label: Current swelling trend and any effect on swallowing, speech, or mouth opening
fields.placeholder: Upper facial swelling slightly improved vs yesterday; swallowing water and speaking in short sentences without difficulty
fields.validation_hint: Include trend and function, such as 'Left mandibular swelling stable; swallowing intact; mouth opening still limited to two fingers'.

fields.label: Most recent temperature and whether any fever-plus-swelling-plus-pain cluster remains unresolved
fields.placeholder: 98.7 F at 7:30 AM; no unresolved fever cluster
fields.validation_hint: Use measured value plus cluster status, e.g. '100.4 F at 6:45 AM; unresolved fever cluster present'.

fields.label: Departure-day oral function status for sipping water, taking medication, and tolerating approved soft food
fields.placeholder: Able to sip water, swallow tablets, and eat yogurt/oatmeal without severe pain or nausea
fields.validation_hint: Address all three functions: fluids, medications, and diet tolerance.

sections.section_title: Medication Continuity and Travel-Day Dosing Control
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the pharmacy labels from Cavallini or the onsite pharmacy, the actual pill supply being packed into carry-on luggage, and the last dose entries from ws-06-a. If any medication was delayed, vomited, or skipped in the last 24 hours, note that here because it changes the reliability of symptom control in transit. This section is complete when the next required doses are timed against airport security, boarding, and the first flight segment without guesswork. Do not mark this section complete if the patient is still requiring rescue dosing more often than prescribed.

fields.label: Next required antibiotic, anti-inflammatory, analgesic, or rinse dose times during airport and first flight segment
fields.placeholder: Augmentin due 2:00 PM in terminal/aircraft; ibuprofen due 1:30 PM before boarding; rinse next due after hotel checkout not during transit
fields.validation_hint: List each relevant medication with an actual due time during travel, not 'take as needed'.

fields.label: Are all remaining Cavallini medications packed in carry-on with labels and enough supply for delays
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only if labeled medication supply is in carry-on and sufficient for delays or missed connections.

fields.label: Any medication event in the last 24 hours that makes symptom interpretation unreliable for travel clearance
fields.placeholder: None
options: Rescue pain medication needed more often than prescribed
Multiple medication-control problems in last 24 hours
fields.validation_hint: Choose the actual event status, e.g. 'Vomited dose within 30 minutes'.

fields.label: Nausea, sedation effect, or dizziness status relevant to airport walking, check-in, and security screening
fields.placeholder: No current nausea; no dizziness when standing; no residual sedation concerns
fields.validation_hint: Describe the actual transport effect, e.g. 'Mild dizziness on standing; requires escort during all walking'.

fields.label: Does current medication use still require an escort to manage transit safely
fields.placeholder: false
fields.validation_hint: Mark true if the patient cannot reliably self-manage medications, mobility, or decision-making during travel.

sections.section_title: Airport Transit Tolerance and Mobility Readiness
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the planned transport from hotel to airport, expected standing and walking requirements, luggage burden, and whether wheelchair assistance is already arranged. Use the current physical condition, not the original itinerary assumptions. If the patient cannot safely walk to the bathroom, enter a vehicle, sit upright for extended periods, or handle delays without destabilizing recovery, this section should fail even if oral symptoms are improving. This section is complete when the actual airport path has been matched to the patient's present function.

fields.label: Can the patient walk from hotel room to lobby, enter transport, and clear airport processing without unsafe instability
fields.placeholder: Yes independently
options: No — not fit for airport transit
fields.validation_hint: Choose the real mobility level, e.g. 'Yes with escort support' or 'No — wheelchair needed'.

fields.label: Estimated time the patient must remain upright from hotel checkout to first in-flight seated rest
fields.validation_hint: Enter the estimated number of hours, e.g. '4'.

fields.label: Hydration and restroom access plan for the full San José airport plus first-flight segment
fields.placeholder: Carry two bottled waters after security, aisle seat assigned, restroom use expected before boarding and once during flight
fields.validation_hint: A complete entry addresses fluids and bathroom access across the actual travel block.

fields.label: Special airport assistance already confirmed if swelling, weakness, or oral surgery limits normal travel pace
fields.placeholder: Wheelchair assistance requested with United for SJO check-in to gate; escort traveling alongside
fields.validation_hint: Record the actual service arranged, e.g. 'Wheelchair assistance confirmed through airline reservation'.

fields.label: What specific condition would force same-day flight postponement instead of proceeding to the airport
fields.placeholder: Any restart of active bleeding, fever 100.4 F or higher with increasing swelling, or inability to take noon antibiotic before departure
fields.validation_hint: Name concrete no-fly triggers tied to this case, not a vague phrase like 'if worse'.

sections.section_title: Final Return Packet Audit and Departure Decision
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, retrieve the full discharge packet from Cavallini, any implant or prosthetic details provided, medication records, procedure summary, and written complication instructions. You should also have ws-06-c available so the clinic and emergency contact routes can be duplicated into the return materials if needed. Do not mark the chapter complete until the packet is duplicated before leaving San José. This section is complete when the return packet would allow a U.S. dentist to triage the case without guessing what procedure, components, or medications were involved.

fields.label: Are procedure summary, written post-op instructions, and complication guidance physically or digitally duplicated before airport departure
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true only after a duplicate copy exists outside the original packet.

fields.label: Implant brand, component, or prosthetic detail included in the packet if applicable to this Cavallini case
fields.placeholder: Nobel Biocare upper arch implants with immediate provisional; component sheet printed and saved to phone
fields.validation_hint: If applicable, record the exact brand/detail present, e.g. 'Zimmer implant details attached to discharge packet'.

fields.label: Final medication list with actual-use notes ready for U.S. follow-up handoff
fields.placeholder: Augmentin started 2026-02-12, one dose delayed 2 hrs on 2026-02-13 due to nausea; ibuprofen used through departure day; chlorhexidine started evening after surgery
fields.validation_hint: A complete entry includes actual-use notes, not just the prescribed names.

fields.label: Overall return-flight clearance decision for this departure date
fields.placeholder: Cleared for commercial travel
options: Not cleared — Cavallini reassessment required before flight
Not cleared — emergency or urgent stabilization required
fields.validation_hint: Select the actual departure decision reached after completing all sections.

fields.label: If not fully cleared, exact action to take before any airport segment proceeds
fields.placeholder: Contact Cavallini at 8:00 AM for reassessment of unilateral swelling and postpone shuttle pickup until clinician confirms travel safety
fields.validation_hint: State the next operational step with timing, e.g. 'Return to clinic before checkout; cancel airport transfer by 9:00 AM if not cleared'.

decision_gates.condition: Pain is controlled, bleeding is stable, swelling is stable or improving, hydration and soft intake are adequate, and no unresolved fever/infection cluster is present.
decision_gates.pass_action: Proceed to medication and mobility review for final flight clearance.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not self-clear for travel; remain in Costa Rica recovery control and obtain Cavallini reassessment or emergency stabilization.

decision_gates.gate_title: Airport Mobility and Medication Continuity Gate
decision_gates.condition: The patient can complete airport transit safely, carry-on medications are available and timed for travel, and no recent medication-control failure makes symptom stability unreliable.
decision_gates.pass_action: Approve departure logistics if return packet is also complete.
decision_gates.fail_action: Postpone departure or arrange additional support; do not proceed to airport on assumption alone.

decision_gates.condition: Procedure summary, instructions, medication record, complication guidance, and implant/prosthetic details when applicable have been duplicated before leaving San José.
decision_gates.pass_action: Close the chapter and trigger U.S. Follow-Up Continuity, Warranty Preservation, and Long-Term Maintenance Control.
decision_gates.fail_action: Halt checkout and obtain the missing documentation from Cavallini before airport departure.

cross_references: Costa Rica Dental Recovery Daily Symptom and Medication Tracking Log
Post-Implant and Full-Mouth Surgery Red-Flag Escalation Checklist for San José Stay
Clinic Contact, Local Emergency Option, and Escalation Routing Form for Post-Op Issues
common_errors: Clearing travel because the patient feels better in bed at the hotel, without testing whether pain control, hydration, mobility, and medication timing will hold through a 6- to 10-hour airport-and-flight block.
Ignoring unresolved bleeding or a fresh fever because flights and airport transfers are already booked; in this domain, travel bookings do not override medical instability.

Checking out without auditing the final return packet for implant brand, prosthetic details, and written complication instructions, which can break U.S. handoff and warranty preservation even if the flight itself is tolerated.

chapters.chapter_title: Stateside Dentist Handoff, 30-Day Complication Capture, Cavallini Warranty Compliance, and Maintenance Scheduling

worksheets.title: U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases
worksheets.purpose: Produces a ranked inventory of U.S. dental offices willing to handle surveillance, urgent triage, or maintenance after Cavallini treatment in Costa Rica.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 40 minutes on first build, 10 minutes per provider update
table_columns: U.S. Dental Office and City/State for Post-Costa-Rica Follow-Up
Named Staff Member Who Confirmed Foreign-Case Acceptance
Accepted Scope for Cavallini Aftercare (Emergency Triage / 7-14 Day Review / Hygiene Maintenance / Prosthetic Adjustment)
Will Office Evaluate Nobel Biocare or Zimmer Implant Cases Placed Abroad?
Required Records Before Scheduling (Implant Brand / Procedure Summary / Radiographs / Medication List)
Earliest Available Post-Return Appointment Window
Inventory Status (Primary / Backup / Partial Coverage / Declined)

decision_gates.gate_title: Named U.S. Follow-Up Provider Confirmed Before Final Return
decision_gates.condition: At least one U.S. office is marked Primary and includes a named contact, accepted scope of care, required records list, and an appointment window after return.
decision_gates.pass_action: Use the Primary office for packet transmission and first post-return review scheduling.
decision_gates.fail_action: Keep the case in active provider search, continue calling offices from this inventory, and do not treat the Costa Rica phase as operationally closed.

decision_gates.gate_title: Case Complexity Matches Provider Support Level
decision_gates.condition: For implant-supported bridge, multi-implant, or All-on-X cases, the chosen provider accepts more than emergency X-rays only and can perform at least surveillance or coordinated triage.
decision_gates.pass_action: Classify the office as sufficient continuity coverage and proceed to handoff packet transmission.
decision_gates.fail_action: Downgrade the office to Partial Coverage and continue building backup specialist pathways.

cross_references: Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers
First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home
common_errors: Recording only the main scheduling line and not the specific staff member who confirmed willingness to see a recent Cavallini patient with implants or All-on-X work
Marking a provider as full follow-up coverage when the office only agreed to emergency X-rays or visual triage for foreign restorative work
Failing to record whether the office will work with Nobel Biocare or Zimmer implant cases, forcing delays when component-specific issues arise

sample_rows.Named Staff Member Who Confirmed Foreign: Melissa R., Treatment Coordinator — confirmed by phone 03/12/2026

sample_rows.Earliest Available Post-Return Appointme: Within 9 days of return if packet received 48 hours in advance

sample_rows.Named Staff Member Who Confirmed Foreign: Carlos M., Surgical Scheduler — email reply 03/14/2026

sample_rows.Will Office Evaluate Nobel Biocare or Zi: Yes — will evaluate both, but component treatment depends on records

worksheets.title: Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers
worksheets.purpose: Verifies that the Cavallini treatment packet is complete, duplicated, transmitted to the U.S. provider, and acknowledged as readable for local follow-up care.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 30 minutes at Costa Rica checkout, 15 minutes after U.S. transmission
checklist_items: Verify that the Cavallini procedure summary for the most recent treatment phase is in hand before leaving the clinic or onsite hotel area — source: printed discharge paperwork or patient coordinator packet; pass = procedure performed, treatment date, and treating provider names are listed; fail = request corrected discharge paperwork from Cavallini the same day and do not depart with the packet marked complete.

Verify that implant brand information for each implant-related phase is explicitly documented as Nobel Biocare, Zimmer, or another named system — source: surgical summary, implant card, or component sheet; pass = brand is named for every implant event; fail = open a records recovery request with Cavallini before airport departure.

Verify that each implant-related document shows the actual procedure date tied to that phase of care — source: operative note, discharge summary, or appointment receipt with clinical detail; pass = every implant placement or prosthetic delivery date is traceable; fail = request dated confirmation from Cavallini and classify the packet as incomplete.

Verify that the packet states whether the delivered prosthesis is provisional, immediate-load, temporary bridge, final crown, or final full-arch prosthesis — source: restorative delivery note or written treatment summary; pass = prosthesis status is explicitly named; fail = ask Cavallini to issue a clarifying note because U.S. providers cannot infer this safely.

Verify that post-operative medication details are documented with drug name, dosage, and timing — source: Cavallini prescription printout or discharge medication sheet; pass = every active medication is listed in readable form; fail = request a replacement medication list from the clinic or onsite pharmacy immediately.

Verify that Cavallini post-op instructions are included in a format the patient can read without internet access — source: printed discharge instructions or downloaded PDF saved locally; pass = a local offline copy exists; fail = save or print the instructions before leaving San José.

Verify that any images or radiographs supplied by Cavallini are copied into both a cloud folder and a local device folder — source: USB drive, email attachment, patient portal download, or messaging transfer; pass = two accessible copies exist in separate locations; fail = duplicate the files before travel day ends.

Verify that the file names for radiographs or imaging exports identify the treatment phase and date — source: digital imaging folder; pass = files can be matched to the treatment event without guessing; fail = rename and organize files before transmitting them to the U.S. provider.

Verify that provider names from Cavallini are captured for the dentist, surgeon, or prosthodontic lead involved in the recent phase — source: treatment summary, clinic correspondence, invoice with provider details; pass = at least one responsible treating clinician is named; fail = request provider attribution from the patient coordinator.

Verify that any warranty document, warranty enrollment condition, or written warranty summary from Cavallini is included in the packet — source: clinic warranty paperwork, quote terms, or emailed policy language; pass = warranty evidence is stored with the packet; fail = request the written warranty terms and note the gap in the warranty register.

Verify that the packet includes the return-home date and planned U.S. review timing — source: travel itinerary and provider scheduling record; pass = the U.S. office can see when the patient returned and when follow-up is expected; fail = add a cover sheet before transmission.

Verify that the packet includes the patient’s current symptom status at departure if any pain, swelling, bite issue, or soft-tissue irritation was present — source: recovery notes or day-of-departure symptom log; pass = the U.S. provider receives a baseline status; fail = create a dated baseline note immediately.

Verify that the U.S. provider’s secure email, upload link, or fax route for foreign dental records has been confirmed before sending files — source: direct call with the office; pass = a named route and recipient are documented; fail = do not send to a generic inbox without confirmation.

Verify that the full packet was transmitted within 48 hours of arriving home — source: sent email, portal upload confirmation, fax confirmation page, or courier tracking; pass = transmission timestamp is within the required window; fail = send the packet the same business day and log the delay.

Verify that the U.S. office acknowledged receipt of the packet in writing — source: email reply, portal message, or fax confirmation plus staff email; pass = a dated acknowledgement exists; fail = follow up by phone and resend the packet within 2 business days.

Verify that the U.S. office confirmed the attachments are readable, especially imaging files — source: written reply from the office after opening the files; pass = office states files opened successfully; fail = request missing or alternate-format files from Cavallini the same business day.

Verify that any unreadable CBCT, panoramic, or radiograph files have an active recovery request logged with Cavallini — source: sent email or message thread to the clinic; pass = request date, contact route, and missing file description are documented; fail = open the request immediately and copy the U.S. office if needed.

Verify that the patient has one cloud-stored packet folder and one local offline copy accessible during travel disruptions or urgent visits — source: phone, laptop, tablet, or printed binder; pass = both copies are available; fail = duplicate before relying on the packet for emergency care.

Verify that the packet includes a concise cover note for the U.S. dentist describing the Costa Rica procedure scope and requested follow-up role — source: patient-prepared summary using this chapter; pass = the office can see exactly whether surveillance, triage, or maintenance is requested; fail = create the cover note before the first appointment.

Verify that any missing packet element has been marked as an open recovery item rather than silently omitted — source: packet index or missing-items log; pass = each missing item has a recovery action and date; fail = classify the handoff as incomplete and continue records retrieval.

Verify that the first post-return review is scheduled or explicitly declined by the U.S. office after packet review — source: appointment confirmation, call log, or office email; pass = appointment date or documented decline exists; fail = move to the next office in the provider inventory.

Verify that the final packet folder includes communication proof for every resend, correction, or file replacement related to the handoff — source: email thread, portal screenshots, or fax logs; pass = transmission history is reconstructable; fail = save all evidence to the warranty register folder immediately.

decision_gates.gate_title: Implant and Prosthetic Packet Sufficiency for U.S. Triage
decision_gates.condition: The packet identifies implant brand and procedure date for each implant-related phase, names prosthesis status, and includes readable supporting records.
decision_gates.pass_action: Transmit to the U.S. provider and proceed to scheduling the first review.
decision_gates.fail_action: Keep the return-to-U.S. handoff open, request missing records from Cavallini immediately, and log the deficiency in the warranty register.

decision_gates.condition: The receiving U.S. office has confirmed in writing that the packet was received and files are readable within 2 business days.
decision_gates.pass_action: Treat the provider handoff as operationally transferred and anchor the first review date.
decision_gates.fail_action: Follow up by phone, resend the packet, and escalate to the next office in the provider inventory if unresolved.

cross_references: U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases
Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
common_errors: Leaving Costa Rica with payment receipts and selfies but without the implant brand, procedure dates, or prosthesis status that a U.S. provider needs to intervene safely
Assuming emailed files were usable without obtaining written confirmation that the U.S. office could actually open the radiographs or attachment formats
Storing the only copy of the handoff packet in cloud storage that cannot be accessed during travel or if internet service fails
example_items: Verify that implant brand information for each implant-related phase is explicitly documented as Nobel Biocare, Zimmer, or another named system — source checked: Cavallini surgical summary dated 04/18/2026 and implant card PDF; pass = Nobel Biocare listed for implants #3, #5, #12, and #14; action taken = filed in cloud folder and sent to North Shore Prosthodontics on 04/21/2026.

Verify that the U.S. office acknowledged receipt of the packet in writing — source checked: email from Melissa R. at North Shore Prosthodontics received 04/22/2026 at 9:14 AM; pass = office confirmed receipt of records and scheduled 04/29/2026 review; action taken = saved email to warranty register folder.

Verify that any unreadable CBCT, panoramic, or radiograph files have an active recovery request logged with Cavallini — source checked: U.S. office reported CBCT export would not open on 04/22/2026; fail = same-day email sent to Cavallini patient coordinator requesting alternate DICOM export and copied U.S. office; status = open recovery item logged.

worksheets.title: First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home
worksheets.purpose: Creates a structured symptom and escalation log for the first 30 days after returning from Cavallini so changes in function, comfort, or tissue health are routed quickly and documented for care and warranty support.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 8 minutes per entry for days 1-7, then 8 minutes every 3 days or when new symptoms appear

sections.section_title: Entry Timing and Return-Home Surveillance Cycle Control
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, retrieve your return-home date, Cavallini discharge instructions, and any instruction about when the first U.S. follow-up review should occur. Also review the chapter rule that entries are required daily for the first 7 days after return, then every 3 days through day 30, plus immediately after any new symptom appears. Complete this section at the top of every issue-capture entry so the symptom timeline can be reconstructed later. This section is complete when the entry date, day-from-return count, treatment phase being monitored, and next required check date are all explicitly recorded.

fields.label: Date this post-return symptom check is being recorded
fields.validation_hint: Enter the actual date of the observation, such as 2026-04-24 for day 3 after landing back in the U.S.

fields.label: Day count since landing back in the United States from San José
fields.validation_hint: Use a whole number tied to the return flight date; e.g., 3 means third day after return home.

fields.label: Cavallini treatment phase currently being monitored
fields.placeholder: Implant-supported provisional bridge delivered in Costa Rica
options: Single crown or bridge treatment without surgery
fields.validation_hint: Choose the exact phase being observed, such as All-on-X provisional delivery or single crown placement.

fields.label: Was this entry triggered by the scheduled surveillance cycle or by a new symptom event?
fields.placeholder: Scheduled day-3 surveillance entry
options: New symptom event after normal recovery
fields.validation_hint: Choose whether the log was routine or prompted by a new concern, such as New symptom event after chewing dinner.

fields.label: Date of most recent prior issue-capture entry for comparison
fields.validation_hint: Enter the previous entry date when available so trend classification is possible.

fields.label: Next required symptom-check date based on this chapter's 30-day monitoring cadence
fields.validation_hint: For days 1-7 enter the next day; later entries may be 3 days later unless a provider instructed otherwise.

sections.section_title: Bite Function, Prosthesis Stability, and Eating-Tolerance Observation
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review your previous issue-capture entry and, if relevant, think about your most recent chewing attempt, speech difficulty, or sensation of movement in the provisional or prosthesis. Use actual function testing from soft-food chewing and normal speaking rather than a vague overall impression. This section is complete when bite contact pattern, chewing ability, mobility concerns, and speech change status are recorded with enough detail that either Cavallini or a U.S. dentist could triage next steps without guessing. If the treatment was non-surgical crown work only, still complete the bite and chewing fields because occlusal interference may emerge after swelling changes.

fields.label: Where does the bite hit first or feel overloaded when teeth come together after Cavallini treatment?
fields.placeholder: Heavy first contact on upper right provisional bridge near back chewing side; left side feels delayed to touch
fields.validation_hint: Describe the exact contact pattern, such as heavy on right posterior provisional and cannot get even bilateral contact.

fields.label: Can you chew soft foods on both sides without pain, rocking, or forced one-sided chewing?
fields.placeholder: Soft foods possible on left only
options: Yes, but only one side comfortably
fields.validation_hint: Choose the option that matches actual function, not hoped-for function.

fields.label: Any sensation that the provisional bridge, crown, screw-retained unit, or full-arch prosthesis is moving or rocking
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true if there is any perceptible loosening, clicking, or rocking during chewing or tongue pressure.

fields.label: If movement or clicking is present, describe exactly when it happens and which restoration feels unstable
fields.placeholder: Small click on right side of upper full-arch provisional when biting scrambled eggs; no visible break but feels less stable than yesterday
fields.validation_hint: Record the location, trigger, and comparison to prior entries, such as rocking only during chewing on left molar area.

fields.label: Speech change noticed after return home (lisping, air escape, clicking, or altered pronunciation)
fields.placeholder: Mild lisp worse than day 1, mostly on S sounds
options: Speech change not applicable to this treatment type
fields.validation_hint: Choose the most accurate speech status since return.

fields.label: Compared with the prior entry, the bite and chewing function are
fields.placeholder: Worsening
fields.validation_hint: Trend classification drives routing; e.g., Worsening if chewing is harder than at the last entry.

sections.section_title: Pain, Swelling, Bleeding, Taste, and Soft-Tissue Trend Capture
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather your prior symptom entry, current medication list, and any photographs taken of the gums, incision areas, or prosthesis contact points. Observe trend, not just intensity: note whether swelling has declined, stayed the same, or increased over the last 24 hours. This section is complete when pain level, swelling pattern, bleeding or drainage, odor or bad taste, and tissue irritation are documented clearly enough to decide whether routine monitoring or same-day escalation is required. If anything is worsening instead of stabilizing, be ready to route immediately using the escalation section that follows.

fields.label: Current pain level related to the Cavallini-treated area on a 0-10 scale
fields.validation_hint: Use a whole number from 0 to 10, such as 5 for moderate pain requiring attention but not emergency-level distress.

fields.label: Pain trend versus the last issue-capture entry
fields.placeholder: Worsening over last 24 hours
fields.validation_hint: Choose the trend, such as Improving after medication taper or Worsening over last 24 hours.

fields.label: Swelling pattern at the implant, gum, cheek, or jaw area since the prior entry
fields.placeholder: Increased after initial decline
fields.validation_hint: An increase after an initial decline is a same-day escalation trigger in this chapter.

fields.label: Any bleeding, drainage, foul taste, or persistent bad odor coming from the treated area
fields.placeholder: Foul taste with light drainage near upper left implant site
fields.validation_hint: Choose the closest match and use the next field for detail if present.

fields.label: Exact soft-tissue problem observed today around the Costa Rica restorative work
fields.placeholder: Red sore spot under right side of temporary bridge rubbing cheek; eating causes sharp irritation and sleep was interrupted last night
fields.validation_hint: State location, trigger, and function impact, such as ulcer under provisional flange causing eating difficulty.

fields.label: Medication use today for this issue, including whether relief was adequate
fields.placeholder: Ibuprofen 600 mg at 8 AM and 2 PM; relief lasted about 3 hours and pain returned with chewing
fields.validation_hint: List actual drug, dose, timing, and response rather than simply writing taking meds.

sections.section_title: Same-Day Escalation Routing and Evidence Preservation
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the decision thresholds in this chapter: worsening pain, swelling, looseness, bleeding, bite interference, foul taste, or drainage over a 24-hour period require same-day contact. Gather any photographs, temperature readings if relevant, and the direct contact routes for both Cavallini and the selected U.S. provider from the provider inventory. This section is complete when the entry states whether escalation was required, who was contacted, when they were contacted, and where supporting evidence is stored. If no contact was made despite a worsening trigger, record the reason and correct the failure the same day.

fields.label: Does today's symptom profile meet this chapter's same-day escalation threshold?
fields.placeholder: true
fields.validation_hint: Mark true if symptoms are worsening, function is impaired, mobility exists, or foul taste/drainage is present.

fields.label: Primary routing channel used for today's concern
fields.placeholder: Contacted both Cavallini and U.S. provider same day
options: Contacted both Cavallini and U.S. provider same day
Attempted contact but no response yet
fields.validation_hint: Choose the actual route taken based on urgency and provider availability.

fields.label: Time and method of most important provider contact made for this entry
fields.placeholder: 04/24/2026 3:40 PM Eastern — emailed symptom summary with photos to Melissa at North Shore Prosthodontics and WhatsApp message sent to Cavallini coordinator
fields.validation_hint: Include date, time zone if useful, contact route, and recipient name.

fields.label: What response or appointment instruction was received from Cavallini or the U.S. office
fields.placeholder: U.S. office moved review to 04/25/2026 10:30 AM; Cavallini asked for close-up photos and advised soft diet until seen
fields.validation_hint: Record the actual disposition, not just called office.

fields.label: Where today's photos, messages, and supporting evidence are stored for warranty and follow-up use
fields.placeholder: Cloud folder: Cavallini Return Home > Day 6 Issue Photos; local iPhone album duplicated to laptop
fields.validation_hint: Name the exact folder or storage path so evidence can be retrieved later.

fields.label: Open next-step deadline created by today's issue entry
fields.placeholder: Attend occlusal adjustment visit by 04/25/2026 and resend photos to Cavallini if mobility increases before appointment
fields.validation_hint: State a dated next step, such as book urgent review by tomorrow morning or await same-day callback by 5 PM.

decision_gates.condition: Pain, swelling, looseness, bleeding, foul taste/drainage, or bite interference has worsened over a 24-hour period rather than stabilizing or improving.
decision_gates.pass_action: Contact the treating channel specified in the discharge instructions the same day and log the contact details in this form.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not leave the issue in routine monitoring; route simultaneously to Cavallini and a local provider from the inventory if the primary office is unavailable.

decision_gates.condition: The bite prevents even bilateral contact, causes repeated one-sided clicking, or makes normal soft-food chewing impossible for more than 48 hours after return.
decision_gates.pass_action: Schedule the earliest available occlusal review with the U.S. provider or Cavallini-directed local provider.
decision_gates.fail_action: Mark the case as active stabilization and continue urgent provider routing until an evaluation is secured.

cross_references: U.S. Dentist Follow-Up Provider Inventory for Cavallini Restorative Cases
Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
common_errors: Writing vague comments like 'feels off' instead of recording which side hits first, whether chewing soft food is possible, and whether the symptom is improving or worsening versus the prior entry
Failing to compare the current entry to the previous entry, which hides the difference between expected healing and same-day escalation triggers
Not logging the exact date and contact route when Cavallini or the U.S. provider was notified about worsening pain, swelling, loosening, or drainage

worksheets.title: Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
worksheets.purpose: Maintains the evidentiary record of treatment dates, maintenance compliance, symptom notifications, provider communications, and stored proof needed to preserve Cavallini warranty rights.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 25 minutes to set up, 5 minutes per event logged
table_columns: Event Date Related to Cavallini Restorative Work
Event Type (Treatment / Return Home / U.S. Review / Hygiene / Imaging / Reported Issue / Cavallini Contact / Missed Maintenance)
Exact Issue or Maintenance Action Logged
Evidence Stored (photo folder / visit summary / email thread / radiograph file)
Who Was Notified or Involved (Cavallini / U.S. office / both)
Response, Recommendation, or Claim-Relevant Outcome
Warranty Compliance Status After This Entry

decision_gates.gate_title: Material Complication Must Be Documented to Preserve Warranty File
decision_gates.condition: Any complication affecting function, appearance, comfort, hardware stability, or tissue health has a date, symptom description, provider notification record, and evidence location logged in the register.
decision_gates.pass_action: Continue claim-ready documentation and link supporting photos, visits, and communications to the entry.
decision_gates.fail_action: Treat the warranty file as noncompliant until retrospective documentation is gathered immediately.

decision_gates.gate_title: Missed Maintenance Over 30 Days Creates Deviation
decision_gates.condition: A scheduled hygiene or surveillance review has been missed by more than 30 days.
decision_gates.pass_action: Open a maintenance deviation entry, reschedule immediately, and preserve proof of corrective action.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not assume passive compliance; note that warranty leverage may be reduced until the deviation is corrected.

cross_references: Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist for U.S. Providers
First-30-Days Bite, Provisional, and Soft-Tissue Issue Capture Form After Return Home
Long-Term Cleaning, Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Schedule for Costa Rica Restorative Work
common_errors: Assuming a phone call to Cavallini preserves warranty rights without saving the date, summary, and any follow-up message or email
Logging problems without linking them to photo locations, visit summaries, or maintenance records, leaving the claim file impossible to prove later
Failing to record missed hygiene or review visits as deviations, which weakens the ability to show compliance with lifetime warranty conditions

sample_rows.Exact Issue or Maintenance Action Logged: Upper implant-supported provisional delivered at Cavallini; discharge packet received
sample_rows.Evidence Stored (photo folder / visit su: PDF discharge summary, panoramic image, prosthesis photos in cloud folder /Trip2_Discharge
sample_rows.Response, Recommendation, or Claim-Relev: Baseline treatment event established for later claim reference

sample_rows.Exact Issue or Maintenance Action Logged: Day-6 bite overload on right side with clicking and reduced soft-food chewing
sample_rows.Evidence Stored (photo folder / visit su: Issue photos in /ReturnHome/Day6 and symptom log ws-07-c entry
sample_rows.Response, Recommendation, or Claim-Relev: U.S. office moved review earlier; Cavallini requested photos and advised soft diet
sample_rows.Warranty Compliance Status After This En: Compliant — documented and reported same day

sample_rows.Exact Issue or Maintenance Action Logged: Annual hygiene and prosthetic review missed by 43 days after due date
sample_rows.Evidence Stored (photo folder / visit su: No visit summary; rescheduling email saved in /Warranty/Deviations
sample_rows.Response, Recommendation, or Claim-Relev: Rescheduled for 2027-03-15; deviation opened for warranty file

worksheets.title: Long-Term Cleaning, Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Schedule for Costa Rica Restorative Work
worksheets.purpose: Builds the ongoing maintenance calendar that ties actual Cavallini restorative work to due dates, responsible providers, and missed-visit escalation rules in the United States.
worksheets.estimated_completion_time: 35 minutes to build, 10 minutes after each completed review
worksheets.left_column_label: Scheduled Maintenance Requirement for Cavallini Restorative Work
worksheets.right_column_label: Completed or Planned U.S. Follow-Up Execution Record

sections.section_title: Restorative Scope-to-Maintenance Obligation Mapping
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the Cavallini discharge packet, treatment summary, warranty terms, and any written maintenance intervals already provided by the clinic. Also review the Post-Return Dental Handoff Packet Completeness Checklist so you are not building a schedule from incomplete records. This section is complete when each type of restorative work delivered in Costa Rica has a corresponding maintenance obligation, or the missing interval is explicitly marked REQUIRES CLARIFICATION. Do not assume that a lifetime warranty replaces the need for actual scheduled maintenance events.

fields.label: Left side — Cavallini restorative work type that must drive the maintenance calendar
fields.placeholder: All-on-X immediate-load provisional on upper arch
fields.validation_hint: Select the actual work completed, such as implant-supported bridge or single crown.

fields.label: Right side — U.S. provider or office assigned to carry out this maintenance obligation
fields.placeholder: North Shore Prosthodontics — Dr. Elena Park for prosthetic surveillance; BrightSmiles Hygiene for routine cleaning
fields.validation_hint: Name the exact office or clinician responsible for the maintenance item.

fields.label: Left side — Cavallini-written maintenance interval or warranty-required timing for this work type
fields.placeholder: First review 7-14 days after return; hygiene every 4 months during provisional phase
fields.validation_hint: Enter the written interval from Cavallini if available; otherwise use REQUIRES CLARIFICATION.

fields.label: Right side — Date this maintenance pathway was confirmed with the U.S. provider
fields.validation_hint: Use the date the U.S. office agreed to the timing or role, not the treatment date.

sections.section_title: Hygiene, Surveillance Imaging, and Prosthetic Review Calendar Build
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather the first U.S. review date, any Cavallini instructions about radiographs or prosthesis checks, and the provider inventory showing who agreed to maintenance. Use real dates when known; if the timing is not supplied in writing, mark the schedule element REQUIRES CLARIFICATION rather than inventing an interval. This section is complete when at least the next hygiene visit, next imaging review, and next restorative review each have a due date or explicit clarification flag. The right column must show whether each event is already booked, completed, or still awaiting scheduling.

fields.label: Left side — Next hygiene/periodontal maintenance due date tied to the Cavallini restorative case
fields.placeholder: 2026-08-29 or REQUIRES CLARIFICATION if Cavallini did not specify interval
fields.validation_hint: Enter a real date if known, or the phrase REQUIRES CLARIFICATION if no written interval exists.

fields.label: Right side — Current booking status for that hygiene maintenance event
fields.placeholder: Booked for 2026-08-26 at 9:00 AM
fields.validation_hint: Choose the actual booking state for the maintenance event.

fields.label: Left side — Next surveillance imaging due date for implant or bone-level review
fields.placeholder: 2026-10-18 panoramic review requested 6 months after implant placement
fields.validation_hint: Use the actual due date or state REQUIRES CLARIFICATION if imaging interval is missing.

fields.label: Right side — Imaging provider or radiology source expected to complete that review
fields.placeholder: Downtown Oral Surgery Group panoramic imaging with copy sent to Cavallini if concerns arise
fields.validation_hint: Name the office responsible for obtaining and storing the surveillance image.

fields.label: Left side — Next prosthetic integrity or bite review due date for the Costa Rica restoration
fields.placeholder: 2026-06-12 provisional bite review after increased function; final prosthesis review timing pending
fields.validation_hint: Use a specific due date or a clarification flag; do not leave blank.

fields.label: Right side — Present status of that prosthetic review event
fields.placeholder: Booked
fields.validation_hint: Choose whether the prosthetic review is already booked, completed, or still unresolved.

sections.section_title: Deviation Control for Missed Maintenance and Open Stabilization Issues
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, review the warranty register and the first-30-days issue capture log to see whether any symptoms remain unresolved or any maintenance event is already overdue. This chapter states that a missed hygiene or surveillance review by more than 30 days must generate a maintenance deviation entry. This section is complete when you can show whether the case is in routine maintenance mode or still in active stabilization, and when any overdue event has a dated corrective action. If open complications remain at day 30, do not mark the case as routine maintenance only.

fields.label: Left side — Are any hygiene, imaging, or restorative review events currently more than 30 days overdue?
fields.placeholder: false
fields.validation_hint: Mark true if any scheduled maintenance item has passed its due date by over 30 days.

fields.label: Right side — If overdue, what corrective action date and rescheduling step were entered in the warranty register
fields.placeholder: No overdue events; or Deviation opened 2027-03-02 and hygiene rescheduled for 2027-03-15
fields.validation_hint: Reference the actual deviation and rescheduling action, not just will reschedule.

fields.label: Left side — Current case mode based on symptom status and maintenance readiness
fields.placeholder: Routine maintenance mode
options: Mixed — maintenance started but issues still open
fields.validation_hint: Choose Active stabilization if symptoms or packet deficiencies remain open.

fields.label: Right side — Lead coordinator responsible for keeping the Cavallini maintenance calendar current
fields.placeholder: Patient self-coordinating with reminders in Google Calendar and copies to spouse
fields.validation_hint: Name the person actually tracking due dates, such as patient, spouse, or treatment coordinator.

sections.section_title: Future-Date Control and Evidence Linkage
sections.instructions: Before completing this section, gather your calendar system, reminder settings, and the storage path used for visit summaries, radiographs, and warranty evidence. The goal here is not merely to list due dates but to ensure every planned event can be proven completed later if Cavallini requests maintenance evidence. This section is complete when reminder timing is set, evidence storage is named, and each completed visit can flow into the warranty register without reconstruction from memory. If your current calendar tool cannot support repeat reminders, identify the replacement method now.

fields.label: Left side — Reminder lead time before each Cavallini-related maintenance event becomes due
fields.placeholder: 30-day reminder plus 7-day reminder before hygiene and imaging visits
fields.validation_hint: Use a concrete reminder pattern, such as 30-day and 7-day alerts before every event.

fields.label: Right side — Calendar or task system where these reminders are actually stored
fields.placeholder: Google Calendar recurring series tagged Cavallini Maintenance + iPhone alerts
fields.validation_hint: Name the real system that will generate the reminders.

fields.label: Left side — Exact storage location for completed hygiene notes, radiographs, and prosthetic review summaries
fields.placeholder: Cloud folder /Cavallini/Warranty Evidence/Maintenance Visits plus local SSD backup
fields.validation_hint: Provide the precise folder or binder location for long-term evidence retention.

fields.label: Right side — Date this maintenance schedule was last reconciled against actual completed visits
fields.validation_hint: Use the most recent date the schedule was checked against real appointments and records.

decision_gates.gate_title: Written Maintenance Timing Exists or Is Flagged for Clarification
decision_gates.condition: Every hygiene, imaging, and prosthetic review interval is either supported by a written Cavallini or U.S. provider instruction or explicitly marked REQUIRES CLARIFICATION.
decision_gates.pass_action: Build the calendar with real dates and proceed to reminder setup.
decision_gates.fail_action: Do not guess intervals; obtain written clarification and keep the schedule open until resolved.

decision_gates.gate_title: Transition from Active Stabilization to Routine Maintenance
decision_gates.condition: The first 30 days after return are complete without unmanaged complication, all documented issues have a disposition, and recurring maintenance dates are entered.
decision_gates.pass_action: Move the case to routine maintenance mode and continue logging completed events in the warranty register.
decision_gates.fail_action: Keep the case in active stabilization mode and continue provider follow-up until open issues are resolved.

Cavallini Lifetime Warranty Compliance and Claim-Evidence Register
common_errors: Using a generic six-month cleaning assumption without checking whether Cavallini gave a different interval for implants, full-arch prosthetics, or warranty compliance
Failing to distinguish between provisional-phase reviews and long-term final prosthesis surveillance, causing the wrong interval to be tracked
Leaving future imaging or prosthetic review timing blank because the U.S. office did not suggest dates, instead of marking REQUIRES CLARIFICATION and obtaining written direction

appendix_builder.life_event: I am an American considering dental tourism at Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic in San José, Costa Rica (aestheticdentistrycr.com) for major restorative dental work — likely implants, crowns, or full-mouth restoration (All-on-X). I have no framework for evaluating an international dental clinic, planning medical travel logistics, coordinating pre-operative records transfer, managing the financial comparison between US and Costa Rica pricing, navigating post-operative recovery in a foreign country, or maintaining continuity of care with a stateside dentist after returning home. I need a complete operational control system that takes me from initial research and clinic evaluation through travel planning, treatment execution, onsite recovery, and long-term follow-up — with decision gates, checklists, cost worksheets, and timeline templates at every stage. The system should be specific to the Cavallini clinic's infrastructure (onsite hotels, onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, IV sedation availability, Nobel Biocare/Zimmer implant systems, lifetime warranty program) and Costa Rica as the destination country.

glossary_terms.definition: The connector piece between a dental implant and the visible crown or bridge. Before leaving Costa Rica, confirm the abutment type and component details are listed in your discharge packet so a U.S. dentist can identify compatible parts if something loosens.

A full-arch restoration supported by a limited number of implants, often used when replacing most or all teeth in one jaw. Use this term to confirm how many implants are actually planned, whether extractions are included, and how many trips to Costa Rica will be required.

Material placed to rebuild or preserve jawbone where implants need support. Ask whether grafting is included in the quote, whether it changes healing time, and whether it can delay implant placement or final prosthetic delivery.

Cone Beam Computed Tomography, a 3D dental scan used to evaluate bone volume, sinus location, nerve position, and implant feasibility. Confirm whether Cavallini requires an existing U.S. CBCT or will take one onsite, because that changes records transfer and total cost.

The handoff process between Cavallini in Costa Rica and your U.S. dentist after you return home. Operationally, this means securing a stateside provider before travel and collecting implant brand, prosthetic details, images, medications, and written follow-up instructions before departure.

The irreversible reshaping of a tooth so a crown can be placed. Do not allow crown preparation tied to Costa Rica treatment until the clinic has reviewed records, issued a written plan, and your travel timing makes it realistic to complete or protect the prepared tooth.
The point at which Cavallini confirms your records are complete enough to issue a preliminary treatment plan. Treat this as a go/no-go control gate before paying deposits or buying flights.

The complete set of documents and instructions you must obtain before leaving Costa Rica after treatment. It should include procedures performed, implant system details, prosthetic information, medications, post-op restrictions, warning signs, and follow-up timing.

A multi-tooth or whole-mouth reconstruction that may combine crowns, implants, bridges, extractions, provisional teeth, and staged healing. Use this label to trigger more aggressive planning for trip count, contingency budget, missed work, and U.S. follow-up support.

A temporary component placed on an implant to shape gum tissue during healing. If one comes loose after you return to the U.S., your follow-up dentist will need the implant brand and component information from Cavallini to address it safely.

Placement of a provisional tooth or bridge on implants very soon after surgery instead of waiting months. Confirm whether your case qualifies, what bite restrictions apply, and whether failure of the provisional changes your stay or future return-trip needs.

The specific manufacturer and component family used for the implant, such as Nobel Biocare or Zimmer. This is not a marketing detail; it is required for warranty tracking, replacement parts, and any emergency intervention by a U.S. dentist.

Medication delivered through a vein to reduce awareness and anxiety during surgery. If your treatment plan includes IV sedation, verify who administers it, what medical clearance is required, whether an escort is mandatory, and how long you must remain locally before travel.

A dental laboratory integrated with or physically close to the clinic that can fabricate or adjust crowns, bridges, and provisional prosthetics. For an international patient, this matters because same-trip remakes or bite adjustments may reduce extra hotel nights and repeat travel.

A medication dispensing capability at or connected to the clinic that supports faster access to prescribed post-op drugs. Confirm whether post-surgical antibiotics, pain medication, and mouth rinse are filled there or must be obtained elsewhere in San José.

The healing process in which the jawbone bonds to the implant surface. This determines why many implant cases require a waiting period between surgery and final restoration, and why premature chewing or missed follow-up can jeopardize results.

A wide dental image showing both jaws, teeth, and major anatomical structures. It is often the minimum starting document for remote review, but it may not be enough for implant planning without CBCT imaging.

A small, focused dental X-ray showing a tooth and surrounding bone in detail. These images can be useful for crown, root, or implant follow-up and should be collected if Cavallini or your U.S. dentist needs localized postoperative assessment.

A temporary bridge, denture, or crown used during healing before the final restoration is delivered. You need written instructions on diet, cleaning, breakage risk, and who to contact if it loosens while you are still in Costa Rica or after you return home.

The clinic's confirmation that you are stable enough to board a commercial flight after surgery or major restorative work. Treat this as an explicit checkpoint tied to bleeding control, swelling, medication tolerance, hydration, and any sedation-related restrictions.

The adult responsible for accompanying you after IV sedation and helping manage transport, medications, and first-night recovery. If required by the clinic, this is not optional travel support; it is a discharge control requirement.

The real-world limits of the clinic's lifetime warranty, including exclusions, maintenance requirements, travel obligations, and what is not covered. Extract this in writing before treatment so you know whether a future correction requires another Costa Rica trip at your expense.

professional_triggers.situation: Cavallini proposes IV sedation, you have significant medical history, or you take blood thinners, diabetes medications, heart medications, or osteoporosis drugs that may affect dental surgery planning.
professional_triggers.professional_type: Primary Care Physician or Treating Medical Specialist

professional_triggers.situation: Your U.S. records are incomplete, outdated, or scattered across multiple offices and you need a current panoramic X-ray, CBCT, periodontal charting, or treatment summary accepted for international planning.
professional_triggers.professional_type: U.S. General Dentist or Oral and Maxillofacial Radiology Provider

professional_triggers.situation: A U.S. quote and the Cavallini quote are not clearly comparable because they differ on implant brand, grafting, sedation, temporaries, or final prosthetic scope.

You are being asked to approve a large deposit, prepay treatment, or rely on a lifetime warranty without understanding refund terms, cancellation exposure, or whether corrective work would require another international trip.
You cannot determine whether the treatment is truly cheaper than staying in the U.S. once airfare, hotel, companion travel, missed work, medications, and revision reserve are included.
You are planning implant placement, sinus lift, extensive grafting, or full-arch All-on-X treatment and want an independent judgment on whether your case is appropriate for travel-based care.
You return to the U.S. with a loose provisional, unstable bite, broken temporary bridge, or gum irritation and you do not have clear local follow-up arrangements.

After surgery in Costa Rica or after returning home, you develop increasing facial swelling, fever, uncontrolled bleeding, pus, severe pain not controlled by prescribed medication, or inability to swallow fluids.
professional_triggers.professional_type: Emergency Dentist, Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon, or Emergency Department Physician

professional_triggers.situation: A U.S. dentist is willing to monitor you after return but requests exact implant brand, platform, component, and torque or prosthetic information before accepting the case.
professional_triggers.professional_type: Implant Coordinator at Cavallini Clinic or U.S. Prosthodontist

layout_mapping.document_title: Cavallini Costa Rica Restorative Dental Travel Control System
layout_mapping.document_subtitle: Operational Manual — I am an American considering dental tourism at Dr. Marco Muñoz Cavallini International Dental Clinic in San José, Costa Rica (aestheticdentistrycr.com) for major restorative dental work — likely implants, crowns, or full-mouth restoration (All-on-X). I have no framework for evaluating an international dental clinic, planning medical travel logistics, coordinating pre-operative records transfer, managing the financial comparison between US and Costa Rica pricing, navigating post-operative recovery in a foreign country, or maintaining continuity of care with a stateside dentist after returning home. I need a complete operational control system that takes me from initial research and clinic evaluation through travel planning, treatment execution, onsite recovery, and long-term follow-up — with decision gates, checklists, cost worksheets, and timeline templates at every stage. The system should be specific to the Cavallini clinic's infrastructure (onsite hotels, onsite lab, onsite pharmacy, IV sedation availability, Nobel Biocare/Zimmer implant systems, lifetime warranty program) and Costa Rica as the destination country.
